items.title: One-button fill Now
items.summary: Pick the next 1-2 roadmap cards to move into Now using WIP limit, blockers, dependencies, size, time-since-touched, and Jason's current energy. This closes the gap where Now can be empty while Next is huge.
items.done: `vanta now` proposes 1-2 cards for building with a short reason and one-keystroke accept, without exceeding the WIP limit.

items.title: Backlog decay / stale-card review
items.summary: Anti-bloat loop for the roadmap: find duplicates, stale items, vague cards, overlarge cards, and cards that should be merged, parked, or split. Suggests cleanup; does not mutate without approval.
items.done: `vanta roadmap prune` lists duplicate/stale/vague cards and proposes merge/park/rewrite actions; approved changes update roadmap.json and regenerate roadmap.html.

items.title: Finish-one-thing posture
items.summary: A dedicated mode for closing one active item. Narrows attention to the current building card, suppresses unrelated idea-expansion unless explicitly requested, and keeps the turn pointed at verified shipping.
items.done: `/ship` focuses on one active building item, refuses unrelated expansion by default, and ends each turn with what remains to ship.

items.title: Frictionless this-broke recorder
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. repl/bug-cmd.ts: /bug <what happened> captures a structured record — description, timestamp, session id, provider/model, last user intent, recent tool calls (recentToolNames), and git state (branch + uncommitted count, best-effort) — via pure formatBugRecord, written to.vanta/bugs/bug-<session>-<ts>.md (durable + greppable). Output points to roadmap_add to promote it to a card on triage. Wired into HANDLERS + catalog. 5 tests; tsc clean; handlers.ts under the 300-line cap.
items.done: `/bug <what happened>` creates a structured bug record or roadmap card with repro steps, context, and source evidence when available.

items.title: Turn current screen into one action
items.summary: Manual first slice of ambient context: use the existing screen vision path to inspect what Jason is looking at and convert it into one concrete next action with visible evidence.
items.done: `/screen-task` looks at the current screen and returns one concrete next action, citing the visible UI/error/text it used.

items.title: Agent-native add-card tool
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. roadmap/add.ts addRoadmapItem (mirrors move.ts): validates the card via RoadmapItemSchema, refuses a duplicate id (case-insensitive), appends, bumps updated, regenerates roadmap.html. tools/roadmap-add.ts exposes the roadmap_add tool (required id+title; defaults status=next, track=Backlog, size=M; optional tier/model/effort) — kernel-Allow via describeForSafety. Registered in tools/index.ts (now 44 incl. mount_mcp); the exact-list registry test + CLAUDE.md count updated. 6 tests (3 add + registry list); tsc clean.
items.done: `roadmap_add` validates the card, refuses duplicate IDs, appends to roadmap.json, regenerates roadmap.html, and returns the created item.

items.title: Diagnostic repro bundle
items.summary: When something fails, assemble a clean redacted diagnostic packet: git status, relevant logs, package/tool versions, current provider/model, recent changes, and a concise failure summary. Safe to paste into another agent or issue.
items.done: `vanta repro` emits a redacted repro bundle plus summary; secrets are excluded and the bundle includes enough context to reproduce the failure.

items.title: Personal model scorecard
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. bench/model-bench.ts: BenchResult schema (model/provider/taskKind/latencyMs/cost/qualityNote), saveBenchResult, loadBenchResults, formatBenchScorecard (avg latency+cost+task count per model), buildRoutingRecommendations (fastest per task kind). vanta models bench CLI. 6 tests; 1737 total; tsc clean.
items.done: `vanta models bench` records latency, cost, and quality notes per model/task type, then recommends routing defaults for main and auxiliary functions.

items.title: Copy-paste handoff for another agent
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. repl/handoff-cmd.ts: /handoff assembles a copy-paste packet from FACTUAL state (no model call) — date, branch, session, provider/model, active goals, git changed-files, recent tool calls, last intent, last result — via pure formatHandoffPacket, with a NEXT-ACTION slot. Wired into HANDLERS + catalog. The deterministic base AUTO-HANDOFF (auto-fire on context pressure) will build on. 3 tests; tsc clean; handlers.ts under cap.
items.done: `/handoff` outputs a copy-paste-ready packet with verified current task state, relevant files, decisions, and the next action.

items.title: Visual reference library for taste engine
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. taste/asset-index.ts: 10 TASTE_TAGS (operator-dossier/schematic-rail/glyph-system/signal-panel/warm-precise/too-generic/too-mascot/editorial/terminal-first/minimal-stark), ingestAsset, loadAssets, searchAssets, formatAssets. vanta taste [add|search|list] CLI. 6 tests.
items.done: Vanta can ingest a visual reference, store taste tags and notes, and use them later when critiquing or generating design direction.

items.title: Vision routes to a dedicated model
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-05. routing/vision.ts (visionEnv + resolveVisionProvider); all 3 image tools (describe_image, look_at_screen, look_at_camera) route to VANTA_VISION_MODEL (+ optional VANTA_VISION_PROVIDER) when set, else the active provider. Fixes vision silently breaking on a text-only main model (DeepSeek V4 Flash, local Ollama). 4 unit tests, full suite green,.env.example + CLAUDE.md updated.
items.done: Vision works while the main model is non-vision (text-only VANTA_PROVIDER + VANTA_VISION_MODEL=gpt-4o-mini).

items.title: TUI + slash-list readability
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-05. TUI fills terminal width (removed 100-col cap); slash palette capped to 8 matches with a fixed command column + width-clipped descriptions (was unbounded + ragged space-between, so typing / dumped all 37 commands); /skills aligns names + clips to one line; skill INDEX in the system prompt clips each description (~100 chars) so weak models stop parroting the library. 310 TUI/repl tests green.
items.done: Slash palette and /skills read like Claude CLI; typing / no longer floods the screen.

items.title: Strip /Claude/agent mentions from published code
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. Stripped prior-agent lineage from all published source code, docs, roadmap, and CLAUDE.md. Functional bridge strings left intact (external binary calls). Old research archive deleted. Committed and pushed to main.
items.done: Published code and user-facing text carry no other-agent references; research docs deleted; git history clean.

items.title: Per-function aux-task model map
items.summary: Generalize AUX-VISION into a function->model/provider map (vision, summarize, title, embed), one resolver extending routing/model-router.ts, surfaced in /status + a /aux command + the setup wizard. Full 'auxiliary tasks' dashboard parity.
items.done: Each auxiliary function can be bound to its own model independent of the main agent model.

items.title: Fix model-persistence regression
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Root cause: the live.env still held the LEGACY ARGO_PROVIDER=codex, and the back-compat shim mirrors ARGO_*->VANTA_* when VANTA_* is unset — the stuck-on-codex symptom. Two fixes: (1) /model <arg> was a no-op print; now routes through the picker's switch path (parseModelArg + repl/model-cmd.ts) — hot-swaps the live convo + post-turn pipeline + persists. (2) upsertEnvMigratingLegacy strips the stale ARGO_ twin on any explicit model write (picker + setup wizard), so the choice is the single source of truth and codex can't shadow it. TUI banner refreshes on /model <arg> via SlashResult.provider. Proven by a clean fresh-process relaunch sim (resolves the picked model, ARGO_PROVIDER gone). +10 tests; full suite 1134 green; tsc clean.
items.done: Pick a model -> it is still active next launch, proven by a relaunch.

items.title: Auto vague-goal to next action
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Extracted buildNextStepResend + isVagueGoal from repl/next.ts (no duplication — /next and the auto-fire share the same prompt builder). The /goal handler now, after setting a goal, runs isVagueGoal (short, or abstract-marker + no concrete anchor like a path/CARD-ID/#issue/code) and if vague auto-returns the single-micro-step resend so a concrete next action surfaces WITHOUT the user typing /next. Opt-out via VANTA_GOAL_ACTION=0. Wired in both hosts via the existing SlashResult.resend path. 7 unit tests (isVagueGoal + goal auto-fire via HANDLERS); tsc clean; handlers.ts still under the 300-line cap.
items.done: Setting a vague goal surfaces one concrete named next action without the user typing /next.

items.title: /restart command (reload the instance)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. /restart (repl/restart-cmd.ts) exits with the sysexits sentinel 75; run.sh now wraps the launch in a relaunch loop (export VANTA_RELAUNCH=1; re-runs tsx on code 75, passes any other code through) so edited source reloads in place without quitting to the shell. Both hosts wired: readline (interactive.ts, process.exitCode=75 + forced exit after cleanup) and TUI (app.tsx app.exit() + launch.tsx forced 75 exit after waitUntilExit). Kernel reconnects via the existing ping/auto-start. Refuses with a hint when VANTA_RELAUNCH is unset (run via npm/old launcher) so it never surprise-quits. 3 unit tests; full suite green; tsc clean. The actual TTY relaunch is Jason's to confirm in a terminal (the loop is offline-reasoned + shell-syntax-checked, like the TUI streaming verify).
items.done: /restart relaunches Vanta in place with fresh code; the kernel reconnects.

items.title: Early fail-detect + safe retry + honest report
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. tool-retry.ts: shouldRetryTool = isRetryableTool (a curated set of idempotent reads — read_file/web_*/git_status/gmail_read/vision/… ; writes/shell/commit/run_code/spawns excluded, unknown tools default off) AND isTransientFailure (network/timeout/rate-limit/5xx patterns; a deterministic ENOENT is NOT retried). Wired into agent.ts dispatchTool: a transient read failure re-runs up to resolveToolRetries() times (VANTA_TOOL_RETRIES, default 1, clamp 0..5), emitting a retry note; the final result is returned as-is so success is never faked. Early-detect + honest-report already existed via EF-ERRORDETECT (isErrorResult + consecutive-failure pause). 10 unit tests; agent suite green; tsc clean;.env.example documents the knob.
items.done: A failed tool call is surfaced immediately; only safe operations auto-retry; no false success claims.

items.title: Operator voice + verify-before-claim discipline
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. prompt.ts stableTier: added rule 10 (Voice — direct/literal/structured, lead with the answer, no filler/hype/AI-magic phrasing, plain operator register, warm-not-robotic) and hardened rule 4 (done-claim must cite the command + its result; never claim done/fixed/working in prose alone). SOUL.md already on-voice (operator, honest, no-fake). Locks the framing with prompt tests asserting both rules present. NOTE: the aesthetic half of done ('reads like the brand guide examples') is Jason's to confirm on real replies; VOICE-NATURAL tunes warmth on top of this base, TRUST-LABELS renders the epistemic markers.
items.done: Vanta's replies read like the brand guide examples; success is never claimed without tool-output evidence.

items.title: Detect stalled goals, propose smallest unblocker
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. repl/stall.ts: a post-turn gate (mirrors EF-INHIBIT/SET-SHIFT). nextStallState resets on a durable-progress tool (write_file/git_commit), else increments; shouldAlertStall fires at VANTA_STALL_THRESHOLD (default 4, 0 disables). buildStallText emits ONE line naming the stalled goal, the duration, and a CONCRETE unblocker — the top buildable backlog card (topNextItems of readNextItems) — falling back to /next. Distinct from inhibit: only fires WITH an active goal and proposes a specific action. Wired into both hosts (interactive.ts + tui/use-agent-send.ts via stallAfterTurn). 8 unit tests; full suite 1163 green; tsc clean;.env.example documents the knob.
items.done: A stalled goal triggers an unprompted one-line 'smallest next step' suggestion.

items.title: Persistent operator task stack + loop-closing
items.summary: A durable task stack with explicit in-progress / blocked / parked / done states, surfaced on demand, and loop-closing ('I said I would do X - here is whether X happened'). Builds on todo/store, closure-gate, and /wm working memory.
items.done: Vanta tracks every committed task to closure and can answer 'where are we?' with accurate states.

items.title: Durable-vs-noise classification + relevance-gated surfacing
items.summary: Classify durable facts (taste, projects, people, workflows, decisions) vs temporary session noise, and surface memory only when relevant to the current task - never as clutter. Builds on brain regions, capped/pruned memory/store, and recall.
items.done: Vanta recalls the right durable fact at the right moment without dumping irrelevant memory.

items.title: Live operator dashboard (brand dossier aesthetic)
items.summary: A unified live surface: active goals, current plan, blockers, recent actions, pending approvals, suggested next move, memory highlights - rendered in the Vanta Brand Style Guide dossier/control-room aesthetic (left status rail, operation cards, signal colors). Subsumes brand-TUI; the seed for the DESKTOP app.
items.done: One glance shows goals, plan, blockers, approvals, next move, and memory in the brand aesthetic.

items.title: Aesthetic direction + compare visual options
items.summary: Use operator/dossier taste to give real aesthetic direction and compare visual options side-by-side (UI states, diagrams, references), instead of generic design feedback. Builds on the aux-routed vision tools.
items.done: Vanta compares two visual options with a reasoned, taste-grounded recommendation.

items.title: Build/debug/design/planning/body-double modes
items.summary: Named operating modes - build (minimal talk, progress), debug (systematic root-cause), design (visual systems + artifacts), planning (bite-sized steps), body-double (low-friction keep-moving prompts) - with one-key switching. Extends modes/builtin.ts.
items.done: A single command switches Vanta's behavior into a named mode.

items.title: Change-watchers for repos/issues/email/calendar
items.summary: Scheduled watchers that monitor repos/issues/emails/calendar for important changes and draft actions, awaiting approval where risk is high. Extends gateway (cron+webhook+platform poll).
items.done: Vanta surfaces an important change and a drafted response without being asked, gated by approval on risk.

items.title: Map the 262 user stories to Vanta coverage
items.summary: Build a coverage matrix of the 262 user-stories (per the use-cases PDF) against Vanta's 45 tools; surface only the genuine gaps. v1 was full parity, so expect mostly covered - the value is finding the real misses.
items.done: A coverage matrix exists; genuine gaps are promoted to roadmap items.

items.title: Periodic memory curator (compress -> merge -> forget)
items.summary: FIRST SLICE of the living-operator arc (wants2 #1, its named step 1). A cheap/local-model pass that runs occasionally (session end / cron / heartbeat) and asks: what changed, what did I learn, what to remember about Jason, what mistake not to repeat, what goal stays salient, what to compress or forget. Writes one durable note per lasting event and merges related notes. Adds verbal controls: 'remember X', 'forget that', 'compress today', 'this is identity', 'not important'. Extends MEM-COMPRESS + MEM-VERSIONING; runs on the E-eff2/AUX-MAP local route so it stays near-free.
items.done: Ending a session triggers a curator pass that emits or merges durable notes and is overridable by a verbal 'forget that' / 'remember X' command, proven by a stored-note diff.

items.title: Aggressive forgetting + memory lifecycle (months in MB)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. memory/forget.ts: pruneStaleBlocks (delete non-durable blocks past TTL, keep durable regardless of age), getMemoryFootprint, formatForgetSummary. vanta memory forget [ttl-days] + vanta memory footprint commands. Default TTL=30 days. 7 tests; 1646 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: Stale raw logs past a TTL are pruned and old notes compressed, with total memory footprint measured before/after and a documented kept-vs-discarded rule.

items.title: Trust calibration: verified / inferred / uncertain / needs-check
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Folded into prompt.ts rule 7 (honesty) rather than appending a new rule: 'Label claims by epistemic status — verified (tool-backed) / inferred / uncertain — so it's clear when you know vs guess; show an unverifiable claim as uncertain, not as flat fact.' prompt test locks the clause. Behavioral rendering on real replies is model-driven (verified live, like BEHAVIOR-VOICE).
items.done: A factual reply renders explicit verified/inferred/uncertain markers, and an unverifiable claim is shown as uncertain rather than stated flat.

items.title: Living-operator scaffold (sentience as direction, not claim)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. self/store.ts: ensureSelf seeds ~/.vanta/self/{identity,values,honesty}.md; selfDigest merges all three for prompt injection; writeSelfFile logs writes to self/changelog.md (checked path). selfContent param added to buildSystemPrompt (prompt.ts) + prepareRun (session.ts). 5 tests; 1651 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: A versioned.vanta/self/ identity+values+honesty file exists, is loaded into the prompt, and updates only through a checked path (no unchecked self-rewrite).

items.title: Reflection + learning from correction (extract the rule)
items.summary: The grows-over-time loop (wants1 #18; wants2 #2/#7; wants2 build-order step 4). When Jason says 'not like that' / 'too generic' / 'should have asked first', extract a reusable rule (e.g. 'keep technical-dossier aesthetic, avoid mascot softness'), store it as a reflection memory, and auto-apply it later. Extends B3 background-review + B4 skill-curator + the reflection layer of BRAIN-NEURO.
items.done: A correction in chat produces a stored reflection rule that demonstrably alters a later response without being restated.

items.title: Taste engine (private reference vocabulary)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. taste/engine.ts: scoreTasteAlignment (tag frequency scoring, avoid > fits > borderline), compareDesigns (two-option comparison), evaluateTaste, findReferences. POSITIVE_TAGS/AVOID_TAGS vocabulary. vanta taste eval <description> CLI. 6 tests; 1750 total; tsc clean.
items.done: Given two design options Vanta picks one with a reason grounded in a stored taste rule, not generic best-practice.

items.title: Anti-slop filter (detect + flag AI-ish drift)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. repl/anti-slop.ts: detectSlop (6-rule PATTERN_RULES: sycophancy/ai-magic/corporate/empty-filler/fake-closing/overcautious) + formatSlopNote. antiSlopAfterText exported from session.ts (lazy import, VANTA_ANTI_SLOP=0 disables). Wired into interactive.ts (console note) and use-agent-send.ts (TUI dispatch note). 12 unit tests; 1639 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: Vanta flags a slop pattern in its own draft and offers a tightened rewrite before the message is sent.

items.title: Project radar (per-project state + idle/near-done signals)
items.summary: Track each project's state -- last touched, next action, risk, blocker, energy level -- and notice 'idle 9 days' or 'one small commit from done' (wants1 #3). Reads git + memory across the workspace (SCOPE-2 readable zones). This is the cross-project state model BEHIND OPERATOR-DASHBOARD (the surface) and AUTO-WATCH (the change-watchers); build it once, both consume it.
items.done: A radar view lists each known project with last-touched, next-action, and one flagged state (idle or near-done), derived from real git/memory data.

items.title: Energy-aware action menus (2-min / low-energy / deep-work / admin)
items.summary: When executive function is the bottleneck (not knowledge), offer next actions binned by cognitive load: a 2-minute action, a low-energy action, a deep-work action, an admin action (wants1 #5). Extends ND1 task-initiation + GOAL-ACTION + EF-CHOICEREDUCE -- the same next-action infra, sliced by energy.
items.done: Asking 'what now' returns up to four concrete actions labelled by energy tier, each a real named step from the active goals.

items.title: Inbox/calendar triage (find the hidden commitments)
items.summary: Summarize what matters, detect commitments hidden in emails, surface 'you probably need to answer this', draft replies (wants1 #6). A behavioral layer on the shipped Google/Gmail/Calendar comms (E-series + vanta auth google) -- triage intelligence, not new plumbing.
items.done: Given an inbox, Vanta lists the messages that imply a commitment or need a reply and drafts one reply, with the source message cited.

items.title: Personal protocol library (reusable routines)
items.summary: User-shaped reusable routines: launch a project, debug a failing build, prep for a meeting, review a PR, clean a repo, make a landing page, turn notes into tasks (wants1 #7; wants2 build-order step 5). Distinct from the generic skills library (D1/D2) -- these are Jason's personal procedures, captured from repeated solutions (ties to REFLECT-CORRECT).
items.done: Running a named protocol (e.g. 'clean repo') executes its stored ordered steps, and a repeated ad-hoc workflow can be saved as a new protocol.

items.title: Pre-send self-evaluation rubric
items.summary: Before sending, score the draft -- useful? grounded? too verbose? did I actually answer? did I avoid the real work? -- then improve it (wants1 #9). Extends B2 post-turn nudges + B3 background-review into an explicit pre-send rubric on the cheap route. Feeds ANTI-SLOP + TRUST-LABELS.
items.done: A weak draft is caught by the rubric (e.g. flagged 'avoided the real work') and revised before it reaches Jason, logged with the rubric verdict.

items.title: Autonomous research loops (overnight brief, tracked uncertainty)
items.summary: Research a topic over time, compare sources, produce a short brief, track uncertainty instead of dumping links (wants1 #10). Composes cron (scheduled) + O2 swarms + the deep-research skill + MEM-CURATOR (store the brief, not the links). Senses/agency = wants2 build-order steps 6/8.
items.done: A scheduled research task produces a cited brief with an explicit confidence/uncertainty note, stored as one durable memory rather than a link dump.

items.title: Task-ending contract (changed / verified / remains / next)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Folded into prompt.ts rule 4 (completion): 'Close a multi-step task with: what changed · what was verified · what remains · next.' — codifies the global CLAUDE.md §14 DONE block as a prompt rule rather than a new rule. prompt test locks the clause. Emission is model-driven (verified live).
items.done: Completing any multi-step task auto-emits the four-line changed/verified/remains/next block.

items.title: Action previews + post-action proof
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. The 'before' half already existed (write_file returns a computed diff → TUI-DIFF). Added the missing 'after' half: write_file now RE-READS the file post-write and confirms the bytes actually landed — 'verified N lines, M bytes on disk' (or a ⚠ if on-disk content differs / re-read fails), instead of reporting only the intended byte count. Real post-action proof, not assumed success. 1 added test (tools.test.ts); full suite 1201 green; tsc clean.
items.done: A file write shows the diff before and a verification readout (re-read / test result) after, both within the turn.

items.title: Visible cost + latency per call (local vs frontier)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. pricing.ts (pure): estimateCostUsd (approx $/1M-token table, gpt-4o-mini-before-gpt-4o ordering, unknown/local→null), isLocalProvider, formatTurnCost (tokens + latency + cost, ~? when unpriced), addTurnCost + formatSessionCost (split local-free vs frontier-metered, with turn counts). Both hosts now print the cost/latency footer per turn and accumulate ReplState.sessionCost; /usage shows the session split. Prices are approximate-for-visibility (documented). 8 unit tests; full suite 1182 green; tsc clean.
items.done: Each turn shows model, token counts, and latency; /status shows session cost split by local vs frontier.

items.title: Conflict-surfacing on settled decisions
items.summary: The unbuilt delta of decision-memory (wants1 #4): MEM-GRAPH stores goals x decisions, but nothing actively surfaces a tradeoff when a NEW choice conflicts with a locked DECISIONS.md entry. Detect the conflict, surface the old rationale, prevent silent re-litigation. Reads DECISIONS.md + MEM-GRAPH.
items.done: Proposing a change that contradicts a locked decision triggers a one-line 'this conflicts with [decision]; the reason was X' before proceeding.

items.title: Natural, warm-but-not-fake voice (not cold/robotic)
items.summary: Goal: Vanta sounds natural and human-legible, not clipped or cold. Jason flagged the safety/operator text as 'very hard and cold.' Resolves the tension with BEHAVIOR-VOICE (direct, literal, no AI-magic): operator-precise AND warm enough -- calm, loyal, plain-spoken, dry register, never fake-cheerful, never corporate, never robotic. Tunes SOUL.md + the prompt + TRUST-LABELS phrasing so honesty reads as care, not coldness. Rationale locked in DECISIONS.md (warm-precise reconciliation). [2026-06-07] Substance is in place — prompt rule 10 carries 'warm enough to be human, never fake-cheerful or robotic' and SOUL.md is already warm-operator. BLOCKED on Jason: the done criterion is 3 before/after sample approvals, which only he can give. Stays next until then.
items.done: Side-by-side, a stock reply reads warmer and more natural than the current clipped voice while staying precise -- verified on 3 before/after samples Jason approves.

items.title: The not-evil charter (inspectable values constitution)
items.summary: wants3's explicit should / should-not list as a written, inspectable values artifact under.vanta/self/ (with SCAFFOLD): loyal to Jason's agency, honest about limits, ask before risk, stay interruptible/inspectable, keep humans central; never deceive, manipulate, seek power, self-preserve against Jason, bypass gates, or replace human connection. JARVIS+HAL+Skynet capability, none of the danger. Wires to the kernel's rule-zero (scope + approval are already enforced) and makes the VALUES explicit + reviewable, not just the gates. Locked in DECISIONS.md.
items.done: A versioned.vanta/self/charter file states the should/should-not list, is loaded into the system prompt, and is referenced by the protection review path.

items.title: Life/world/business schema (Phase 1 data foundation)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. life-os/schema.ts: LifeOsSchema (14 entity kinds: projects, tasks, opportunities, contacts, revenue, expenses, decisions, agents, creativeSystems, learningTracks, risks, routines). life-os/store.ts: loadLifeOs, saveLifeOs, upsertById, removeById, formatLifeOsSummary. 8 tests; 1687 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: A typed schema + store exists for the 14 entity kinds, seeded from current goals/projects and queryable by the dashboards.

items.title: Bounded agent-role council (CEO/CTO/COO/.../Reflection)
items.summary: wants3 Phase 2. A reusable library of named, bounded agent roles on top of O2 swarms + O1 agent-chosen-model + WORKFLOWS: Coordinator + CEO/CTO/COO/CFO/Sales/Marketing/Product/Research/Creative-Director/Personal-Life/Learning/Protection/Home/Reflection. Each role carries a fixed brief -- role, limited context, allowed tools, output format, memory boundary, success condition, report-back, and NO risky action without approval (kernel-gated). Adds 'vanta agents run "goal"' that routes a goal to the right roles and synthesizes. Creative-Director pairs with TASTE-ENGINE; Reflection with MEM-CURATOR; Protection with PROTECTION-AGENT.
items.done: 'vanta agents run' spawns 2+ named role-agents with enforced tool/memory boundaries and returns a synthesized, kernel-approved plan.

items.title: Protection agent (scams, privacy, unsafe actions, agent overreach)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. protection/agent.ts: scanForThreats (6 rule kinds: scam/privacy/unsafe-cmd/manipulation/agent-overreach/contract-trap) + formatThreatReport. protect tool registers in ALL_TOOLS. 9 unit tests; 1696 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: Given a risky input (e.g. a manipulative contract clause or an unsafe command), the protection agent flags it with the specific risk and a recommended action.

items.title: vanta today / vanta brief (JARVIS-like 'what matters now')
items.summary: wants3 min-useful-version, the smallest slice of the command center: 'vanta brief' / 'vanta today' = a compact summary of what matters now -- top 3 actions, appointments, urgent messages, blockers, energy level. Sits on LIFE-OS-SCHEMA + PROJECT-RADAR + ENERGY-PLAN + COMMS-TRIAGE. One command, real data, no dashboards yet.
items.done: 'vanta brief' prints top-3 actions, today's appointments, flagged messages, and current blockers from real goal/calendar/project data.

items.title: Money + escape-the-9-to-5 dashboard (CFO facet)

items.done: 'vanta money' shows current vs target income, opportunities ranked by profit/effort, and the next single money action.

items.title: Daily / weekly / monthly review cadence
items.summary: wants3 Phase 4. Recurring review loops: daily (top-3, money action, blocker check, protection/risk check), weekly (revenue, project, opportunity, creative, life-systems, next-week plan), monthly (escape-9-to-5 progress, business-model, financial, identity/memory review, what to prune/forget). Composes cron + MEM-CURATOR (the monthly prune) + BRIEF-CMD/MONEY-OS (the surfaces). Cadence + prompts, not new plumbing.
items.done: A scheduled daily review emits the top-3/money/blocker/risk block, and the monthly review triggers a MEM-CURATOR prune pass.

items.title: Auto mode-selection (silent-executor / collaborator / critic / researcher / debugger)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. repl/mode-detect.ts: pure detectMode classifies the request into silent-executor / collaborator / critic / researcher / debugger / assistant (specific stances win over the generic executor — e.g. 'failing, fix it' → debugger). buildModeHint returns a one-line stance directive (or null for the neutral default = no injection). Both hosts prepend the hint to the SENT message only (display stays clean); opt-out VANTA_MODE_DETECT=0. Distinct from MODES-v2 (user-set). 9 unit tests; tsc clean.
items.done: A 'just do it' request gets silent-executor mode and a 'what do you think' gets collaborator/critic mode, switched automatically without Jason naming the mode.

items.title: Automatic per-task model routing + ephemeral subagents
items.summary: Keep the main brain on the selected premium model (e.g. codex/gpt-5.5) while Vanta automatically spins up scoped subagents on local/cloud models for bounded tasks -- summarize, classify, research, verify, memory compression, first-pass code inspection -- then tears the worker down (local Ollama unloads via keep_alive:'0s'). A ROUTER LAYER on top of delegate/swarm, not a new agent system: classifyTaskKind + resolveTaskRoute(env) + routeEnv, per-kind VANTA_ROUTE_* config, fail-soft to main, high-risk tasks (security/financial/irreversible/commits/kernel) forced to main. Adds an auto_delegate tool + /routes status + setup-wizard aux config; absorbs AUX-MAP as a slice. Full 4-slice build plan: docs/auto-router.md.
items.done: With the main model active, Vanta auto_delegates a low-risk subtask to a configured local/cloud worker (e.g. ollama/qwen2.5:14b), returns the output, unloads the local model, and synthesizes through the main brain; /routes shows the routing table + availability.

items.title: Collapsible 'think out loud' (accordion, no wall of text)
items.summary: Vanta thinks out loud, but long reasoning/design output should FOLD by default and expand on a keypress (cmd/ctrl+O or an accordion) so it never floods the screen with a wall of text. Extends TUI-THINK (collapsible thinking, shipped) + UI-READABILITY + TUI-SMOOTH: apply the fold to ALL verbose output (long tool reasoning, multi-section design dumps like the vanta ability design), default-collapsed with a one-line summary + expand affordance. Pairs with nd-sensory-load (compressed by default).
items.done: A long reasoning/design block renders as a one-line collapsed summary with a keybind/accordion to expand; the screen is not flooded by default.

items.title: Built-in code-size gate (industry-standard, always on)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. lint/size.ts: pure analyzeSource via the TS compiler API — file ≤300, function ≤50, params ≤4, cyclomatic complexity ≤10 (decision points incl. &&/||/??, not double-counting nested fns); each violation = file:line + metric + limit + fix. lint/run.ts + `vanta lint [files|--staged]` (default = all git-tracked.ts/.tsx; tests +.d.ts exempt). Pre-commit (scripts/pre-commit) now runs it on staged TS — WARN-ONLY by default (VANTA_LINT_BLOCK=1 to enforce) because a hard block would stop all commits: the gate surfaced 85 pre-existing violations (agent.ts 334L, cli.ts 401L, interactive.ts runChat 240L, providers/index.ts resolveProvider cx 24, …) — real debt for a follow-up 'size-debt paydown' card, not fixed here. 'Not just the factory' = the gate now serves human commits + `vanta lint`; the factory keeps its file-line born-small check. [in-agent, 2026-06-07] write_file now runs analyzeSource on every TS/TSX write and surfaces violations in the tool result (advisory, never blocks) so the AGENT writes born-small / self-corrects in-loop — closing the human+agent+factory triad. 10 unit tests; full suite 1210 green; tsc clean.
items.done: A commit or factory cycle with an oversized file/function/complexity is flagged by Vanta's own gate (not just the factory), reporting file:line + limit + fix; passing code commits clean.

items.title: Authenticated browser profile for logged-in sites
items.summary: Opt-in persistent Playwright profile so Vanta browses logged-in sites (X, GitHub, Google, dashboards) WITHOUT stealing cookies from your real browser. 'vanta browser auth <site>' opens a headed window in a dedicated profile under ~/.vanta/browser-profiles/<site>/ (outside the repo) -> you log in manually (Vanta never sees password/2FA) -> browser_extract_auth reads logged-in pages headless via launchPersistentContext. Domain-bound per profile, read-only by default (navigate/click/post = explicit approval each time), NEVER returns cookies/localStorage/auth headers. Extends the existing browser tools + isAllowedDomain. Packaged as Vanta's FIRST capability plugin (tools + skill + lazy deps in OS cache). Design + slices: docs/plugins-and-auth-browser.md.
items.done: 'vanta browser auth x.com' opens a dedicated profile, Jason logs in manually, then browser_extract_auth reads X search results from that profile while cookies stay confined to ~/.vanta/browser-profiles and are never exposed to the model; tests prove id-sanitization + domain-binding + no-cookie-leak.

items.title: WhatsApp adapter (Node subprocess bridge; Business-API alt)

UNOFFICIAL -> account ban risk + breaks on protocol changes; WhatsApp Business API (Meta-verified) is the ToS-safe alternative (captured as 'official' mode). Bridge dep installs to ~/.vanta, never the repo (PLUGIN-SYSTEM). Build last.
items.done: After a QR pair, 'vanta gateway' receives a WhatsApp message via the Node bridge and replies; bridge spawn/health/restart handled; pure parse tested offline; risk + Business-API alt documented.

items.title: Plugin framework (register(ctx) + PluginContext, like Claude/Codex)
items.summary: A unified plugin framework so Vanta extends without core edits, like Claude/Codex: a register(ctx) entry + PluginContext API (register_tool/hook/command/cli/skill/provider/platform, llm.complete, dispatch_tool). Discovery precedence bundled(vanta-ts/plugins/) -> user(~/.vanta/plugins/) -> project(.vanta/plugins/, env-gated) -> npm; opt-in plugins.enabled allow-list. Ties together the pieces Vanta already has (tool registry, MCP/mount_mcp, hooks, MSG-REGISTRY, skills) under one contract. Vanta's kernel stays the boundary — every plugin tool routes through assess() (unlike other agents that run plugins unsandboxed). Builds on PLUGIN-SYSTEM (install hygiene). 'vanta plugins install/enable/disable/remove/list'. Detail: docs/plugin-framework.md.
items.done: A third-party plugin dropped in ~/.vanta/plugins/ and added to plugins.enabled registers a kernel-gated tool + a slash command that work next session; disabled/not-enabled plugins don't load.

items.title: Full composer keybindings (readline/Emacs + macOS Cmd)
items.summary: Build every known line-editing shortcut in the composer (tui/composer.tsx). Shipped: Ctrl+U/W, Esc, up/down history, Shift+Enter. To build: cursor movement (Ctrl+A/E start/end, Ctrl+B/F char, Alt+B/F + Ctrl+arrows word, Cmd+arrows line, Cmd+up/down top/bottom, Alt+up/down move-line), deletion/kill (Ctrl+D/Delete forward, Alt+Backspace/Ctrl+Backspace word-before, Alt+D word-after, Ctrl+K kill-to-end, Cmd+Backspace/Delete to line start/end), edit/kill-ring (Ctrl+Y yank, Alt+Y yank-pop, Ctrl+T/Alt+T transpose, Alt+U/L/C case, Ctrl+_/Cmd+Z undo, redo), control (Ctrl+L clear, Ctrl+R reverse-history-search, Ctrl+G cancel). Full matrix + status in docs/tui-keybindings.md. Detect terminal capability + degrade gracefully (some chords need kitty-keyboard/Cmd forwarding).
items.done: The composer supports the full readline/Emacs editing set + macOS Cmd conventions per docs/tui-keybindings.md, verified against a capable terminal; unsupported-chord terminals degrade without breaking.

items.title: Shift-select + clipboard ops in the composer
items.summary: A selection model (anchor+cursor) + the shift-select shortcuts: Shift+arrows (char/line), Shift+Alt+arrows (word), Shift+Cmd+arrows (to line start/end), Shift+Home/End, Cmd+A select-all; typing/cut replaces the selection; copy (Cmd+C) reads it, cut (Cmd+X), paste (Cmd+V). Prerequisite for the selection-based shortcuts in TUI-KEYS. Builds on the existing /copy + /paste. docs/tui-keybindings.md.
items.done: Shift+arrow/word/line extends a visible selection in the composer; Cmd+A selects all; Cmd+C/X/V copy/cut/paste operate on the selection; typing over a selection replaces it.

items.title: Collapse long pastes to [Pasted text #N] (Claude-CLI style)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-05 (core). tui/paste.ts (pure: shouldCollapse/pasteRef/collapse/expandPastes + PasteStore) + composer.tsx wiring: a large pasted block (>=200 chars or >=4 lines, delivered as one input event) is replaced in the composer with a compact '[Pasted text #N +L lines]' reference and stashed; expanded back to the full text on submit; store resets on clear. Stops a long paste from flooding the TUI (the wall-of-text Jason hit). 9 unit tests, full suite 1101 green, tsc clean. CAVEAT: collapse fires only when the terminal delivers the paste as one event (bracketed paste); char-by-char terminals fall back to normal insert. Interactive feel needs a real-terminal paste to confirm. Spec: docs/tui-keybindings.md.
items.done: Pasting a long block shows a one-line [Pasted text #N +L lines] in the composer instead of flooding it; submitting sends the full expanded text. Pure core unit-tested; interactive verification on a real terminal pending.

NOT a generic dashboard — match interaction model first, then Vanta's operator/dossier aesthetic.
items.done: Closes when DESKTOP-P0..P12 ship: a native Electron Vanta app with streaming chat, sessions, approval-first actions, model control, file/terminal/preview rails, palette + command center.

items.done: Pick a model in the TUI → it's still the model next launch.

items.title: @-context references
items.summary: Composer @-autocomplete for files — extended (per the audit) to @file/@diff/@url context references (multiple agents including Claude Code have this). A cheap externalize-context affordance: less typing/recall to hand Vanta context. ND fit F2+F4. Promoted from horizon by the audit.
items.done: Type @ in the TUI → autocomplete files/diff/url and inline them as context.

items.title: Calmer TUI (less firehose)
items.summary: tui/tool-display.ts (pure, 14 tests): toolDisplay(name,args)→{icon,verb,detail} computed at dispatch time — clean verbs + a per-tool verb map (read/wrote/ran/searched/saw screen/git·sub/…), abbrevPath (temp dirs incl NSIRD → basename, $HOME→~, deep→…/last/two), never raw JSON. Detail comes from ARGS not raw output (kills the temp-path leak; output→model only). On error a red firstLine tail stays visible. partitionBlocks groups consecutive tool rows into one indented cluster. transcript.tsx renders the parts purely; app.tsx reducer carries icon/verb/detail + errorLine. Design note: docs/tui-smooth-design.md. DEFERRED (needs Jason's interactive design): collapse/expand folding + the 'does it feel calm' judgment — Hashmark turned out to be a web app, not a transferable TUI reference.
items.done: A multi-tool turn reads as a calm grouped feed — no raw temp paths, no JSON dumps, indented clusters. Interactive collapse/expand flagged for brainstorming.

items.title: Dark factory (self-improving codebase)
items.summary: factory/ module: triage→plan→execute→verify→commit. vanta improve + vanta factory approve.
items.done: Live end-to-end: verifier correctly rejected bad model output.

items.title: Autonomy ladder (L1–L4, kernel-bounded)
items.summary: factory/run.ts now runs at a configurable autonomy level: L1 suggest (plan, no branch) · L2 implement (branch+execute+verify, stop for review) · L3 commit (no push) · L4 push. resolveAutonomyLevel(sub,env): improve=L1, approve=VANTA_AUTONOMY_LEVEL (default 4). Split commitAndPush→commitSlice+pushBranch; new CycleResult statuses implemented + committed{pushed}. Kernel is_protected_path still blocks skeleton/brainstem at every level. L5 auto-merge + low-risk classifier deferred (riskiest rung).
items.done: vanta factory approve with VANTA_AUTONOMY_LEVEL=2|3|4 stops exactly at implement/commit/push; 15 run.test.ts incl. resolveAutonomyLevel.

items.title: Autonomy L5 (auto-merge low-risk)
items.summary: factory/merge.ts: assessMergeRisk fails-closed through 3 gates — armed (VANTA_AUTONOMY_ALLOW_MERGE, default OFF), safe target (resolveMergeTarget default factory/integration, never main/master, --no-ff never force), low-risk (limbs/reflexes/memory only via O11, no dep/env/config/migration file, ≤20 files & ≤400 diff lines). MAX_AUTONOMY_LEVEL 4→5. run.ts: after L4 push, an armed+low-risk slice merges into the integration branch (mergeIntoTarget restores HEAD, aborts on conflict/missing target → stays L4). The git lifecycle runs outside the kernel assess() gate, so the classifier is the whole safety story; it's pure + 11 merge.test.ts. Merge git sequence smoke-verified (clean + conflict-abort + missing-target).
items.done: A low-risk verified slice auto-merges when armed; anything failing the classifier stops at L4 push. Default OFF (riskiest rung — opt-in).

items.title: Compartmentalized self-repair (body model)
items.summary: factory/compartments.ts: classifyCompartment(file)→tier (skeleton/brainstem/limbs/reflexes/memory), compartmentMaxAutonomy(tier)→max L (skeleton 0 · brainstem 2 · limbs/reflexes/memory 5), autonomyCapForFiles(files)→most-restrictive cap. run.ts clamps the requested autonomy level to the cap of the files a slice ACTUALLY touched (mixed slice → minimum). A brainstem fix can't auto-commit even at L4; only limbs/reflexes/memory reach the full ladder. is_protected_path stays the hard boundary; this is the soft policy it can't express.
items.done: A tier map exists + the factory refuses to exceed a compartment's max autonomy: limb fixes flow autonomously, brainstem/skeleton don't. 12 compartments.test.ts.

Out-of-zone still refused (backstop vs yes-fatigue).
items.done: Live-verified: write_file wrote directly to ~/Desktop in one step — no repo pollution, no shell copy.

items.title: Readable zones (read across the workspace)

Mirror of SCOPE-1: read_file now reads from readable zones — default = the project's PARENT dir (sibling repos in the same workspace) + the writable zones; VANTA_READABLE_DIRS override. Shared isInZone/expandHome helper. Follow-up: secret-filename read-guard (.env/keys) even in-zone.
items.done: Live-verified: read theft-kit/design-html/SKILL.md (64KB); ~/.ssh/id_rsa still refused.
