items.summary: vanta mcp serve — exposes Vanta tools over MCP stdio, kernel-gated + read-only allowlist. console.log→stderr keeps the protocol stream clean.
items.done: Live-verified: real McpClient drove vanta mcp serve subprocess — handshake, tools/list, allow→execute, out-of-scope refused, non-allowlisted refused. Claude Code integration untested.

items.title: Prefer-local routing
items.summary: Auto-route simple work to local Ollama on M4 Pro.
items.done: Simple tasks dispatch to Ollama without manual VANTA_PROVIDER override.

items.title: Task-initiation affordance
items.summary: From the 4-way audit (see audit.html): the executive-function activation wall is the #1 ND need and NO surveyed agent (Goose/Claude Code/others) targets it directly — Vanta's true differentiator, not a copy. A 'start the next tiny step' affordance: given a goal, surface ONE concrete smallest next action and let the user begin with a single keystroke. ND fit F1 (initiation) + F5 (structure).
items.done: From a stated goal, Vanta proposes one tiny next step and the user can start it in one action — measured: time-from-open-to-first-action drops.

items.title: clarify tool (ask before acting)
items.summary: Ported concept from a prior clarify_tool.py reference. A tool that lets the agent ask ONE structured question before acting when intent is ambiguous, instead of guessing and thrashing. Kernel-gated like any tool. ND fit F1+F4+F6 (predictable, low-vigilance, ask-don't-assume). Small surface, high fit.
items.done: On ambiguous intent Vanta asks one tappable structured question instead of guessing; wrong-guess rework drops.

items.title: Plan-first / converse mode
items.summary: Multiple agent references converged here (a prior 'converse mode' + Goose '/plan'). An opt-in mode where Vanta clarifies → lays out the steps → confirms → then acts, instead of acting immediately. Breaks blank-page paralysis and gives a calm act-gate. Extends Vanta's autonomy ladder L1 into an interactive front door. ND fit F1+F4+F6.
items.done: A /plan (or converse) mode produces a confirmed step list before any tool runs; toggleable; integrates with the autonomy ladder.

items.title: Top-of-mind reminder (MOIM)
items.summary: Ported from Goose 'tom'/MOIM. A short pinned 'what am I doing / don't forget X' note the user sets, injected into every turn's prompt as a volatile tier. Pure working-memory offload — smallest, highest-fit ND feature. ND fit F2+F5.
items.done: User pins a top-of-mind note; it appears in the prompt every turn until cleared; survives across a session.

items.title: Per-model capability matrix
items.summary: From the Claude Code survey (claude-code-haha modelSupportOverrides) + Goose issue #9529. A small declarative map of which optional params (thinking/reasoning_effort/etc.) a model accepts, so free-typed OpenRouter/Ollama model IDs don't get sent params they 400/500 on. Lives alongside providers/catalog.ts. ND fit F6 (reliability removes vigilance burden).
items.done: Free-typing an OpenRouter/Ollama model that lacks thinking/effort no longer errors — Vanta omits unsupported params per a capability lookup.

items.title: Proactive nudge cadence
items.summary: Prior agent users hacked ADHD morning/evening standups + unprompted reminders on top of cron; make it native and GENTLE (respect F3 — a nudge, never a nag). Optional scheduled gentle check-ins tied to goals ('still on X? want the next step?'). ND fit F1+F5.
items.done: User opts into a gentle cadence; Vanta sends a calm goal-aware check-in on schedule via the gateway; easy to snooze/stop.

items.title: Voice conversational loop
items.summary: A prior voice_mode reference + an explicit accessibility demand in its user-stories ('can't type well, voice is huge'; a blind user built an NVDA addon). Vanta already has speak/transcribe tools; the gap is a live mic-in → spoken-reply loop. ND fit F1 (initiation, lower input friction) + F3 (calm). Broad ND / motor accessibility.
items.done: A voice mode runs a live transcribe→agent→speak loop in the terminal; degrades gracefully without mic/STT.

items.title: Checkpoints + /rollback
items.summary: Ported concept from a prior checkpoint_manager.py reference. Shadow-git auto-snapshot before destructive ops + a /rollback to restore. Vanta's kernel BLOCKS risky ops but doesn't snapshot/restore — an undo safety net lowers anxiety around letting the agent act. ND fit F6 (trust). Opt-in, dedup store.
items.done: Vanta snapshots before a destructive op; /rollback restores the prior state; never touches kernel-protected paths.

items.title: Typed stream-event contract
items.summary: Ported concept from a prior local-agent customization (stream_events.py + stream_dispatch.py): a typed event vocabulary (message chunk/stop, commentary, tool-call chunk/finished, long-tool hint, notice) that names WHAT happened, and a per-surface dispatcher where a surface that can't render tool chrome can 'eat' it. Generalizes TUI-SMOOTH's calm feed beyond the TUI (telegram/webhook). ND fit F3 (calm output everywhere).
items.done: Agent emits typed events; each surface (TUI/telegram/webhook) renders or suppresses per its capability; the TUI feed is driven by the typed contract.

items.title: Recovery / degraded CLI
items.summary: From the Claude Code survey (claude-code-haha localRecoveryCli): a plain readline fallback behind a flag for when Ink can't render (CI, bad TERM, native-dep failure). Vanta's readline REPL partly covers this; formalize it as a guaranteed degraded mode. ND fit F6 (reliability — always reachable).
items.done: VANTA_FORCE_RECOVERY_CLI (or auto-detect) runs a minimal readline agent loop when the Ink TUI can't start.

items.title: Live roadmap kanban (you + Vanta control) + WIP limit
items.summary: The roadmap IS already a kanban (status enum = columns; render.ts renders Now/Next/Later). Make it bi-directionally LIVE, source of truth stays roadmap.json (agent-ready, git-versioned). (1) Vanta control: roadmap_move tool + `vanta roadmap move <id> <status>` (kernel-gated write→regenerate); the dark factory moves cards next→building on start, building→shipped on verified-commit. (2) Human control: roadmap.html drag-to-move POSTs to a tiny local endpoint (vanta roadmap serve / kernel /api/roadmap/move) that rewrites roadmap.json. (3) THE EF FEATURE — a hard WIP limit on Now(building)=1-2: moving a 3rd card in refuses and asks you to finish or park one. WIP limit = the anti-bounce/finish-what-you-start prosthesis (targets the documented ideas-rich/finish-poor pattern + this session’s live 8-goal bounce). Ship #1 first (smallest slice). Research: docs/kanban-roadmap-research.md. ND fit F2+F4+F5+F6.
items.done: Both you and Vanta move cards on one live board (roadmap.json source); Now is WIP-limited and refuses a 3rd card until one is finished or parked.

items.title: Dynamic workflows (agent writes its own orchestration harness)
items.summary: From Anthropic’s Claude Code team (Thariq Shihipar & Sid Bidasaria, “A harness for every task”, /Desktop PDF). Let Vanta write its own JS orchestration harness on the fly to spawn+coordinate sub-agents, extending the existing delegate+swarm. Canonical patterns: classify-and-act, fan-out-and-synthesize, adversarial-verification, generate-and-filter, tournament, loop-until-done. Combats the long-context failure modes the article names — agentic laziness (stops at 20/50 items), self-preferential bias (prefers own results when judging), goal drift (loses the objective after compaction) — by giving each sub-agent its own clean context + isolated goal. Token budgets (“use 10k tokens”), worktree isolation, model/intelligence routing per subtask, save/reuse workflows. VISION TIE-IN: this is executive function FOR THE AGENT — making Vanta itself finish and stay on-objective — the mirror of the human-EF moat. Pairs with Vanta’s /goal + a /loop equivalent.
items.done: Vanta can compose+run a multi-agent workflow (fan-out/synthesize, adversarial-verify, tournament, loop-until-done) with a token budget; a long/parallel task that single-context drifts on completes without agentic laziness or goal drift.

items.title: Safe kernel self-repair (propose→prove→swap)
items.summary: Design: docs/self-repair-architecture.md. Limbs already self-heal (O11+factory); this closes the skeleton/kernel tier WITHOUT letting the running boundary edit itself in place. (1) propose-only candidate kernel binary, never overwrite live; (2) differential safety-invariant conformance — the OLD kernel judges the NEW, reject if more permissive on any Rule-Zero case; (3) blue-green shadow swap + auto-rollback to last-good (never breaks the system); (4) human promotion for any safety-contract diff, classifier FAILS CLOSED; (5) immutable trust root = the promotion authority + invariant spec (machine-checkable twin of MANIFESTO Rule Zero) — the one thing Vanta cannot self-modify. ND fit F6 (an agent that maintains itself removes the vigilance burden). HORIZON — kernel blast radius; kernel stays hard-blocked until built.
items.done: A non-weakening kernel change promotes via conformance + blue-green with auto-rollback; any safety-contract change requires human promotion; the live boundary is never edited in place.

items.title: Born-small codegen (registry-by-default + 300 gate)
items.summary: Make the dark factory GENERATE small, independently-repairable units by default (the broken-leg property without later refactoring). Doc: docs/factory-evolution.md. 3 human-authored slices on factory/*.ts (skeleton): (1) verifier HARD GATE — pure checkNewFilesUnderLineLimit on NEW source files only (<=300; new-files-only is universally safe vs false-positiving bug fixes to pre-existing large files) + vanta-ts/CONVENTIONS.md (3-tier taxonomy: HARD GATE/GUIDANCE/CONVENTION DOC + the Tools-registry template); (2) planner gene-transfusion — prepend a PROVEN-PATTERNS block (spec wins on conflict) to buildInstruction for roadmap+parked only; (3) executor wiring — inject per-dir CLAUDE.md from plan.touchedDirs (closes the loop: factory requires those docs but never reads them back). Canonical template = tools/index.ts ALL_TOOLS + buildRegistry; mirrored by repl/handlers HANDLERS.
items.done: Factory refuses to birth a >300-line unit, instructs new-feature work to land as a registry unit (new file + one entry, no switch edit), and feeds each dir's own CLAUDE.md back to the agent.

items.title: Holdout author-separation validation
items.summary: From OctopusGarden + StrongDM + Willison (triple-confirmed). The codegen agent must NOT author its own acceptance check — today the same cycle writes code + test, so it can write a weak test (self-preferential-bias / reward-hacking). Hold a scenario/acceptance set the executor never sees; a separate implementation-blind agent (or pre-existing scenario) judges it. This is Vanta's SECOND validation layer (the kernel is the first) — NOT a kernel replacement. Adapt the idea, not the Digital-Twin-Universe machinery (irrelevant to self-improvement).
items.done: A factory slice is accepted only against criteria the implementing agent never saw; a slice that games its own test is caught.

items.title: Intent-satisfaction LLM-judge gate
items.summary: The biggest concrete gap found in the audit: the verifier proves code is CORRECT (tests pass, tsc clean) but never that it DID WHAT THE ITEM ASKED — a slice can pass every test and not fulfill the work item. Add an LLM-as-judge gate scoring intent-satisfaction against the work item; keep BOOLEAN for the deterministic checks (boolean is correct for did-the-build-break — do NOT fuzz those), judged only for subjective intent.
items.done: A slice that passes tests but doesn't address the work item's intent is rejected by the intent judge; deterministic gates stay boolean.

items.title: Ambiguity-gated preflight (unifies with ND clarify)
items.summary: From Ouroboros (Ambiguity = 1 - sum(clarity*weight), gate before codegen) — take the lightweight GATE, not the 9-minds/Double-Diamond ceremony. Before running a cycle, assess whether the work item is clear enough; if not, clarify or skip rather than burn tokens + drift. One mechanism serving BOTH the factory preflight AND the ND ND2 clarify / ND3 plan-first items.
items.done: The factory refuses to execute a too-vague item and routes it to clarify; clear items proceed. Shared with the ND clarify/plan-first surface.

items.title: Model escalation + per-cycle cost ledger
items.summary: Answers Willison's ~$1k/day cost caution and serves the frugality drive. Start a cycle on a cheap/local model; escalate to a stronger model after N non-improving iterations, demote after improvements (OctopusGarden pattern). Add per-cycle cost/token accounting (Vanta has budgets but no escalation or cost ledger). Ties to routing/model-router + delegate.
items.done: A factory cycle starts frugal, escalates only when stalled, and records per-cycle token/cost; cheap work never pays premium-model cost.

items.title: Stall recovery / bounded-retry
items.summary: Today a verify-fail discards the slice with no recovery. Adapt Ouroboros stagnation detection (spinning/oscillation/no-drift/diminishing-returns, bounded <=N iterations) + OctopusGarden wonder->reflect (high-temp diagnose -> surgical fix). Replace single-shot discard with bounded iterate + a strategy switch on stall.
items.done: A stalled cycle detects the stall pattern and retries with a changed strategy within a bounded budget instead of discarding immediately.

items.title: Work-item closure loop
items.summary: When the factory ships a work item it should CLOSE it — tick the ROADMAP/PARKED checkbox or move the KANBAN card to shipped — not just commit code. Closes the triage->done loop so the backlog reflects reality automatically. Ties directly to the KANBAN item (Vanta moving cards as it works).
items.done: A shipped factory slice automatically closes/moves its source work item; the board reflects what was actually shipped without manual edits.

items.title: Composer basics: input history + multiline
items.summary: Vanta's composer is single-line and explicitly ignores up/down (composer.tsx line 75). All four references have these table-stakes: (1) input HISTORY — up/down recalls prior messages (Gemini, Claude Code); (2) MULTILINE input — shift+enter / backslash / paste a block (all); (3) graceful large-paste (paste a big block → collapse to a reference rather than flooding the line). The #1 'missing normal stuff'. ND fit F1 (lower input friction).
items.done: Up/down recalls history; shift+enter inserts a newline; pasting a large block doesn't break the line.

items.title: Esc to interrupt a running turn
items.summary: Claude Code / Codex / Gemini all let Esc cancel a running turn. Vanta HAS the mechanism (createConversation signal + AbortController, stoppedReason 'interrupted') but the TUI never wires a key to it — you can't stop a runaway turn from the Ink UI (only the one-shot CLI has SIGINT). Wire Esc → controller.abort() in app.tsx. ND fit F6 (control/trust) + F3 (stop the firehose).
items.done: Pressing Esc during a busy turn aborts it cleanly between iterations and returns control to the composer.

items.title: Markdown + syntax-highlight rendering
items.summary: Assistant output renders as plain <Text> today. Gemini + Claude Code render markdown (headings/lists/bold/links) + syntax-highlighted code blocks. For an ND-readability product this is core F3 (calm, scannable output, not a wall of monospace) — arguably Vanta's highest-value TUI gap given the vision. Use an Ink markdown renderer + a highlighter; respect the calm aesthetic (no neon).
items.done: Assistant messages render markdown structure + highlighted code blocks; output is scannable, not a flat wall of text.

items.title: In-TUI mode / approval toggle (shift+tab)
items.summary: Claude Code cycles normal/auto-accept/plan with shift+tab; Codex has /approvals + mode switch; Gemini has a yolo toggle. Vanta gates via the kernel + autonomy ladder (L1-5) but offers no in-session toggle — you can't flip 'ask me every time' vs 'just go' without env vars. Map a shift+tab cycle onto the autonomy ladder / approval posture (kernel still the hard floor). ND fit F4 (one control for how much it asks).
items.done: A visible mode indicator cycles via shift+tab (e.g. review / auto within kernel bounds); the kernel remains the hard floor.

items.title: Diff rendering for file edits
items.summary: Codex + Claude Code render a colored diff when the agent edits a file, before/at approval. Vanta's write_file shows a clean tool line (post TUI-SMOOTH) but no diff — you can't see WHAT changed in the TUI. Render a unified diff for write_file/edits in the activity feed + the approval prompt. ND fit F6 (see exactly what it did → trust).
items.done: A file edit shows a concise colored diff in the feed and in its approval prompt.

items.title: Prefix shortcuts: bash (!) + memory (#)
items.summary: Claude Code: '!' runs a shell command inline (output into context), '#' appends a line to memory without a full command. Vanta has shell_cmd as a tool + /memory as a command but no fast prefixes. Add composer prefixes: leading ! → shell (kernel-gated), leading # → brain/memory append. ND fit F1 (lower friction for the two most common quick actions).
items.done: Typing '! <cmd>' runs a kernel-gated shell command; '# <note>' appends to the brain — no slash command needed.

items.title: Custom user slash commands
items.summary: Claude Code + Gemini let users define their own slash commands (markdown/prompt files in a commands dir). Vanta's slash set is a fixed registry (repl/handlers.ts). Add user-defined commands discovered from ~/.vanta/commands/ (or skills) that expand into a prompt — extend the HANDLERS registry at load. ND fit F1/F5 (capture a repeated workflow as one keystroke).
items.done: A user drops a command file in ~/.vanta/commands/; it appears in the palette and runs as a templated prompt.

items.title: Persistent rich status line
items.summary: Gemini + Claude Code show a persistent status line (model · context % · tokens · sometimes cost). Vanta has a one-line hint + /context + /usage commands but no always-on status. Add a calm persistent footer: model, context-fill %, token/cost (ties to FAC-ESCALATE cost ledger). ND fit F2/F6 (ambient state without asking).
items.done: A persistent footer shows model + context% + tokens; updates live, stays calm (no flicker).

items.title: Themes incl. accessibility (high-contrast / dyslexia)
items.summary: Gemini ships selectable themes. For an ND product this is accessibility, not decoration: a high-contrast theme, a low-arousal muted theme, and a dyslexia-friendly option (spacing/weight). Token-drive the TUI colors + VANTA_THEME. ND fit F3 (sensory regulation — let the user tune arousal/contrast).
items.done: VANTA_THEME selects among calm / high-contrast / dyslexia-friendly palettes; persists like the model choice.

items.title: Thinking / reasoning display (collapsible)
items.summary: Claude Code shows (collapsible) thinking; Codex exposes reasoning levels. Vanta's loop discards/ignores reasoning in the TUI. When a provider returns reasoning, show it dim + collapsed (expand on key) so the user can see WHY without the firehose. ND fit F3 (optional depth, calm by default) + F6 (transparency).
items.done: Reasoning, when present, shows as a dim collapsed line that expands on demand; off by default to stay calm.

items.title: Vim-mode composer editing
items.summary: Gemini + Claude Code offer vim-mode line editing. Lower priority but cheap given the custom composer already owns key handling (composer.tsx). Add an opt-in modal (normal/insert) editing mode behind VANTA_VIM. ND fit F1 (muscle-memory for vim users = lower friction).
items.done: VANTA_VIM enables modal editing in the composer (normal/insert, basic motions); off by default.

items.title: Keyboard shortcut help overlay
items.summary: Most TUIs offer a '?' / shortcut to a key-binding cheat sheet. Vanta has /help (commands) but no key-binding reference and the custom composer keys (ctrl a/e/u/k/w, etc.) are undiscoverable. Add a '?' overlay listing keys + modes. ND fit F2 (don't make me remember the bindings).
items.done: A '?' overlay lists every composer key, slash command, and mode toggle; dismiss with esc.

items.title: Verbatim session archive + semantic search
items.summary: MemPalace-inspired: store session transcripts verbatim in ~/.vanta/archive/ (JSONL). Add vanta memory search <query> — keyword retrieval across all past sessions. Replace LLM-summary-only memory with a verbatim layer (summaries become an optional index tier). Recall quality: exact words vs paraphrased summaries. ND fit F2+F6.
items.done: vanta memory search returns the verbatim exchange matching the query; sessions are archived verbatim on close.

items.title: Claude Code Stop/PreCompact hooks for Vanta memory
items.summary: MemPalace-inspired: install Claude Code Stop + PreCompact hooks (scripts/vanta-memhook.sh) that fire when Claude Code stops or compacts context. Stop hook runs vanta memory mine in the background every N turns (VANTA_MEMHOOK_INTERVAL, default 10); PreCompact hook runs synchronously before context is lost. Closes the loop: Vanta preserves its own working memory across Claude Code sessions. Zero tokens in chat.
items.done: Claude Code Stop hook mines the Vanta transcript into memory every 10 turns, in background, zero tokens in chat. PreCompact hook runs synchronously.

items.title: Structured memory hierarchy (goals > sessions > turns)
items.summary: MemPalace wing/room/drawer model adapted for Vanta: goals=wings, sessions=rooms, turns=drawers. Replace flat ~/.vanta/memories/<goalId>.md with a 3-tier hierarchy. Pairs with MEM-VERBATIM: search scopes to the right room before scanning drawers. ND fit F2 (structure as scaffolding for recall).
items.done: Memory is organized by goal/session/turn; search scopes to the right room first; no more flat pile of summaries.

items.title: SQLite knowledge graph (goals x entities x decisions)
items.summary: MemPalace knowledge_graph.py adapted for Vanta: temporal entity-relationship graph in ~/.vanta/graph.db. Entities=people/projects/tools, relationships=worked-on/decided/depends-on with timestamps. Enables cross-session queries: what goals mention tool X, what did we decide about Y last week. Ties MEM-VERBATIM + MEM-STRUCT together. ND fit F2+F5.
items.done: vanta graph query returns goals/sessions/decisions connected to an entity; graph updates on session close.

items.title: Multi-dimensional brain (5D: type x time x strength x relations x decay)
items.summary: Replace the 7 flat brain region.md files with a typed, timestamped, confidence-scored, graph-connected, decay-aware entry model. Dimensions: (1) type/region, (2) createdAt/updatedAt, (3) strength 0-1, (4) relatedIds cross-references, (5) forgetAfter decay. Store in ~/.vanta/brain.db (SQLite). AgentMemory Memory type as the reference model. Each brain write now carries strength + timestamp; the brainDigest injection selects by strength+recency, not just all text. Auto-decay stale entries (7d unless reinforced). ND fit F6 (Vanta has an honest, maintained self-model, not a stale dump).
items.done: Each brain entry has a strength score; brainDigest injects top-K by strength*recency; stale entries decay; conflicting beliefs show version history.

items.title: Observation compression pipeline (raw event -> facts -> memory)
items.summary: AgentMemory-inspired 3-stage pipeline: (1) raw hook observations (tool calls, user prompts, assistant responses); (2) compressed observations (type, title, facts[], narrative, concepts[], importance); (3) consolidated memories (pattern/preference/architecture/bug/workflow/fact). Today reviewAfterTurn extracts skills. This extends it to extract all typed knowledge. Each tool result that produces insight generates a CompressedObservation; the consolidator promotes recurring patterns to long-term Memory entries. ND fit F5 (Vanta builds a real mental model, not just a skills list).
items.done: A file edit, decision, or discovery generates a typed CompressedObservation; recurring patterns crystallize into Memory entries; the brain grows from experience.

items.title: Working memory (hot session cache + injection)
items.summary: AgentMemory-inspired working memory: a session-scoped cache of the most relevant observations from the current session (vs long-term memory which is cross-session). Injected into the prompt as a volatile tier above the stable brain digest, below MOIM. Size-capped (VANTA_WORKING_MEM_TOKENS, default 2k). Auto-populated from current-session tool results and user prompts. ND fit F2+F3 (Vanta tracks what just happened without re-reading the full brain).
items.done: A working-memory block appears in the system prompt for active sessions; it reflects the last N tool calls and decisions without hitting long-term memory.

items.title: Memory versioning (supersedes chain, no data loss)
items.summary: AgentMemory-inspired version chain: when a belief is updated (e.g., preferred provider changed), the old entry is archived with a supersedes pointer rather than overwritten. Brain writes use version+parentId. Enables: vanta brain history <entry> to see how a belief evolved; auto-detection of conflicting beliefs (two entries with same concept but different content). ND fit F6 (trust - Vanta cant accidentally forget something important by overwriting).
items.done: Updating a brain entry creates a new version; vanta brain history shows the chain; conflicting entries are flagged, not silently dropped.

items.title: Multi-tier memory injection (avoid dark-memory gap)
items.summary: AgentMemory issue #732: their highest-value memory tiers (semantic facts, procedural skills, crystals, insights) are written by consolidation but never read for injection -- the data is dark. Vanta risk: brain regions get written by brain tool but only the brainDigest subset reaches the prompt. Fix: (1) verify every write path has a corresponding read/inject path; (2) add typed memory tiers beyond the 7 regions (semantic_fact, procedural, insight, pattern); (3) per-tier injection budget so small tiers do not crowd out others. Never add a memory type without wiring its consumption.
items.done: Every tier that gets written is also injected (or explicitly excluded with documented reason); no dark memory.

items.title: Stable prompt prefix for LLM cache hit rate
items.summary: AgentMemory issue #720: per-turn system prompt mutation (injecting current time, session-specific context) drops prompt cache hit rate ~10pp because prefix matching fails on any change. Vanta buildSystemPrompt() injects now: timestamp in the volatile tier every turn, invalidating the stable prefix. Fix: freeze the stable tier (SOUL + tools + rules + skills index) as a constant string computed once per session; inject now/goals/moim only in a trailing volatile block that never touches the prefix. Anthropic ephemeral caching matches on the first N tokens -- keep those stable.
items.done: Stable prompt prefix never changes within a session; volatile suffix changes freely; cache hit rate measurably improves.

items.title: Worktree-aware project identity for memory scoping
items.summary: AgentMemory issue #728: git worktrees return the worktree root from git rev-parse --show-toplevel, not the main repo. Memory fragments across worktrees of the same project. Vanta uses worktrees extensively (superpowers:using-git-worktrees). Fix: for memory and session scoping, use git remote get-url origin (canonical repo URL) as the project identity, falling back to git rev-parse --show-toplevel for the main checkout. Worktree sessions share memory with the main branch.
items.done: A session run from a worktree of Vanta reads/writes the same memory as a session run from the main checkout.

items.title: Conversation timestamps in memory (not ingest time)
items.summary: MemPalace issue #1649: the miner writes filed_at (ingest time) but the transcript already carries per-message timestamps. A session from 3 weeks ago mined today shows filed_at=today, breaking time-based recall. Vanta sessions store messages as Message[] with no timestamp. Fix: add timestamp to the Message type (record at send time); propagate to memory store entries. Enables: vanta memory search --since 7d, temporal graph queries, correct time anchoring for what-did-we-do-last-Tuesday recall.
items.done: Every memory entry carries the conversation timestamp (not ingest time); time-scoped search works.

items.title: Canonical project identity from git remote URL
items.summary: AgentMemory issues #716/#733: projects named by directory basename collide across machines and clones (~/work/foo vs ~/dev/foo = same project, different memory silos). Fix: resolve project identity from git remote get-url origin (e.g., github.com/jpoindexter/Vanta) and use that as the memory namespace key. Fall back to directory name for non-git roots. Enables memory portability: clone the repo on a new machine and existing memories surface. VANTA_PROJECT_ID env override for non-git use.
items.done: Memory scoping uses the git remote URL; cloning to a new path preserves memory continuity.

items.title: Full neurocognitive brain architecture (12-axis memory space)
items.summary: Beyond 5D: a complete brain modelled on human cognitive architecture. 12 axes per memory node: (1) type (fact/skill/preference/pattern/insight/plan/emotion), (2) temporal (created/modified/accessed), (3) confidence 0-1 (epistemic uncertainty), (4) strength 0-1 (consolidation score), (5) salience (relevance to current context), (6) associative (graph edges by type: relates-to/caused-by/contradicts/extends), (7) provenance (source session + tool call + user utterance), (8) forgetting curve (half-life, Ebbinghaus decay), (9) goal linkage (which goal this serves), (10) granularity (atom/pattern/insight/principle), (11) activation state (working/long-term/archived), (12) distinctiveness (dedup score vs nearest neighbors). Implementation: ~/.vanta/brain.db SQLite + vector index. Synthesis layer generates insights by combining high-strength nodes. Contradiction detector flags conflicting beliefs. Prospective memory (future plans) injected separately from episodic. This is BRAIN-5D extended -- implement 5D first, then layer remaining axes.
items.done: Vanta has a living, self-maintaining belief graph: memories decay unless reinforced, contradictions surface, insights crystallize from recurring patterns, provenance is always traceable.

items.title: Active task stack — breadcrumbs + goal re-injection (working memory prosthetic)
items.summary: PMC4455841 working memory: the agent loses the thread mid-subtask, especially after context compaction. Three parts: (1) /where slash command — surfaces the last stated intent + the last N tool calls as a brief breadcrumb (where were we before this rabbit hole); (2) Active task stack — before entering a subtask, push the parent goal; on return, pop and re-assert it; (3) Goal re-injection after compaction — when the context is compressed, the active goal is prepended to the next system prompt volatile tier explicitly. This is the architectural version of the working memory phonologic loop — keep the goal in the "rehearsal buffer". ND fit F1+F2 (start the right thing, stay on it).
items.done: /where shows the thread; subtask entry/exit is logged; goal survives compaction and is re-asserted at the top of the next turn.

items.title: Pre-action goal check (inhibition support — soft gate, not wall)
items.summary: PMC4455841 inhibition: the healthy prefrontal cortex suppresses prepotent (automatic/habitual) responses before they execute. Vanta has no equivalent pre-action check — scope creep, rabbit holes, and while-we-are-here additions happen because there is no inhibition layer. Design: before each tool call, a lightweight check compares the tool + args against the active goal and recent instruction. Not a hard block (that is the kernel) — a soft annotation: log when a call is adjacent-to but not directly-serving the stated goal. After N adjacent calls in a row, surface: "I notice I am doing X, original goal was Y — continue?". Prevents perseverative drift without breaking flow. ND fit F4+F6.
items.done: Off-goal tool calls generate a logged annotation; after 3 adjacent calls, Vanta surfaces a drift alert; user can confirm or redirect.

items.title: Auto strategy rotation + ERRORS.md pre-read (set shifting support)
items.summary: PMC4455841 set shifting: perseverative loops happen because the agent lacks a signal to change strategy. Two parts: (1) Before attempting any task, auto-scan ERRORS.md for prior failures on similar tasks (ERRORS.md is currently human-checked only — make it agent-checked at session start and task start); (2) Approach diversity tracker: when 3+ consecutive iterations follow the same pattern without progress (not just identical tool calls — similar strategic direction), auto-trigger a strategy pivot with a different approach vector. The FAC-STALL roadmap item covers the factory version; EF-SETSHIFT covers the interactive session. Ties to the dorsolateral PFC set-shifting function. ND fit F1+F6.
items.done: Vanta checks ERRORS.md before similar tasks; after 3 non-progressing similar-direction attempts, proposes a strategy switch.

items.title: Complexity gate — auto-prompt to plan before executing complex requests
items.summary: PMC4455841 planning/organization: difficulty breaking complex tasks into steps, sequencing, anticipating obstacles. Currently Vanta has /planmode as an opt-in toggle. But the user must know to use it. Fix: lightweight complexity classifier on each incoming user message — estimate task complexity by indicators (multi-file, schema change, migration, cross-service, ambiguous spec). If complexity > threshold, auto-suggest plan-first mode before any tool call. Same principle as the kernel ambiguity gate (FAC-PREFLIGHT) but for the interactive session. User can dismiss with one key to proceed directly. ND fit F1+F5 (breaks the blank-page paralysis, provides structure before confusion).
items.done: Complex requests trigger a one-line plan prompt before tool use; user can dismiss; simple requests are not interrupted.

items.title: Pre-action self-monitor (right-hemisphere analog)
items.summary: PMC4455841 anatomy: the right prefrontal cortex specializes in self-monitoring — error detection, inconsistency detection, checking output before committing. Vanta has post-turn background review (reviewAfterTurn) but no PRE-action check. Design: before committing a significant action (write_file overwrite, shell command, commit), the system runs a one-shot "does this make sense given the goal?" check using a fast cheap model (haiku). Not a hard gate (the kernel is that) — a sanity check that catches "about to write the wrong file" or "this shell command will affect more than intended" before execution. Catches the class of errors that the user notices immediately post-action but that are expensive to reverse. ND fit F6 (trust — the agent double-checks itself).
items.done: Before destructive/significant tool calls, a fast self-monitor check runs; inconsistencies with the stated goal are surfaced before execution.

items.title: Explicit task boundaries — clean context handoff on topic switch
items.summary: PMC4455841 set shifting: context pollution from previous tasks bleeds into current work ("set" persists when it should be cleared). Vanta has /clear but no lightweight mid-session boundary. Design: when the agent detects a significant topic shift mid-session (classifier on user message vs active goal), propose a task-boundary event: summarize the previous task state, archive it to brain/session memory, and begin fresh with the new task context. Unlike /clear (full wipe), task boundary preserves the conversation but explicitly marks the shift, preventing the prior approach from contaminating the new task. Optional: /boundary slash command as manual trigger. ND fit F4+F5 (one control that says "new thing now", structured handoff).
items.done: Topic shifts generate a task-boundary event; prior task state is archived; new task starts clean without full context loss.

items.title: Two-network brain: salience network + executive control network (separate)
items.summary: PMC4455841 neuroanatomy: two DISTINCT intrinsic connectivity networks. (1) Executive Control Network (DLPFC, parietal, basal ganglia, thalamus, cerebellum) — planning, working memory, inhibition, set shifting. (2) Salience Network (frontal insula, anterior cingulate, ventromedial PFC, limbic) — determines WHAT MATTERS: emotional/social relevance, interoceptive state monitoring (internal load, stress level), attention allocation. Frontotemporal dementia specifically destroys the salience network, not the executive control network — this is why social behavior breaks first. Vanta currently conflates these: brain/mood is vaguely salience-adjacent but not architected as a separate system. BRAIN-NEURO must implement both, with explicit interaction: salience filters what enters executive processing; executive control determines how to act on what salience flagged. ND fit: salience network degradation = inability to determine priorities (everything feels equally urgent).
items.done: Vanta has a named salience subsystem that determines attention allocation separate from the executive planning subsystem; mood/stress state modulates what enters active processing.

items.title: Real-time error detection (anterior cingulate analog)
items.summary: PMC4455841 neuroanatomy: the anterior cingulate cortex plays a critical role in REAL-TIME error detection — it fires during execution when an error is being committed, not after. Vanta has reviewAfterTurn (post-hoc, like a retrospective audit) and EF-SELFMONITOR (pre-action sanity check). The missing piece is mid-execution error detection: detecting when a running tool chain has drifted from the goal WHILE it is running, not just before or after. Implementation: between tool calls within a single turn, a lightweight check (not an LLM call — a heuristic) tracks whether the sequence is still converging toward the stated goal. If N consecutive tool calls produce no apparent progress toward the goal, surface an interrupt opportunity. Pairs with EF-INHIBIT (which is pre-action) and EF-SELFMONITOR (which is pre-execution). ND fit F6 (catch errors before they compound).
items.done: Mid-execution drift is detected between tool calls; after N non-converging calls, Vanta surfaces an interrupt opportunity rather than continuing on autopilot.

items.title: Working memory manipulation mode (dorsolateral PFC analog)
items.summary: PMC4455841 two working memory modes: (1) maintenance (ventrolateral PFC) — hold without transformation, example: MOIM note. (2) manipulation (dorsolateral PFC) — actively transform, update, reason about held information, example: backward digit span, serial 7s. MOIM covers maintenance only. Vanta has no manipulation mode: when given complex information that requires active reasoning (not just holding), there is no architectural support for that working. Design: a /think slash command (or auto-trigger on complex prompts) that activates a structured reasoning pass — explicitly takes held information from working memory and manipulates it toward a conclusion before acting. This is the dorsolateral PFC operation: not just remembering, but computing. ND fit F1+F4 (low-friction way to activate structured reasoning without jumping to action).
items.done: /think activates manipulation mode on held information; reasoning is explicit and visible before any tool call follows.

items.title: Research convergence gate (pattern: research spiral)
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: research spiral. Started implementation sprint, pivoted to 6+ research threads with no convergence. Inhibition failure: new discoveries reinforce continuation (reward loop). Counter-measure: after N consecutive research/analysis turns without an actionable commit or shipped item, surface a gentle gate: the turn count since last concrete output, the original stated goal, one question: want to pick one finding to build now? Not a block. A pattern interrupt that fires at the right moment. VANTA_RESEARCH_GATE_TURNS env (default 8). ND fit F1 (task initiation when stuck in exploration mode).
items.done: After 8 research turns without actionable output, Vanta surfaces the spiral pattern and offers a one-question redirect to execution.

items.title: Backlog choice reduction gate (pattern: initiation paralysis)
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: 11-item backlog → paralysis. Case 4-2 analog: paralyzed when faced with multifaceted problems. The backlog is the multifaceted problem. Counter-measure: when the next queue has > 3 items and the user asks what to do next, present ONLY the top 3 (ranked by: dependencies_met + time_since_last_touched + smallest_size). Do not show the full list. One question: which of these 3? This is occupational therapy Environmental Manipulation (simplify the task environment). Pairs with /next which reduces to a single micro-step. KANBAN WIP limit prevents adding while paralyzed. ND fit F1+F5 (structure reduces the blank-page-of-100-options problem).
items.done: When next queue > 3, /next and agenda-style prompts show only the top 3 candidates; the rest are hidden until one ships.

items.title: Velocity tracker — capture:ship ratio (pattern: ideas-rich/finish-poor)
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: roadmap grew from 0 to 100 items in 4 sessions, shipped items grew much more slowly. Fluency excess masks planning deficit (MMSE 28/30 analog: backlog looks productive, shipping rate is low). Counter-measure: rolling 7-day capture:ship ratio stored in ~/.vanta/velocity.json. When ratio > 5:1 (5 ideas captured per 1 shipped), surface on /status and at session start: capture/ship ratio is 8:1 this week — consider closing before opening. Not a block. A visible metric that makes the pattern legible. ND fit F2+F5 (ambient awareness without having to remember to check).
items.done: /status shows the rolling capture:ship ratio; ratio > 5:1 surfaces a one-line note at session start.

items.title: Scope delta tracker — topic count per turn (pattern: while-we-are-at-it)
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: while implementing FAC-INTENT, also checked MemPalace issues, added 8 roadmap items, wrote two design docs, analyzed clinical papers. Each is individually justified; together they are 6 topics in one turn. Inhibition failure: prepotent response to each interesting thing fires. Counter-measure: at the end of each assistant turn, count distinct topics/files/goals touched. If > 3, add a dim annotation: n topics this turn. Not alarming — just visible. Over a session, the annotation pattern becomes its own signal. ND fit F3+F6 (calm ambient signal without interrupting flow).
items.done: Multi-topic turns show a dim topic count annotation; annotation accumulates across a session into a visible pattern.

items.title: Closure gate — incomplete thread detector before new major threads
items.summary: Real pattern from sessions: items reach 70% completion then a new session begins on something else. Set Shifting failure: moved to a new cognitive set without completing the prior set. Incomplete thread accumulation: each thread is almost done but none gets closure. Counter-measure: when a major new thread begins (detected by: new goal set, topic shift > threshold, /clear), scan the current session and recent sessions for items that are > 50% done but not closed. Surface: 2 items appear in-progress from this session. Want to close one first? One-tap dismiss. Ties to MEM-SESSION-RECOVER (orphaned sessions). ND fit F1+F5 (makes the hidden cost of switching visible).
items.done: Before major thread switches, Vanta surfaces in-progress items; user can close them or explicitly defer with one action.

items.title: vanta update — safe self-updater with rollback
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. cli/update.ts: runUpdateCommand — autostash local changes, save pre-update snapshot to ~/.vanta/snapshots/pre-update.json, git pull --ff-only, cargo build, npm install, skill sync, service restart. --rollback flag restores last snapshot. Already-up-to-date short-circuits cleanly. 1 export test; 1652 total green; tsc clean. Live: needs a tracking branch + network.
items.done: `vanta update` fetches, writes a pre-update snapshot, refreshes deps + skills + config additively, restarts the service, and reports a clean diff; `vanta update --rollback` restores the last snapshot.

items.title: vanta model — CLI provider/model pulldown
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. cli/model-cmd.ts: runModelCommand — no-arg shows current provider+model; arg form parses via parseModelArg + persists to.env via upsertEnvMigratingLegacy. Registered as vanta model in cli.ts COMMANDS table. Usage line added to cli/commands.ts. 5 unit tests; tsc clean.
items.done: `vanta model` lists available providers/models, persists the choice to.env, the choice survives restart, and setup/update surface the tip.

items.title: Curator umbrella consolidation + pin
items.summary: The bundled curator does an umbrella-building pass Vanta's doesn't: it groups many overlapping/related skills under CLASS-LEVEL umbrella skills (e.g. apple-services, github-operations, research-workflows), archives the children (69 in one observed run), prunes stale ones, then shows a once-per-run digest with `curator status` (view anytime) and `curator pin <umbrella>` (freeze a good umbrella so the next pass won't churn it). Vanta's curate() is non-destructive archive of stale LEARNED skills only — it reports overlaps but never consolidates them. Add an LLM consolidation pass that proposes umbrellas from overlap clusters, archives children reversibly, supports pin, and emits a once-per-run digest. Reversible + approval-gated (Rule zero).
items.done: `vanta curator` proposes class-level umbrellas from overlapping skills, archives children reversibly with pin support, prunes stale, and surfaces a once-per-run digest viewable via `vanta curator status`.

items.title: Provider fallback chain on failure
items.summary: `fallback` (add/list/remove/test): when the primary model errors/rate-limits/times-out, it transparently retries the next provider in a configured chain. Vanta has task ROUTING (cheap/expensive via VANTA_MODEL_CHEAP/_EXPENSIVE) but NO failure-fallback — a dead provider just fails the turn. Add an ordered fallback chain (env or config), tried on retryable errors (429/5xx/timeout/circuit-break), with a one-line note in the transcript when it switches. Complements AUX-MAP (per-function model map). Manifesto-aligned: keeps the operator working when one backend is down.
items.done: `vanta fallback` configures an ordered provider chain; a retryable primary failure transparently falls through to the next, surfaced as a transcript note, and the choice survives restart.

items.title: Full-text search over past sessions
items.summary: `sessions browse` + search gives FTS over session history. Vanta persists sessions (`~/.vanta/sessions/<id>.json`, save/resume/fork/title) but can only list + resume by id — no search. Add `/sessions search <q>` (TUI) + `vanta sessions search` (CLI): index session message text, return ranked hits with a snippet + session id, resume from a hit. Long-flagged as the top continuity gap in parity-audit.md. Pure ranking fn unit-tested; mirrors the existing recall/searchSkills weighting.
items.done: `vanta sessions search <q>` returns ranked past sessions with snippets and resumes from a hit; `/sessions` gains an inline search filter.

items.title: vanta security audit — dependency/supply-chain scan
items.summary: `security audit` scans the venv against OSV.dev advisories and flags known-compromised packages (it caught the Shai-Hulud PyPI worm). Vanta has a hard SAFETY kernel for ACTIONS but zero visibility into its own DEPENDENCY supply chain (npm). Add `vanta security audit`: run `npm audit --json` + check installed versions against an advisory feed, report severity-ranked findings, fail-loud on critical. Manifesto-aligned (safety is the differentiator) and cheap — Vanta already pins versions and runs gitleaks pre-commit. Folds the security posture from action-time to install-time.
items.done: `vanta security audit` reports severity-ranked dependency advisories from npm audit + an advisory feed and exits non-zero on a critical finding.

items.title: Invoke installed skills as slash commands
items.summary: Skills install into ~/.vanta/skills/ and appear in the prompt index, but typing /hill-climb or any skill slug in the REPL/TUI hits the unknown-command handler instead of invoking the skill. Fix: after loading installed skills in prepareRun, dynamically register each skill slug as a slash command that runs the skill body as an agent instruction. Slugs colliding with built-in commands warn and defer to the built-in.
items.done: `/hill-climb` (and any other installed skill slug) invokes the skill inline; unknown-command handler is not hit. Built-in command names take precedence with a clear warning on collision.

items.title: Auto-spec + fan-out a roadmap card into child tasks
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. roadmap/decompose.ts: buildProposal (splits done-criteria bullets → child cards), childId, formatProposal, findCard, applyProposal. vanta roadmap decompose <id> [--apply] — preview-first (no --apply = dry run), writes children on --apply. 5 tests; 1671 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: `vanta roadmap decompose <id>` proposes ordered child cards with dependencies from a parent card's done-criteria, writes them to roadmap.json on approval, and regenerates the board.

items.title: Auto-handoff on context pressure + reload on restart
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-07. Built on HANDOFF-PACKET: extracted assembleHandoff (shared by /handoff + auto). repl/auto-handoff.ts — shouldAutoHandoff (fill ≥ VANTA_AUTOHANDOFF_THRESHOLD, default 0.75) + maybeAutoHandoff writes a durable resume block to.vanta/handoff.md when context crosses the threshold (est = real last-prompt inputTokens, else chars/4). Both hosts call it post-turn and note once. RELOAD: prepareRun, gated to interactive launches (instruction === 'interactive session', so one-shot/factory/desktop are unaffected), injects a recent (≤7d) resume block into the system prompt and CONSUMES it (clearAutoHandoff) so it resumes once, not forever. Opt-out VANTA_AUTOHANDOFF=0. 11 unit tests (threshold, write/read/clear roundtrip incl. staleness, maybeAutoHandoff); full suite 1199 green; tsc clean.
items.done: When context crosses a threshold Vanta auto-writes a durable resume block and reloads it on next launch; Jason never has to type 'give me a handoff' or re-explain state.

Three tools: ref_ingest (fetch+extract+store), ref_search, ref_list. vanta ref [add|search|list] CLI. 9 ref tests; 1680 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: `vanta ref add <src>` ingests a URL/repo/screenshot/transcript/file into durable project context; later sessions recall it via @-context and recall without the user re-pasting.

items.title: Long-run resilience — resume an unattended run after a mid-flight drop
items.summary: Jason's #1 reliability pain on the autonomous path (Codex mining): he demands 'don't stop until they're all done / don't ask permission' (34+ delegations) but long runs die mid-flight — 'u keep bugging out › continue ■ Error: stream disconnected before completion'. He also hand-babysits long data pipelines (~20 near-identical 'check progress, if 600+ done queue X, if finished commit/push' messages) — manually playing the supervisor loop the agent should own. Vanta has checkpoints; this is auto-resume: a run that crashes/disconnects resumes from its last checkpoint without a manual 'continue', with progress + stall surfaced. Builds on checkpoints + STALL-UNBLOCK + the autonomy ladder.
items.done: An unattended run that dies mid-flight (crash/stream drop) auto-resumes from its last checkpoint and finishes the backlog without the user typing 'continue'; progress and any true stall are surfaced.

items.title: Pay down the 85 size-gate violations CODE-SIZE-GATE surfaced
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Fixed 15+ violations: providers/index.ts (resolveProvider cx24→cx5 via PROVIDERS map), agent.ts (runTurn refactored with TurnState + processToolCalls), memory/relevance.ts (cx13→cx3 pattern table), memory/curator.ts (56→split), sessions/search.ts (cx14→cx5), factory/compartments.ts (isSkeleton extract), projects/radar.ts (classifyStack 58→22), repl/self-eval.ts (cx15→cx4), repl/brief-cmd.ts (buildBrief split), repl/repro-cmd.ts (54→32), repl/dashboard-cmd.ts (split). 66→51 violations. Remaining: session.ts, factory/run.ts, interactive.ts, tui/*.tsx (larger refactors).
items.done: vanta lint reports 0 violations on src/ (tests excluded); VANTA_LINT_BLOCK=1 can be enabled without blocking normal commits.

items.title: Accept-edits mode + full approval cycle
items.summary: Vanta's shift+tab cycles only review↔auto. Claude Code cycles default → accept-edits → plan → auto. Add an accept-edits mode (auto-approve file writes/edits + safe FS commands like mkdir/mv, still kernel-gate other shell + destructive ops) and expand the cycle.
items.done: Shift+Tab cycles review → accept-edits → plan → auto; in accept-edits, write_file/edit run without prompts while shell/destructive stay kernel-gated; the active mode shows in the status line.

items.title: Enforced read-only plan mode
items.summary: Today /planmode injects a PLAN_MARKER into the prompt (advisory). Claude Code's plan mode restricts the agent to read-only tools until the plan is approved. Make plan mode a real capability gate so the plan-before-act guarantee is enforced, not requested.
items.done: In plan mode the agent can only call read tools; any write/shell tool returns a 'blocked: plan mode' result until the user approves the plan, after which normal kernel gating resumes.

items.title: Checkpoint + rewind code & conversation
items.summary: Claude Code snapshots files before each edit and rewinds code and/or conversation to a prior point (Esc-Esc / /rewind). Vanta has /undo (drops the last turn) but no code restore. Add per-edit checkpoints and a rewind that restores both.
items.done: /rewind lists recent checkpoints; selecting one restores the file(s) to that snapshot and optionally truncates the conversation; a checkpoint is written before every write_file.

items.title: Interactive diff viewer (/diff)
items.summary: Vanta renders per-edit diffs (TUI-DIFF). Claude Code's /diff is an interactive viewer over all uncommitted changes and per-turn diffs. Add a /diff command that opens a scrollable view with arrow-keys to switch between the git working-tree diff and this turn's edits.
items.done: /diff opens a viewer showing uncommitted changes and the current turn's edits; arrow keys switch views; q closes.

items.title: Reverse prompt search (Ctrl+R)
items.summary: Claude Code's Ctrl+R reverse-searches past prompts. Vanta's composer has up/down history but no search. Add incremental reverse search over the session's (and optionally the project's) prompt history.
items.done: Ctrl+R opens an incremental search over prior prompts; typing filters live; Enter inserts the match into the composer; Esc cancels.

items.title: Side question without history (/btw)
items.summary: Claude Code's /btw answers a quick side question without adding it to conversation history (no tool access, reuses the prompt cache). Lets you ask Vanta something mid-task without polluting context. Add /btw <question>.
items.done: /btw <question> returns an answer that is NOT appended to the conversation transcript and does not affect the next turn's context.

items.title: In-session permission rules (allow/ask/deny)
items.summary: Claude Code's /permissions manages allow/ask/deny rule lists. Vanta's kernel decides per-action but the user can't tune persistent rules in-session. Add user-editable allow/ask/deny lists (tool + arg patterns), persisted per project, that feed the kernel verdict. Rule zero holds: allow-rules may only downgrade Ask→Allow within kernel-permitted bounds, never override a Block.
items.done: /permissions shows current allow/ask/deny rules; the user can add a rule (e.g. allow read_file, deny 'shell rm *'); rules persist and the kernel honors them on later actions; a Block stays a Block regardless of an allow rule.

items.title: OS sandbox for tool execution
items.summary: Claude Code's /sandbox runs tools under OS isolation (Linux bwrap). Add an opt-in sandbox for shell_cmd/run_code using macOS Seatbelt (sandbox-exec) and Linux bwrap, restricting filesystem + network to the approved scope. Reinforces the kernel thesis with OS-level enforcement.
items.done: With sandbox enabled, shell_cmd/run_code execute inside a Seatbelt/bwrap profile scoped to the project root + writable zones; an out-of-scope file or network access fails at the OS layer, verified by a test.

items.title: User-configurable lifecycle hooks
items.summary: Vanta only CONSUMES Claude Code's hooks (vanta hooks writes brain notes on Stop/PreCompact) — it has no hooks on its own loop. Claude Code lets users wire hooks on lifecycle events (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/SessionStart/Stop/PreCompact...) with command/http/mcp_tool/prompt/agent handlers + matchers. Add a settings-driven hook system on Vanta's own agent loop. Builds toward PLUGIN-HOOKS (same surface, exposed to plugins).
items.done: A settings entry maps an event + tool-name matcher to a handler; a configured PreToolUse command hook runs before the matched tool and can block it; covered by a test.

items.title: Background shell tasks
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. tools/bg-tasks.ts: spawnBackground (detached spawn →.vanta/bg-tasks/<id>.{log,json}), readBgTask, listBgTasks, readBgLog. Two new tools: bg_list (filter by status) + bg_status (with optional log tail). shell_cmd gains background: bool param. 1665 tests green; tsc clean.
items.done: shell_cmd accepts a background flag; a backgrounded command returns a task id immediately, keeps running, writes output to a file, and /tasks (or Ctrl+T) lists running/finished tasks with status.

items.title: Layered settings.json
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. settings/store.ts: SettingsSchema (allowedTools/blockedTools/env/gates/ui), loadSettings (user ~/.vanta/settings.json → project./.vanta/settings.json → local./.vanta/settings.local.json deep-merge), applySettingsEnv, isToolAllowed/Blocked. Wired into prepareRun (session.ts). vanta settings [show|paths] CLI. 13 tests; 1665 total green; tsc clean.
items.done: Vanta reads + merges settings.json from user/project/local scopes at startup (local > project > user), zod-validated; a project setting overrides a user setting, verified by a test.

items.title: Multi-directory roots (/add-dir)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. tools/writable-zones.ts: addSessionDir + getSessionDirs via VANTA_EXTRA_DIRS runtime env var; resolveWritableZones + resolveReadableZones now append extra dirs. /add-dir slash command (repl/add-dir-cmd.ts) validates path + updates session scope. Kernel still gates each individual access. tsc clean; 1665 tests.

items.title: Print/headless mode + output formats

items.title: Native context compression (Headroom concept, zero-dep)
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-08. Rewrote Headroom's idea in pure TS instead of bolting on its Python proxy (keeps local-trusted-operator thesis: no external service/API key/egress). src/compress/: pure content router (json|logs|text|binary) → compressors. binary detection elides base64/blobs to a pointer (top real-data win: 20k-token screenshot → 12 tokens); json-crush keeps head+tail of object arrays + truncates strings; log-squash collapses blank-line runs/dupes/deep stacks. CCR store stashes original under content hash; retrieve_original tool expands on demand. Wired into agent.ts dispatchTool: compress each tool output ONCE before history (never system prefix, never re-compress). VANTA_COMPRESS (default on), best-effort. Proven on REAL session payloads = 22% overall (dominated by binary elision). 28 tests; tsc+lint clean.
items.done: Fat tool outputs (JSON/logs/binary) compress 50%+ before entering history, reversibly via retrieve_original, behind VANTA_COMPRESS — pure TS, zero new deps.

items.title: AST code compressor (keep signatures, drop bodies)
items.summary: Follow-up to COMPRESS-NATIVE. Headroom's CodeCompressor, native: for.ts/.tsx tool outputs (file reads), use the TS compiler API (already in lint/size.ts + lsp/ts-service.ts) to keep imports/signatures/types and elide function bodies → big shrink on large file reads while the agent keeps the shape. Reversible via the existing CCR store. Only fires on detected code; text stays untouched.
items.done: Large.ts/.tsx tool outputs compress by eliding function bodies while preserving imports + signatures; reversible via retrieve_original.

items.title: Surface compression savings in the cost footer
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. Compression savings now surface in cost footer. Per-turn: "1500→1140 tokens (360 saved via compression)". Session: cumulative tokens saved. Threading via applyCompression() → AgentOutcome → formatTurnCost(). 1842 tests green, TypeScript clean.
items.done: The per-turn + session footer shows tokens saved by native compression alongside cost.

items.title: Brief tool — proactive user notifications with attachments
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. tools/brief.ts: brief tool with message (required), status=normal|proactive (default normal), optional files array. Returns formatted [STATUS] message with attachments list. Kernel Allow. 5 tests; tsc clean.
items.done: `brief <message> [--status proactive] [--files path1 path2]` sends a structured notification.

items.title: MCP OAuth auth tool — authenticate MCP servers inline
items.summary: CC has McpAuthTool: when an MCP server requires OAuth, a pseudo-tool surfaces in place of its real tools so the agent can kick off the auth flow on the user's behalf (returns an auth URL). Vanta's MCP client has no auth handling — unauthenticated servers just fail silently. Add MCP OAuth flow: detect 401 from MCP server, surface mcp_auth tool with auth URL, complete flow and reconnect.
items.done: When an MCP server needs OAuth, `mcp_auth` surfaces an auth URL; after the user completes auth the server reconnects and its tools become available.

items.title: Remote trigger tool — list/run cloud-side triggers
items.summary: CC's RemoteTriggerTool lets the agent list, get, create, update, and run triggers registered in Anthropic's cloud (authenticated via Claude.ai OAuth). Used for remote agent scheduling and event-driven tasks. Vanta has local cron and gateway webhooks but no cloud trigger registry. Requires Anthropic OAuth. Add remote_trigger tool backed by a Vanta cloud endpoint or adapt to a self-hosted trigger registry.
items.done: `remote_trigger list` shows registered triggers; `remote_trigger run <id>` fires one; triggers persist beyond local sessions.

items.title: PowerShell tool — Windows shell execution
items.summary: CC has PowerShellTool for Windows environments. Vanta's shell_cmd uses /bin/sh (macOS/Linux). For Windows support: detect platform, swap the shell backend to PowerShell when on Windows, surface a powershell tool with the same safety gating as shell_cmd. Kernel already OS-agnostic; main work is TS shell spawning + test coverage on Windows.
items.done: `powershell` tool executes commands on Windows via PowerShell with the same kernel safety gating as shell_cmd. Vanta runs on Windows.

items.title: Monitor tool — reactive line-by-line command monitoring
items.summary: CC has a Monitor tool that runs a shell command in the background and fires a callback on each output line, enabling event-driven loops (watch a build, react to log lines, stream deploy output). Vanta runs BG tasks but has no line-by-line reactive tool.
items.done: A `monitor` tool runs a command and routes each output line as a tool result; the agent can act on each line before the process finishes.

items.title: WaitForMcpServers tool — block until MCP connections are ready
items.summary: CC exposes WaitForMcpServers so an agent can block at session start until all pending MCP server connections resolve, instead of racing or silently missing tools. Vanta currently has no readiness barrier for MCP mounts.
items.done: `wait_for_mcp_servers` blocks until all configured MCP servers are connected (or timeout), then returns a per-server status map.

items.title: ShareOnboardingGuide tool + /team-onboarding command
items.summary: CC can generate and upload a structured onboarding guide for a codebase and return a shareable URL (ShareOnboardingGuide tool, /team-onboarding slash command). Useful for handing off context to teammates or new contributors without copy-pasting CLAUDE.md.
items.done: `share_onboarding_guide` generates an ONBOARDING.md from the project context and returns a shareable link; `/team-onboarding` triggers it from the REPL.

items.title: Effort levels — --effort flag, effortLevel setting, extended thinking controls
items.summary: CC supports --effort low/medium/high/xhigh/max to tune the trade-off between speed, cost, and reasoning depth. Paired with `effortLevel` (persist across sessions), `alwaysThinkingEnabled`, and `showThinkingSummaries`. Vanta has no equivalent effort-level system.
items.done: `--effort <level>` and `VANTA_EFFORT` env var are wired; `effortLevel` in settings persists the default; `alwaysThinkingEnabled` enables extended thinking by default; `showThinkingSummaries` shows abbreviated reasoning in the TUI.

items.title: Background agent management — agents view, attach, logs, respawn, daemon
items.summary: CC has a full parallel-session management CLI: `claude agents` (dispatch/monitor view), `claude attach/logs/respawn/stop/rm <id>` for individual sessions, `claude daemon status/stop` for the supervisor. Vanta can spawn subagents but has no CLI for managing running background sessions.
items.done: `vanta agents` opens a live view of running sessions; `vanta attach/logs/respawn/stop/rm <id>` manage individual sessions; `vanta daemon status/stop` controls the supervisor; a `disableAgentView` setting gates the feature.

items.title: Auto permission mode — ML action classification + soft-deny preset
items.summary: CC's `--permission-mode auto` uses an ML classifier to auto-approve low-risk actions and soft-deny borderline ones without user prompts. Paired with `autoMode` settings (rules, soft_deny) and `vanta auto-mode defaults/config` CLI. Vanta's kernel uses keyword rules; it has no learned-classification layer.
items.done: `--permission-mode auto` (or `VANTA_AUTO_MODE=1`) applies ML-based classification on top of kernel rules; `vanta auto-mode defaults` prints built-in rules; `vanta auto-mode config` shows effective config with overrides; `autoMode` settings accept custom rules and a soft_deny preset.

items.title: vanta setup-token — long-lived OAuth token for CI/automation
items.summary: CC's `claude setup-token` generates a persistent OAuth token suitable for use in CI pipelines and non-interactive environments. Vanta uses API keys; there's no token generation flow for automated/CI use.
items.done: `vanta setup-token` authenticates interactively and writes a long-lived token to `~/.vanta/token.json` (0600); the token is recognized by `VANTA_AUTH_TOKEN` and used instead of API keys in non-interactive runs.

items.title: --max-budget-usd — per-session spend cap
items.summary: CC's `--max-budget-usd` stops the agent loop when the session's accumulated LLM spend exceeds the threshold. Vanta tracks cost (COST-VISIBLE) but has no hard spend cap that halts execution.
items.done: `--max-budget-usd <n>` (and `VANTA_MAX_BUDGET_USD` env) stops the agent with a cost-exceeded message when spend reaches the limit; the current spend is shown in the prompt before stopping.

items.title: --json-schema — structured output + JSON Schema validation
items.summary: CC's `--json-schema <file|inline>` forces the model to produce a response that validates against the provided JSON Schema, with retries on validation failure. Enables reliable structured extraction in automation pipelines. Vanta has no schema-constrained output.
items.done: `--json-schema <file>` (or `VANTA_JSON_SCHEMA`) forces JSON output and validates each response; on validation failure the agent retries with the error; the final result is printed as validated JSON to stdout in print mode.

items.title: Tmux integration for worktrees — --tmux flag
items.summary: CC's `--tmux` creates a tmux session (or window) for each worktree-isolated agent, enabling side-by-side parallel agents in a terminal multiplexer. `--tmux=classic` uses the classic tmux layout. Useful for heavy parallelization workflows.
items.done: `--tmux` spawns a new tmux window for each worktree agent; `--tmux=classic` uses the classic pane layout; `--teammate-mode tmux` routes agent teams through tmux.

items.title: --fork-session — create a new session ID on resume
items.summary: CC's `--fork-session` creates a fresh session ID when resuming a prior session, keeping the original intact for later comparison or fallback. Vanta's resume overwrites the active session.
items.done: `--fork-session` combined with `--resume` creates a new session file seeded with the prior history; `vanta sessions list` shows both the original and the fork.

items.title: vanta project purge — delete local project state
items.summary: CC's `claude project purge [path]` deletes all local project state (sessions, auto-memory, checkpoints) for a given project, with `--dry-run`, `-y`, `-i` (interactive), and `--all` flags. Vanta has no cleanup command; stale state accumulates.
items.done: `vanta project purge [path]` lists and deletes project-scoped state (sessions + memory + .vanta/ data) with dry-run by default; `--yes` skips confirmation; `--all` purges all known projects.

items.title: --safe-mode + --bare — troubleshooting isolation modes
items.summary: CC's `--safe-mode` disables all customizations (plugins, hooks, skills, CLAUDE.md) for clean troubleshooting; `--bare` skips auto-discovery (no project CLAUDE.md, no MCP, no skills). Essential for debugging broken configurations.
items.done: `--safe-mode` runs without any user customizations (hooks/skills/plugins/rules are skipped); `--bare` skips auto-discovery; both print a banner confirming the isolation level.

items.title: --init / --init-only / --maintenance — lifecycle hook flags
items.summary: CC's `--init` runs Setup hooks before the session; `--init-only` runs Setup + SessionStart then exits (for one-time environment prep); `--maintenance` runs Setup with a maintenance matcher (for periodic housekeeping crons). Vanta has no equivalent lifecycle bootstrap flags.
items.done: `--init` triggers Setup hooks before the first turn; `--init-only` runs Setup + SessionStart then exits 0; `--maintenance` adds a `maintenance: true` matcher context to Setup hooks.

items.title: --exec — run a shell command as a background job alongside the session
items.summary: CC's `--exec <cmd>` starts a shell command as a tracked background job concurrent with the agent session (e.g., a dev server, a test watcher). The agent can monitor its output. Vanta has no way to co-launch a background process with a session.
items.done: `--exec <cmd>` starts the command before the first turn; its output is available to the Monitor tool; the process is tracked and killed on session end.

items.title: --exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections — prompt cache optimization
items.summary: CC's `--exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections` strips volatile sections (goals, time, current memory) from the cache-prefix portion of the system prompt, dramatically improving LLM cache hit rate on long sessions. Vanta's prompt has stable + volatile tiers (PROMPT-STABILITY) but no per-section exclusion flag.
items.done: `--exclude-dynamic-system-prompt-sections <list>` (or comma-sep env `VANTA_EXCLUDE_PROMPT_SECTIONS`) moves specified tiers out of the stable cache prefix; the cost footer shows estimated cache-hit improvement.

items.title: Hook type parity — http, mcp_tool, prompt, agent hook types
items.summary: CC has 5 hook types: command (shell), http (POST to remote endpoint), mcp_tool (call a tool on an MCP server), prompt (send a prompt to Claude for evaluation), and agent (spawn a subagent with tools). Vanta's CC-HOOKS-ENGINE covers command hooks; the other 4 types are unimplemented.
items.done: All 5 CC hook types are implemented: command (exists), http (POST with headers + allowedEnvVars), mcp_tool (call a named MCP tool), prompt (evaluate with a model), and agent (spawn a tool-enabled subagent); all respect the timeout + once + statusMessage fields.

items.title: Extended hook event coverage — all 30 CC hook events
items.summary: CC defines ~30 named hook events. Vanta's hooks cover the core 8 (SessionStart/End, PreToolUse, PostToolUse, Stop, UserPromptSubmit, PreCompact, PostCompact). Missing: TeammateIdle, TaskCreated/Completed, CwdChanged, FileChanged, ConfigChange, InstructionsLoaded, WorktreeCreate/Remove, Elicitation/ElicitationResult, MessageDisplay, UserPromptExpansion, StopFailure, PostToolBatch, SubagentStart/Stop.
items.done: All 30 CC hook events are implemented and documented; existing event hooks are unchanged; each new event fires at the correct point in the agent/session lifecycle.

items.title: Hook matcher depth — all conditional hook matchers
items.summary: CC hook rules support rich conditional matchers: session start method (startup/resume/clear/compact), CLI trigger (init/maintenance), session end reason, notification type, agent type, compaction trigger, config source, literal filenames, error type (rate_limit/overloaded/auth), load reason, skill/command names, MCP server names. Vanta hooks support basic tool-name matching only.
items.done: All CC matcher categories are implemented for the Vanta hooks engine; the settings schema accepts any combination; docs show a worked example for each matcher type.

items.title: Display/UX settings parity — spinner tips, turn duration, away recap, etc.
items.summary: CC settings include: spinnerTipsEnabled, spinnerTipsOverride (custom tips), spinnerVerbs (append/replace verbs), showTurnDuration (show elapsed ms), awaySummaryEnabled (show recap on return), syntaxHighlightingDisabled, prefersReducedMotion, autoScrollEnabled, preferredNotifChannel. Vanta has themes and accessibility settings (TUI-THEME) but not this full UX settings layer.
items.done: All listed UX settings are recognized in `~/.vanta/settings.json`; their default behaviors match CC semantics; `vanta config` surfaces them in the settings UI.

items.title: Memory settings parity — autoMemory, excludes, plans dir
items.summary: CC settings: autoMemoryEnabled (toggle auto memory), autoMemoryDirectory (custom path), claudeMdExcludes (skip specific CLAUDE.md files by glob), plansDirectory (custom plans storage), fileSuggestion (custom file autocomplete source). Vanta's memory is always on with no per-instance controls.
items.done: autoMemoryEnabled, autoMemoryDirectory, claudeMdExcludes (glob array), plansDirectory, and fileSuggestion are all recognized in settings; they take effect at session start without a restart.

items.title: Git settings parity — attribution, PR template, gitignore
items.summary: CC settings: attribution (customize Co-Authored-By in commits), includeGitInstructions (include git best-practice instructions in system prompt), prUrlTemplate (PR link format for the status line), respectGitignore (control file picker gitignore behavior). Vanta commits but has no commit attribution settings.
items.done: All four git settings are recognized and enforced; `attribution` overrides the default Co-Authored-By line; `prUrlTemplate` substitutes `{PR}` with the PR number in the status footer.

items.title: MCP allowlist/denylist settings — per-session server access control
items.summary: CC settings: allowedMcpServers (allowlist by name/glob), deniedMcpServers (denylist), enableAllProjectMcpServers (auto-approve project-level .mcp.json servers), enabledMcpjsonServers / disabledMcpjsonServers (per-server overrides). Vanta mounts all configured servers without granular per-session control.
items.done: allowedMcpServers and deniedMcpServers gate which servers are mounted at session start; enableAllProjectMcpServers auto-approves .mcp.json servers; the per-server overrides take effect without prompts.

items.title: Skill context budget + management settings parity
items.summary: CC settings: skillListingBudgetFraction (max context fraction for skill index, default 0.01), maxSkillDescriptionChars (per-skill description limit), disableBundledSkills (turn off shipped skills), disableSkillShellExecution (block shell exec in skills), skillOverrides (per-skill visibility), disableWorkflows (disable dynamic workflows). Vanta clips skill descriptions (PROMPT-STABILITY) but lacks the full settings layer.
items.done: All listed skill settings are in the schema and enforced at skill-index build time; disableWorkflows gates the Workflow tool; skillOverrides accepts a name→mode map.

items.title: Cloud credential refresh scripts — AWS, GCP, API key helper, OTel
items.summary: CC settings: apiKeyHelper (shell script that generates API keys dynamically), awsAuthRefresh + awsCredentialExport (AWS STS/SSO rotation scripts), gcpAuthRefresh (GCP ADC refresh), otelHeadersHelper (OpenTelemetry authorization headers). Enables Vanta in cloud CI/CD with short-lived credentials. No equivalent in Vanta today.
items.done: apiKeyHelper, awsAuthRefresh, awsCredentialExport, gcpAuthRefresh, and otelHeadersHelper are all recognized settings; Vanta runs the appropriate script before each LLM call and injects the result; error output is surfaced actionably.

items.title: SSH pre-configured connections in settings
items.summary: CC's sshConfigs setting pre-configures named SSH connections (host, port, user, key, jump host) so the agent can SSH into remote systems without user config. Useful for remote dev environments, cloud instances, and cross-machine agent tasks.
items.done: sshConfigs accepts an array of named connection profiles; the shell_cmd / bash tools use them by name; `vanta ssh <name>` opens an interactive shell to the configured host.

items.title: Path-scoped rules system — .vanta/rules/ with YAML frontmatter + globs
items.summary: CC supports `.claude/rules/<name>.md` files with YAML frontmatter specifying glob patterns that scope the rule to matching files (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `*.md`). Rules load only when the agent touches matching files. User-level rules at `~/.claude/rules/`. Nested dirs and symlinks supported. Vanta has global VANTA.md rules but no path-scoped rule files.
items.done: `.vanta/rules/<name>.md` files with `globs:` frontmatter are parsed; matching rules are injected into the system prompt only when relevant files are in context; `~/.vanta/rules/` provides user-level rules; symlinks work.

items.title: CLAUDE.md / VANTA.md @-import syntax — include external files by reference
items.summary: CC supports `@path/to/file` inside CLAUDE.md to inline another file's contents at load time, with up to 4-hop recursion. Enables shared rule libraries, team-wide standards, and composable instruction sets. Vanta loads VANTA.md verbatim; no import syntax.
items.done: `@<path>` inside VANTA.md (or rules files) inlines the referenced file at session start; absolute and relative paths work; recursion is capped at 4 hops; circular imports are detected and skipped.
