items.title: --from-pr — resume sessions linked to a specific pull request
items.summary: CC's `--from-pr <url|number>` resumes all sessions previously associated with a given PR, enabling continuity across review cycles. Vanta's session resume is by session ID only; no PR linking.
items.done: `--from-pr <url|number>` loads the session(s) previously tagged with that PR; `vanta sessions list --pr <n>` shows PR-linked sessions; sessions auto-tag when `vanta pr-comments` runs.

items.title: Away summary — recap on return to an idle session
items.summary: CC's `awaySummaryEnabled` setting shows a brief recap of what happened when the user returns to a session that ran while they were away (background agents, long runs). Vanta has no idle-return awareness.
items.done: When the user returns to an interactive session after >2 min of inactivity and Vanta ran tool calls in the interim, a 2-3 sentence recap is shown before the next prompt; `awaySummaryEnabled: false` disables it.

items.title: Auto-updates channel + version enforcement settings
items.summary: CC settings: autoUpdatesChannel (stable/latest release channel), minimumVersion (prevent downgrade below version). `vanta update` already exists (SELF-UPDATE); what's missing is the channel and minimum-version enforcement in settings.
items.done: `autoUpdatesChannel` in settings selects stable/latest; `minimumVersion` refuses to run on older versions with an actionable upgrade message; `vanta update --channel <stable|latest>` switches channel.

items.title: Session cleanup period — cleanupPeriodDays setting
items.summary: CC's `cleanupPeriodDays` (default 30) auto-expires sessions older than N days on startup. Vanta sessions accumulate indefinitely; there's no TTL or auto-purge.
items.done: `cleanupPeriodDays` in settings (default 30) prunes sessions older than N days on session start; `vanta sessions prune --older-than <days>` runs it manually; a summary of pruned sessions is logged.

items.title: --teammate-mode — agent team UI display mode
items.summary: CC's `--teammate-mode auto/in-process/tmux` controls how agent team members render in the UI: in-process (inline in the same terminal), tmux (one pane per teammate), or auto (pick by environment). Complements CC-TEAMS for the UI layer.
items.done: `--teammate-mode <mode>` controls agent team rendering; `in-process` shows teammates inline; `tmux` opens a pane per agent; `auto` picks based on TERM_PROGRAM/tmux availability.

items.title: --prompt-suggestions — emit predicted next prompts for IDE/shell integration
items.summary: CC's `--prompt-suggestions` emits a predicted list of likely next prompts after each response, enabling IDE extensions and shell completions to offer context-aware suggestions. Enables tight editor integration.
items.done: `--prompt-suggestions` includes a `suggested_prompts: string[]` field in each streamed response; the TUI ignores it; IDE extensions can surface these as quick-actions.

items.title: skipWebFetchPreflight — bypass web fetch domain safety check
items.summary: CC's `skipWebFetchPreflight` setting skips the domain safety preflight check before web_fetch calls (useful in air-gapped or fully-trusted environments). Vanta's web_fetch has an allowed-domain gate; no setting to disable the preflight.
items.done: `skipWebFetchPreflight: true` in settings bypasses the domain approval check in web_fetch; a warning is printed at session start when enabled; default is false.

items.title: --plugin-url / --plugin-dir — load plugins from URL or local path
items.summary: CC's `--plugin-url <url>` fetches a plugin .zip from a URL and loads it; `--plugin-dir <path>` loads from a local directory or .zip. Vanta's plugin system (PLUGIN-SYSTEM shipped) installs from git; there's no direct URL or local-path load path.
items.done: `--plugin-url <url>` downloads, verifies, and loads a plugin .zip at session start; `--plugin-dir <path>` loads a local dir/.zip; both trigger the same plugin activation path as `vanta plugin install`.

items.title: /tasks slash command — list and manage background tasks from the REPL
items.summary: CC's `/tasks` slash command lists all running background tasks with status, attach/stop controls. Vanta can run background tasks but has no slash command to inspect or manage them mid-session.
items.done: `/tasks` lists all background tasks with their status, output tail, and attach/stop/logs shortcut; keyboard shortcuts follow CC conventions.

items.title: /describe slash command — generate file and directory descriptions
items.summary: CC's `/describe` generates structured descriptions for files and directories, useful for auto-populating CLAUDE.md, onboarding docs, and code search indexes. No equivalent in Vanta.
items.done: `/describe [path]` generates a concise description of the file or directory and optionally appends it to VANTA.md or a designated descriptions file.

items.title: /stop slash command — gracefully stop the current agent turn
items.summary: CC's `/stop` command gracefully halts the current turn mid-execution (as opposed to Esc which aborts immediately). Vanta has Esc-abort; `/stop` would issue a soft stop that lets the current tool call finish before halting.
items.done: `/stop` sends a stop signal; the agent finishes the current tool call, then exits the turn cleanly and presents a summary of what was completed.

items.title: /feedback slash command — report issues or suggestions from within the session
items.summary: CC's `/feedback` captures the current session context and opens a report (GitHub issue or form). Vanta has `/bug` for crashes; `/feedback` is for suggestions, UX reports, and non-crash issues.
items.done: `/feedback [message]` captures the last N turns and opens a pre-filled GitHub issue or writes to a local `.vanta/feedback/` file; distinct from `/bug` which is for crashes.

items.title: /reload-plugins — activate plugins installed during the current session
items.summary: CC's `/reload-plugins` activates any plugins installed since session start without requiring a restart. If you `vanta plugin install` during a session, the new plugin isn't active until `/reload-plugins` or restart. Vanta has no in-session plugin reload.
items.done: `/reload-plugins` re-scans the plugin directory, loads any newly installed plugins, and prints a diff of what was added; existing plugins are not re-initialized.

items.title: Managed policy scope — org-wide CLAUDE.md and dynamic settings
items.summary: CC supports an org-wide managed policy layer: a CLAUDE.md at /Library/Application Support/ClaudeCode/ (macOS), dynamic settings via policyHelper script, forceLoginMethod/forceLoginOrgUUID, requiredMinimumVersion, allowManagedHooksOnly, companyAnnouncements, parentSettingsBehavior (first-wins/merge). Enables enterprise fleet control of Vanta installations.
items.done: A managed policy file at the OS-level config dir is loaded before user settings; policyHelper runs to compute dynamic rules; forceLoginMethod/Org block non-compliant auth; requiredMinimumVersion/Max enforce version bounds; companyAnnouncements show on startup.

items.title: Session teleport + remote — cross-device session continuation flags
items.summary: CC's `--teleport` resumes a cloud session from claude.ai in the local terminal; `--remote <task>` creates a new session on claude.ai from the terminal; `/teleport` and `/desktop` are the slash-command equivalents. Enables fluid handoff between web and terminal. CC-H-REMOTE-CONTROL tracks the horizon feature; this tracks the specific flags/commands.
items.done: `--teleport` resumes the most recent web session locally; `--remote <task>` creates a new cloud session and streams output locally; `/teleport` and `/desktop` are REPL equivalents.

items.title: Virtual message list — scrolling, in-transcript search, jump navigation
items.summary: CC's VirtualMessageList (1081 lines) renders only visible messages, supports Ctrl+R search-within-transcript, jump-to-match navigation, and sticky prompt headers when scrolling up through history. Vanta renders all messages eagerly — long sessions slow down and have no search.
items.done: Long conversations render only visible rows; Ctrl+R searches within the transcript with match highlighting and arrow-key navigation; sticky prompts show context when scrolling up.
items.notes: VirtualTranscript renders viewport slice (maxVisible=termRows-12) in alt-screen mode. scrollBy/scrollReset actions + pgup/pgdn keys. Static kept for non-alt-screen. All three resolved by one implementation.

items.title: Rich status line parity — rate limits, lines diff, session name, worktree
items.summary: CC's StatusLine shows: 5-hour and 7-day rate limit utilization % + resets_at timestamps, lines added/removed in the session, session name, output style name, worktree context, vim mode state, and hooks into the hook system for custom fields. Vanta's status line (CC-S-STATUSLINE shipped) shows model + mode; missing the rest.
items.done: Status line includes rate-limit utilization bars, lines-added/removed delta, session name, worktree indicator, and a hook call point for custom status segments.

items.title: Teammate spinner tree — parallel agent visualization
items.summary: CC's TeammateSpinnerTree renders a live tree of all running agents: leader verb + token count, each teammate's name + status, selection mode for switching focus, idle text when an agent finishes. Vanta's spinner shows one agent; parallel agents are invisible.
items.done: While multiple agents run, the spinner area shows a tree: leader line (verb + tokens), one line per teammate (name + current action); Shift+arrow cycles focus between agents.

items.title: Stalled spinner animation — visual state change when agent is stuck
items.summary: CC's spinner switches to a 'stalled' animation after a configurable idle threshold — visually distinct from the normal working state. Helps users notice when an agent hasn't progressed. Vanta's spinner runs the same animation regardless of progress.
items.done: After N seconds without a tool call or token output, the spinner switches to the stalled variant; once output resumes it returns to the normal animation.

items.title: Glimmer / shimmer text animation during processing
items.summary: CC's GlimmerMessage + ShimmerChar render animated shimmer/glimmer effects on status text while the model is generating — gives visual feedback that the agent is alive. Vanta uses a static spinner; no animated text shimmer.
items.done: While the model is streaming, status text shimmers character-by-character; `prefersReducedMotion` disables it; the animation is driven by use-animation-frame.

items.title: Compact boundary message — visual separator after context compaction
items.summary: CC renders a distinct '✻ Conversation compacted (Ctrl+O for history)' message at the compaction point, with a dynamic shortcut hint. Vanta compacts context (CC-S-COMPACT shipped) but shows no in-transcript marker where compaction occurred.
items.done: After compaction, a separator line appears in the transcript at the compaction boundary with the shortcut to view full history; it uses the active keybinding, not a hardcoded key.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. New transcript Entry kind "compactBoundary" (magenta ──── ✻ … ──── separator) + reducer action { t: "compactBoundary"; text }. app.tsx onAutoCompact now dispatches the boundary (was a plain note) with "compacted N messages · <summary preview>", so the transcript shows a distinct visual marker where the model's context was summarized. +2 tests (reducer + EntryRow render); full TUI suite 226 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Rich rate-limit message — upsell, /upgrade, /extra-usage, tier-aware
items.summary: CC's RateLimitMessage is context-aware: shows different actions based on subscription tier (free → /upgrade, Max20x → /extra-usage, team/enterprise → different copy), displays when limits reset, and optionally auto-opens an options menu. Vanta shows a plain error string.
items.done: When rate-limited, a structured message shows the applicable action (/upgrade or /extra-usage), the reset time, and subscription-appropriate copy; team/enterprise users see a different path.

items.title: Plan approval message — rendered plan + approve/reject in transcript
items.summary: CC renders plan-mode plans inline in the transcript with a dashed box border + plan file path reference + approve/reject controls. The approval response also appears as a message. Vanta's plan mode (CC-PLAN-MODE-REAL next) needs this visual layer to be complete.
items.done: Plan-mode plans render in a boxed 'planMode' border with markdown content; approve/reject updates the transcript with a response message; plan file path shows dimmed.

items.title: Grouped parallel tool calls — batch visual rendering
items.summary: CC groups parallel tool calls into a single GroupedToolUseContent block, delegating rendering to each tool's renderGroupedToolUse method. Each tool can show progress/error/resolved state per-call within the group. Vanta shows parallel calls as sequential individual lines.
items.done: Parallel tool calls from the same batch are rendered as a group; each tool can provide a renderGroupedToolUse implementation; in-progress / resolved / error states render per-call within the group.
items.notes: isGrouped on ToolEntry (set in reducer); EntryRow renders │ connector for grouped tools. True collapse-to-one-row deferred to CC-VIRTUAL-LIST.

items.title: Hook progress message — live hook execution in transcript
items.summary: CC's HookProgressMessage shows hook execution status inline in the transcript (running, completed, error) with the hook name and statusMessage. Vanta will run hooks (CC-HOOKS-ENGINE next) but has no visual feedback for hook execution.
items.done: Running hooks show a progress line with hook name + statusMessage; on completion the line updates to success/error; multiple concurrent hooks show stacked.

items.title: Message timestamps — per-message time display
items.summary: CC renders a MessageTimestamp on each message showing elapsed time since session start and absolute time. Useful for understanding latency, reviewing session history, and correlating with external events. Vanta shows no timestamps.
items.done: Each message shows a dim timestamp (hh:mm:ss relative + HH:MM absolute); a settings key `showMessageTimestamps` toggles them; they're always on in export/headless mode.

items.title: Message selector — select and copy transcript messages
items.summary: CC's MessageSelector lets users select individual messages or ranges in the transcript and copy them to the clipboard. Essential for sharing outputs, extracting code, and referencing prior turns. Vanta has no message-level selection.
items.done: A key (e.g. Ctrl+Shift+C or configurable) enters selection mode; arrow keys move the cursor, Shift extends the selection; Enter copies selected messages to clipboard; Esc cancels.

items.title: Interrupted message — distinct visual when user interrupts a turn
items.summary: CC renders a distinct InterruptedByUser message when Esc aborts a turn, showing what partial work was done before the stop. Vanta aborts cleanly but shows no marker in the transcript that a turn was interrupted vs completed.
items.done: When a turn is aborted via Esc, an 'Interrupted' marker appears in the transcript with the partial tool-use count and a prompt to continue or ask again.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. New transcript Entry kind "interrupted" (yellow ⎋ marker) + reducer action { t: "interrupted" } that preserves partial streamed text as an assistant entry, appends the marker, and ends the turn (busy=false, idle). use-agent-send.ts Esc handler now dispatches "interrupted" (was a generic note) and sets interruptedRef; the send .catch short-circuits on interruptedRef so the AbortError no longer also surfaces as a noisy "error:" note. interruptedRef reset at each turn start. +3 tests (reducer preserves-partial + EntryRow render); full TUI suite 224 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Distinct bash input/output message types
items.summary: CC renders bash calls as two distinct message types: UserBashInputMessage (the command, with syntax highlighting) and UserBashOutputMessage (the result, scrollable). Vanta shows shell tool results as plain text inside a generic tool result block.
items.done: Bash commands render with a $ prompt and syntax highlighting; output renders in a distinct scrollable block with a line count; long outputs show a truncation hint with expand option.

items.title: Shell command timing — elapsed time display per command
items.summary: CC's ShellTimeDisplay + ShellProgressMessage show real elapsed time for each shell command, updating live during execution. Helps identify slow commands, understand build times, and debug performance issues. Vanta shows no timing.
items.done: Running shell commands show a live elapsed timer (HH:MM:SS); on completion the final time is shown dimmed next to the command; sub-second commands show ms.

items.title: Collapsed read/search content — auto-fold long tool output
items.summary: CC's CollapsedReadSearchContent auto-folds long file reads and search results to N lines by default, with a 'show more' control. Prevents large file reads from flooding the transcript. Vanta shows full tool output inline.
items.done: File reads and search results > N lines (configurable, default 20) render collapsed to a summary line; Ctrl+O or a keybinding expands/collapses; the full output is never lost.
items.notes: lineCount on ToolEntry; INLINE_MAX=5/FOLD_PREVIEW=12 in tool-result.ts; short outputs inline always, long fold behind ^O; hasFoldable fixed.

items.title: Channel message display — incoming platform messages in transcript
items.summary: CC renders UserChannelMessage as a visually distinct message type showing the source platform (Telegram, Slack, etc.) and sender alongside the message content. Vanta's gateway handles incoming messages (E2 shipped) but they're rendered as plain user text.
items.done: Incoming channel messages (Telegram, iMessage, etc.) render with a platform badge + sender name + message content, distinct from direct user input.

items.title: Context visualization — per-category breakdown + autocompact buffer + suggestions
items.summary: CC's ContextVisualization shows context usage broken down per category (tools, memory, messages, autocompact buffer, etc.) with a CollapseStatus showing what context-collapse did. ContextSuggestions offers proactive actions to reduce context. Vanta shows a context % number (CC-S-CONTEXT shipped) but no breakdown.
items.done: A /context view shows per-category context usage as bars with labels; autocompact buffer size is called out; ContextSuggestions appear when context is >70% used; CollapseStatus shows if collapse happened.

items.title: Effort indicator — visual effort level callout in TUI
items.summary: CC's EffortCallout + EffortIndicator render the active effort level visually (low/medium/high/xhigh/max) with a colored indicator. Pairs with CC-EFFORT (next) for the flag/setting. Vanta has no effort-level display even once the setting is added.
items.done: The status line (or spinner area) shows the active effort level as a colored indicator; /effort <level> changes it with immediate visual feedback.

items.title: Agent creation wizard — guided multi-step agent definition UI
items.summary: CC has a full guided wizard for creating custom named agents: 12 steps (Type, Method, Color, Description, Memory, Model, Tools, Prompt, Generate, Location, Confirm, ConfirmWrapper). Each step is a dedicated screen with keyboard navigation. Agents are saved as typed config files. No equivalent in Vanta.
items.done: /new-agent (or vanta agents new) launches the wizard; each step validates before advancing; the finished agent is saved to ~/.vanta/agents/ and immediately available as a delegate target.

items.title: Per-tool permission request UIs — dedicated dialogs per tool type
items.summary: CC has dedicated permission request components for each tool: BashPermissionRequest (shows command + options), FileEditPermissionRequest (shows diff), FileWritePermissionRequest (shows new content), NotebookEdit, PowerShell, Sandbox, Sed, Skill, WebFetch, ComputerUse. Each shows the relevant context for informed approval. Vanta shows a generic y/n prompt.
items.done: Each tool type has a purpose-built permission dialog: Bash shows the command with syntax highlight + options; FileEdit shows a diff; FileWrite shows new content; all support Allow/Deny/Always/Never rules.

items.title: Sandbox settings UI — config, dependencies, doctor, overrides tabs
items.summary: CC's SandboxSettings UI has 4 tabs: Config (enable/disable, filesystem/network isolation), Dependencies (packages to pre-install), Doctor (health diagnostics), Overrides (per-tool sandbox rules). Pairs with CC-SANDBOX (next). Vanta has no sandbox UI.
items.done: /sandbox opens the settings UI; Config tab toggles sandbox + isolation levels; Dependencies lists pre-installed packages; Doctor diagnoses issues; Overrides lets specific tools bypass or enforce.

items.title: MCP management panel — list, tool detail, reconnect, elicitation
items.summary: CC's MCP UI has: MCPListPanel (all servers + connection status), MCPToolDetailView (tool schema + description), MCPToolListView (all tools per server), MCPReconnect (retry failed server), ElicitationDialog (MCP server requesting user input mid-call), MCPAgentServerMenu, MCPRemoteServerMenu. Vanta mounts MCP servers but has no management UI.
items.done: /mcp opens the panel; each server shows connected/error status with reconnect option; tool detail shows name/schema/description; ElicitationDialog appears when a server needs user input.

items.title: Memory management UI — file selector + update notifications
items.summary: CC has MemoryFileSelector (browse + select memory files for edit/delete) and MemoryUpdateNotification (inline notification when a memory was saved or changed). Vanta has memory (MEM-* shipped) but no in-session memory management UI.
items.done: /memory opens the file selector; selecting a file opens it in the editor; MemoryUpdateNotification appears in the transcript when memory is written; notifications include file name + change type.

items.title: Hooks configuration menu — event/hook/matcher mode selectors
items.summary: CC's hooks UI has: HooksConfigMenu (list all hooks), SelectEventMode (pick event type), SelectHookMode (pick hook type: command/http/etc), SelectMatcherMode (configure matchers), ViewHookMode (inspect a hook). Pairs with CC-HOOKS-CMD (next) for /hooks command. Vanta will have /hooks but needs this interactive config UI.
items.done: /hooks opens the config menu; arrow keys navigate; selecting a hook opens view mode; new hook wizard walks through event → type → matcher → action; all hooks are editable without touching settings.json.

items.title: BypassPermissions confirmation dialog — explicit gate for dangerous mode
items.summary: CC shows BypassPermissionsModeDialog when entering bypassPermissions mode, requiring explicit confirmation and showing a risk summary. Prevents accidental activation of the most permissive mode. Vanta allows mode switching without a dedicated confirmation step.
items.done: Switching to bypass/bypassPermissions mode shows a modal: risk summary, list of what will be allowed without prompts, explicit confirmation; confirmed mode is shown with a persistent 'dangerous' badge.

items.title: Cost threshold dialog — alert when approaching budget cap
items.summary: CC's CostThresholdDialog alerts when cumulative session cost approaches a threshold (80% and 100% of --max-budget-usd). Pairs with CC-BUDGET-CAP (next). Without this UI, the agent silently stops at budget without user awareness.
items.done: At 80% of the budget cap, a non-blocking warning shows in the status; at 100%, a dialog offers to continue with a new cap, stop now, or ignore; actual stop behavior is configurable.

items.title: Token warning — approaching context limit alert
items.summary: CC's TokenWarning renders a visible alert when the conversation is approaching the context window limit (e.g. >85% full), prompting the user to compact or start a new session. Vanta tracks context % (CC-S-CONTEXT) but has no alert threshold UI.
items.done: When context fill crosses a configurable threshold (default 85%), a persistent warning appears near the composer with context % and a /compact shortcut; at 95% the warning turns urgent.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. tui/status-bar.tsx: pure tokenWarningLevel(estTokens, contextWindow, env) → none/warn/urgent + tokenWarnFractions(env) (VANTA_TOKEN_WARN_FRAC default 0.85, VANTA_TOKEN_URGENT_FRAC default 0.95). The status bar (already below the composer, receiving estTokens+contextWindow) colors the % yellow and appends '▲ /compact' at warn, red 'context full · /compact' at urgent — zero app.tsx changes (StatusBar self-computes; env read at render time, not module load). +12 tests; full TUI suite 218 green, tsc clean. Documented in .env.example.

items.title: Export dialog — conversation export UI
items.summary: CC's ExportDialog lets users export the transcript in multiple formats (markdown, JSON, plain text) with options to include/exclude tool calls, thinking, and metadata. Vanta has /export (CC-S-EXPORT shipped) but no format-selection dialog.
items.done: /export opens a dialog with format options (markdown/JSON/text), include/exclude toggles for tool calls and thinking, and destination (file/clipboard); a preview shows the first N lines of the export.

items.title: Global search dialog — search across all sessions and messages
items.summary: CC's GlobalSearchDialog (Ctrl+K or similar) searches across all stored sessions and messages, showing ranked results with session context. Vanta has per-session search and SESSION-SEARCH (shipped) for session listing, but no full-text cross-session search UI.
items.done: Ctrl+K opens the global search overlay; results show session name + date + matched message excerpt; Enter opens the session at the match; Esc dismisses; results are ranked by recency + relevance.

items.title: Markdown table rendering — GFM table support in terminal output
items.summary: CC's MarkdownTable renders GitHub-Flavored Markdown tables with aligned columns, borders, and header separators in the terminal. Vanta's markdown renderer (CC-S-MARKDOWN shipped) handles headers/bullets/code but not tables.
items.done: GFM table syntax renders as aligned terminal tables with column borders; wide tables wrap or scroll; empty cells and alignment directives (left/right/center) are respected.

items.title: Feedback survey UI — in-session survey with transcript sharing
items.summary: CC's FeedbackSurvey shows periodic in-session surveys with multi-digit rating input, memory-related questions, post-compact prompts, and optional transcript sharing. Includes useDebouncedDigitInput for smooth rating entry and TranscriptSharePrompt. No equivalent in Vanta.
items.done: A non-blocking survey prompt appears at configured intervals; digit input captures ratings; transcript sharing consent is shown separately; results are sent to the configured analytics endpoint or local file.

items.title: Mouse support — click events, hit testing, focus in TUI
items.summary: CC's ink layer has full mouse support: click-event.ts, hit-test.ts, focus-event.ts, terminal-event.ts — elements can declare click handlers and receive mouse focus. Enables clickable links, buttons, and list items in the TUI. Vanta's Ink TUI is keyboard-only.
items.done: Terminal mouse events are captured; UI elements register hit areas; clicking a link, button, or list item triggers its handler; focus follows click; mouse can be disabled for performance.

items.title: Text selection + clipboard in TUI
items.summary: CC's ink layer has selection.ts + use-selection.ts + searchHighlight.ts enabling range-based text selection within the transcript and copy to clipboard. Distinct from MessageSelector (which selects whole messages); this selects arbitrary text spans. Vanta has no TUI text selection.
items.done: Shift+arrow extends a text selection in the transcript; Ctrl+C copies the selection to clipboard; a visual highlight shows the selected range; selection clears on next keystroke.

items.title: Alternate terminal screen — fullscreen mode for dialogs
items.summary: CC's AlternateScreen component switches the terminal to the alternate screen buffer for fullscreen dialogs and views (settings, MCP panel, diff viewer, etc.), restoring the main screen on exit. Vanta's dialogs render inline, pushing content down.
items.done: Full-screen views (settings, MCP panel, diff, agent wizard) use the alternate screen; main transcript is preserved and restored on exit; Esc always exits the alternate screen.
items.notes: VirtualTranscript renders viewport slice (maxVisible=termRows-12) in alt-screen mode. scrollBy/scrollReset actions + pgup/pgdn keys. Static kept for non-alt-screen. All three resolved by one implementation. 2026-06-10: resize ghost-frame regression killed + banner restored via tui/alt-frame.tsx (always-overflow blank-filler viewport forces Ink's clearTerminal full-rewrite path instead of the cursor-relative eraseLines path that under-erases after a resize rewrap; a redraw nonce defeats log-update's identical-string skip) + tui/use-term-size.ts (reactive rows/cols + debounced resize redraw). Banner unified as a title-bordered two-column card across both screen modes. Commits a07f84c + 8b10fa6; see ERRORS.md 2026-06-10 pt 2.

items.title: ScrollBox — proper scrollable content containers
items.summary: CC's ScrollBox provides bounded scrollable regions with keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Page Up/Down, Home/End) independent of terminal scroll. Used for file pickers, MCP tool lists, permission dialogs with long content. Vanta relies on terminal scroll only.
items.done: ScrollBox renders a bounded height region; arrow keys scroll content without scrolling the whole terminal; scroll position is shown as a mini scrollbar; Home/End jump to top/bottom.
items.notes: VirtualTranscript renders viewport slice (maxVisible=termRows-12) in alt-screen mode. scrollBy/scrollReset actions + pgup/pgdn keys. Static kept for non-alt-screen. All three resolved by one implementation.

items.title: Tab navigation — focus management between UI elements
items.summary: CC's tabstops.ts + use-tab-status.ts enable Tab/Shift+Tab focus traversal between interactive elements (buttons, inputs, list items). Required for the settings UI, MCP panel, hooks config, and agent wizard to be keyboard-navigable. Vanta's TUI has no focus traversal.
items.done: Tab moves focus to the next interactive element; Shift+Tab moves back; focused elements show a visual indicator; Enter activates; all dialogs and panels are fully keyboard-navigable.

items.title: Terminal window title — session name / active task in title bar
items.summary: CC's use-terminal-title.ts sets the terminal window/tab title to the current session name or active task, so users can identify Vanta sessions in a terminal multiplexer or across multiple windows. Vanta sets no terminal title.
items.done: The terminal title updates to 'Vanta — <session name>' at session start and '<active task>' while working; resets to 'Vanta' on idle; disabled with an env var.

items.title: TUI design system — Byline, Dialog, Divider, FuzzyPicker, ProgressBar, Ratchet, Tabs, themed components
items.summary: CC has a full internal design system for the TUI: Byline (attribution line), Dialog (modal container), Divider (horizontal rule), FuzzyPicker (generic fuzzy picker), ProgressBar (bounded progress indicator), Ratchet (progressive disclosure), Tabs (tabbed pane), ThemedBox/ThemedText (theme-aware wrappers), color token system. Vanta uses raw Ink primitives ad-hoc.
items.done: A vanta-ts/src/tui/design-system/ module exports all core primitives; all existing dialogs/pickers/panels are refactored to use them; theme tokens control colors from settings.

items.title: Output style picker UI — in-TUI style switcher
items.summary: CC's OutputStylePicker renders an interactive in-TUI list of available output styles (default + custom from settings) with live preview. Pairs with CC-OUTPUT-STYLE (next) for the /output-style command. Vanta needs the visual picker to make the feature usable.
items.done: /output-style opens the picker; arrow keys preview each style; Enter applies; the active style shows in the status line; custom styles defined in settings appear alongside built-ins.

items.title: Session preview — visual session content preview in resume picker
items.summary: CC's SessionPreview renders a thumbnail-style preview of a session's content (first user message, message count, last activity, model) in the session resume picker. Vanta's session list (/resume) shows ID + timestamp only.
items.done: /resume shows a list with session previews: first prompt (truncated), message count, last-active timestamp, model used; arrow keys navigate; Enter resumes; sessions are searchable by content.

items.title: Worktree exit dialog — confirmation when leaving a worktree
items.summary: CC's WorktreeExitDialog confirms worktree exit when there are uncommitted changes, showing a summary of modified files and offering to commit, stash, or discard before exiting. Pairs with CC-WORKTREE (next). Without this, leaving a worktree silently loses work.
items.done: Exiting a worktree with uncommitted changes shows a dialog listing changed files and offering Commit / Stash / Discard / Cancel; the chosen action is applied before returning to the main worktree.

items.title: Status notices — notices area above the status line
items.summary: CC's StatusNotices renders a notices area above the status line for non-critical informational messages: IDE connection status, channel setup hints, keybinding warnings, LSP recommendations. Separate from the status line so notices don't clutter the primary info. Vanta has no notices layer.
items.done: A notices area appears above the status line when there are active notices; each notice has a type (info/warning/action), message, and optional dismiss shortcut; notices auto-expire after N seconds.

items.title: Structured diff renderer — color diff, file list, detail view
items.summary: CC has StructuredDiff (colorized inline diff with context lines), StructuredDiffList, DiffDetailView (expandable hunk view), DiffDialog, and DiffFileList (multi-file diff navigator). The /diff command uses these. CC-DIFF-VIEWER (shipped) covers the basic /diff command; Vanta is missing the proper color diff renderer and file-list navigator.
items.done: File edits render with color-diff (red/green lines, context collapse); /diff opens a multi-file navigator with DiffFileList; DiffDetailView shows individual hunks with expand/collapse.

items.title: Syntax-highlighted code blocks — HighlightedCode with language detection
items.summary: CC's HighlightedCode renders code blocks with full syntax highlighting (language-specific token coloring) and a Fallback for unsupported languages. Vanta shows code in fenced blocks (CC-S-MARKDOWN) but without syntax-token coloring.
items.done: Code blocks in the transcript detect language from the fence label and render with token-level syntax coloring; the Fallback shows plain monospace when the language is unknown or highlighting is disabled.

items.title: ThinkingToggle — dedicated expand/collapse control for reasoning
items.summary: CC has a standalone ThinkingToggle component separate from the thinking display itself — a dedicated control that toggles all thinking sections at once, with state persisted across the session. CC-S-THINKING (shipped) shows thinking collapsible; this is the persistent global toggle.
items.done: A ThinkingToggle in the status or footer toggles all thinking sections at once; state persists in settings; keyboard shortcut cycles thinking display (hidden / summary / full).

items.title: ToolUseLoader — dedicated loading animation per tool call
items.summary: CC's ToolUseLoader shows a distinct animated loading state while a tool is executing (between call and result), including the tool name and a progress indicator. Vanta shows the spinner globally; individual tool calls have no per-tool loading state.
items.done: While a tool is running, its entry shows a tool-specific loader (name + animated indicator); on completion it transitions to the result; parallel calls each have their own loader.

items.title: QuickOpenDialog — Ctrl+P fuzzy open for files, sessions, commands
items.summary: CC's QuickOpenDialog (Ctrl+P / Cmd+P) is a unified fuzzy picker for files, past sessions, slash commands, and skills — similar to VS Code's command palette but covering all entry points at once. Vanta has a slash palette but no unified quick-open.
items.done: Ctrl+P opens QuickOpenDialog; typing filters across files (current project), sessions (recent), commands (/), and skills; Enter opens the result; category icons distinguish the types.

items.title: Settings panel UI — Config, Status, Usage tabs
items.summary: CC's Settings UI has four tabs: Config (all settings as an editable form), Status (connection status for providers/MCP/IDE), Usage (token/cost breakdown per session and total). Vanta has /config (CC-CONFIG next) but no full panel UI. The Settings panel makes settings discoverable without editing JSON.
items.done: /settings opens the panel; Config tab lists all settings with types + current values editable inline; Status tab shows provider/MCP/IDE connection states; Usage tab shows token + cost charts.

items.title: Task management panel — TaskListV2, background task dialogs
items.summary: CC has TaskListV2 (live list of all tasks with status badges), BackgroundTasksDialog, BackgroundTask + BackgroundTaskStatus components, plus detail dialogs: AsyncAgentDetailDialog, DreamDetailDialog, InProcessTeammateDetailDialog, RemoteSessionDetailDialog, ShellDetailDialog. Vanta has CC-TASKS-CMD (next) for the /tasks command; this is the full panel UI.
items.done: /tasks opens the panel; each task shows type (agent/shell/remote) + status + elapsed; selecting a task opens the detail dialog with full output log; tasks can be stopped/respawned from the panel.

items.title: Teams UI — TeamsDialog, TeamStatus, task assignment messages
items.summary: CC has TeamsDialog (create/manage agent teams), TeamStatus (live team state), TaskAssignmentMessage (shows task assignment in transcript), UserTeammateMessage (teammate-to-leader comms). Pairs with CC-TEAMS (next tool). Vanta has no teams management UI.
items.done: /teams opens TeamsDialog for creating and managing agent teams; running teams show TeamStatus in the spinner area; task assignments and teammate messages appear distinctly in the transcript.

items.title: TrustDialog — project trust + MCP server trust confirmation
items.summary: CC's TrustDialog prompts the user to confirm trust for new projects (allowing CLAUDE.md to load) and new MCP servers (before mounting their tools). Blocking trust decisions behind explicit confirmation is a key security UX. Vanta loads VANTA.md and mounts MCP without a trust gate.
items.done: First time a project is opened, TrustDialog shows its CLAUDE.md contents and asks to trust; first time an MCP server is mounted, a dialog shows its tools and asks to trust; decisions are persisted in project settings.

items.title: SkillsMenu — in-TUI skill browser and picker
items.summary: CC's SkillsMenu is a browsable, searchable in-TUI menu of all installed skills with their descriptions and invocation syntax. Vanta shows skills in the banner and /skills lists them, but there's no interactive in-TUI skill picker.
items.done: /skills opens SkillsMenu; arrow keys navigate; Enter invokes the skill; search filters by name/description; installed vs available skills are distinguished.

items.title: Agent management UI — AgentDetail, AgentEditor, AgentsList, AgentsMenu
items.summary: CC has AgentDetail (full agent view with model/tools/memory), AgentEditor (edit agent config inline), AgentsList + AgentsMenu (browse all defined agents), plus the full creation wizard. Pairs with CC-BG-AGENTS (next) and CC-AGENT-WIZARD (horizon). Vanta has no agent management UI.
items.done: /agents list shows all defined agents; selecting one opens AgentDetail with config; AgentEditor allows inline editing; a ColorPicker and ModelSelector are embedded within the editor.

items.title: Advisor message — stronger-model reviewer response in transcript
items.summary: CC's AdvisorMessage renders the response from an advisor (stronger model) in a visually distinct message type in the transcript — different border/color from regular assistant messages. The advisor pattern is used by CC's built-in code reviewer. Vanta calls the advisor but renders its output identically to other tool results.
items.done: When the advisor tool returns, its response renders in a distinct bordered block with an 'Advisor' label; the model name appears dimmed; the content renders as full markdown.

items.title: ShutdownMessage — graceful session end display
items.summary: CC's ShutdownMessage shows a structured session-end summary: what was accomplished, shutdown reason (user exit / budget exceeded / context full / error), and a handoff note. Vanta exits without a session summary in the transcript.
items.done: On session end, a ShutdownMessage appears showing the shutdown reason, a brief session summary (goals accomplished, files changed, cost), and any handoff note for resuming.

items.title: Auto mode opt-in dialog — confirmation flow for ML permission mode
items.summary: CC's AutoModeOptInDialog walks users through enabling auto mode with an explanation of how ML classification works, what actions it auto-approves, and a soft_deny preview. Pairs with CC-AUTO-MODE (next). Without this dialog, auto mode activation has no UX scaffolding.
items.done: Enabling auto mode shows AutoModeOptInDialog: explains ML classification, shows the soft_deny preset, previews which actions would be auto-approved in the current session, then confirms.

items.title: InvalidConfigDialog + InvalidSettingsDialog — actionable config error UX
items.summary: CC renders dedicated dialogs for invalid config (.mcp.json parse errors, settings.json schema violations) showing the specific error, file location, and a suggested fix instead of crashing or silent fallback. Vanta logs parse errors to console with no UI.
items.done: Config parse errors show InvalidConfigDialog with: which file, what's wrong, the offending line, and a 'Fix in editor' button; settings schema violations show ValidationErrorsList with all violations.

items.title: WorkflowMultiselectDialog — multi-select UI for workflow steps
items.summary: CC's WorkflowMultiselectDialog lets users select which workflow steps to run, skip, or configure before launching a multi-agent workflow. Pairs with WORKFLOWS (shipped). Vanta runs workflows automatically without a step-selection UI.
items.done: Before a workflow runs, WorkflowMultiselectDialog shows all phases with checkbox selection; users can skip steps or reorder; confirmed steps proceed; skipped steps are logged as intentionally omitted.

items.title: KeybindingWarnings — conflict detection and resolution hints
items.summary: CC's KeybindingWarnings detects and surfaces conflicting keybindings (e.g. user-defined bindings that shadow built-ins) with a notice and suggestions to resolve. Pairs with CC-TMUX (horizon) and TUI-KEYS (next). Without conflict detection, users silently lose bindings.
items.done: On session start, KeybindingWarnings checks for conflicts; StatusNotices shows a warning for each conflict with the conflicting keys and a resolution hint; /keybindings shows the full conflict report.

items.title: Animated startup — AnimatedAsterisk, feed system, channels notice
items.summary: CC's LogoV2 system has an animated startup sequence: AnimatedAsterisk (spinning logo glyph), AnimatedClawd (animated character), a Feed with FeedColumn layout for startup tips + notices, ChannelsNotice (new channel setup), WelcomeV2 (first-run welcome), and upsell callouts. Vanta shows a static banner.
items.done: Session start shows an animated logo + a feed of context-relevant notices (new skills, channels available, reminders); animation respects prefersReducedMotion; feed items are prioritized by relevance.

items.title: In-TUI auto-updater — update notification + one-click upgrade
items.summary: CC has AutoUpdater + AutoUpdaterWrapper + NativeAutoUpdater + PackageManagerAutoUpdater — components that detect new versions, show an inline notification with changelog summary, and allow one-key upgrade without leaving the session. Vanta has `vanta update` (SELF-UPDATE shipped) but no in-session update notification.
items.done: When a new version is available, a StatusNotice shows version + summary; pressing the suggested key runs the upgrade inline; the session resumes after restart; PackageManager flavor handles npm/brew/homebrew installs.

items.title: SearchBox — in-transcript search with highlight and navigation
items.summary: CC's SearchBox is a reusable in-TUI search component: renders a search input overlay on the transcript, highlights all matches using searchHighlight.ts, and supports arrow-key navigation between matches with a match count indicator. Used by VirtualMessageList and GlobalSearchDialog.
items.done: A SearchBox can be bound to any scrollable content; it highlights all matches inline; arrow keys jump between matches with a '3 of 14' indicator; Esc closes and clears highlights.

items.title: Bidirectional text — RTL/LTR mixing in TUI output
items.summary: CC's ink/bidi.ts handles bidirectional text (Hebrew, Arabic, mixed RTL/LTR) for correct rendering order in the terminal. Required for proper display when agents work with RTL-language content or codebase comments in RTL languages. Vanta's renderer assumes LTR.
items.done: Text blocks with RTL characters render with correct bidi ordering; mixed RTL/LTR paragraphs use the Unicode Bidi Algorithm; terminal width calculations account for bidi reordering.

items.title: Resource update message — MCP resource change in transcript
items.summary: CC's UserResourceUpdateMessage renders MCP resource subscription updates (when a subscribed MCP resource changes) as a distinct message type in the transcript, showing what changed and from which server. Pairs with CC-MCP-RESOURCES (next).
items.done: When an MCP resource changes, a UserResourceUpdateMessage appears in the transcript: server name, resource URI, and a summary of what changed; these can be filtered from the transcript view.

items.title: Bash auto-classifier — ML-based auto-approval for safe bash commands
items.summary: CC runs an async classifier against every bash command before surfacing a permission dialog. Coordinator workers await the result sequentially; swarm workers try it before forwarding to the leader. Races against user interaction with a resolve-once guard.
items.done: Bash commands classified as safe auto-approve without dialog; unsafe/unknown fall through to normal permission flow; coordinator and swarm workers respect classifier result.

items.title: PR status polling — live review state during session
items.summary: Polls `gh` every 60 s during an active session to show PR review state (approved / changes-requested / pending) for the current branch. Backs off after 60 min idle; self-disables if a fetch exceeds 4 s.
items.done: PR number, URL, and review state visible in session status; polling pauses when session is idle >60 min; disabled if gh is slow.

items.title: Process memory monitor — high/critical heap alerts
items.summary: Polls Node.js heap usage every 10 s and emits a notification at high (1.5 GB) and critical (2.5 GB) thresholds. Suppresses re-renders when status is normal.
items.done: Notification shown when heap crosses 1.5 GB; escalates at 2.5 GB; clears when usage drops back below threshold.

items.title: Background file index — lazy singleton for @-mention completions
items.summary: Lazy singleton FileIndex built from git ls-files (tracked) + ripgrep (untracked), respecting .gitignore. Chunks work with event-loop yields. Emits a signal on build completion so typeahead upgrades partial results. Also surfaces CLAUDE.md / rules files as suggestion sources.
items.done: @path completions return git-tracked and untracked files; .gitignore entries excluded; partial results upgrade to full after index completes; CLAUDE.md paths surfaced.

items.title: Unified @-completion typeahead — files, MCP resources, agents, slash, Slack, shell history
items.summary: Single typeahead covering: @path file completions, @AgentType agent completions with color, MCP resource completions, slash-command + argument hints, #channel Slack completions (when Slack MCP active), shell history completions, directory path completions. Fuse.js fuzzy matching. Inline ghost text for best match.
items.done: Typing @ surfaces files/agents/MCP resources; typing / surfaces commands with argument hints; typing # surfaces Slack channels; ghost text shows best match inline; Fuse fuzzy matching ranks results.

items.title: IDE diff integration — open file edits in IDE diff viewer
items.summary: When the IDE extension is connected via MCP WebSocket, routes file-edit permission dialogs to the IDE's native diff viewer (callIdeRpc) instead of the TUI diff. Supports VS Code and JetBrains. Windows-to-WSL path conversion included.
items.done: File edits show in IDE diff viewer when extension connected; falls back to TUI diff when no IDE; WSL paths mapped correctly.

items.title: Swarm permission routing — workers forward permission asks to lead agent
items.summary: Swarm workers serialise permission-ask actions and forward them to the lead agent via mailbox IPC. Poll every 500 ms for allow/reject response. Schema-validates incoming PermissionUpdate entries; drops malformed ones. Coordinator workers run hooks + classifier sequentially before falling through to the interactive dialog.
items.done: Worker agent permission asks appear in the lead agent's permission queue; lead's allow/reject propagates back to the worker within ~500 ms; malformed entries logged and dropped.

items.title: Plugin install hints via <claude-code-hint> — stderr-driven plugin prompts
items.summary: CLIs and SDKs emit `<claude-code-hint plugin='slug' />` tags to stderr. CC intercepts, deduplicates (show-once per plugin ever), and surfaces a one-time notification to install the plugin from the marketplace. Shown/dismissed state recorded in config.
items.done: Running a tool that emits a hint tag shows a one-time install prompt; dismissing or installing prevents re-prompt; disabling hint recommendations suppresses all future hints.

items.title: User keybinding customization — ~/.claude/keybindings.json with hot-reload
items.summary: CC loads user keybindings from ~/.claude/keybindings.json (schema-validated via Zod, hot-reloads via chokidar). Users define context-scoped bindings with named action IDs (app:interrupt, history:previous, etc.); default bindings ship as a documented template. /doctor reports validation errors.
items.done: Users can add/modify keybindings in ~/.claude/keybindings.json; changes reload without restart; invalid entries reported by /doctor; generating the template pre-fills all defaults.

items.title: Chord bindings — multi-keystroke sequences (e.g. ctrl+k ctrl+s)
items.summary: Keybinding parser handles chord sequences (two or more keystrokes). Resolver tracks pending-chord state; results include chord_started, chord_cancelled, match, none. Platform modifier aliases normalised (ctrl/alt/meta/cmd/super).
items.done: Multi-keystroke chords defined in keybindings.json fire correctly; partial chord shows pending state; invalid followup cancels without side effects; modifier aliases all work.

items.title: Keybinding contexts — 17+ named UI contexts for context-sensitive bindings
items.summary: Bindings are scoped to named contexts: Global, Chat, Autocomplete, Confirmation, Help, Transcript, HistorySearch, Task, ThemePicker, Settings, Tabs, Attachments, Footer, MessageSelector, DiffDialog, ModelPicker, Select, Plugin. Context registers/unregisters at React mount/unmount; resolver applies priority by active context set.
items.done: Bindings only fire in their declared context; Global bindings work everywhere; context stack updates on dialog open/close; conflicts within a context surface a warning.

items.title: useShortcutDisplay — render user-configured keybinding hints in UI
items.summary: React hook and non-React function that return the display text for any action+context pair from the user's live keybinding config. Used in help overlays, confirmation dialogs, and typeahead footers so shown shortcuts always match the user's custom config.
items.done: Keyboard hints in help overlays and dialogs show the user's configured binding, not hardcoded defaults; non-React callers (commands, services) use the sync variant.

items.title: /skillify — capture current session as a reusable skill
items.summary: Bundled /skillify skill that reads session memory + user messages, then generates a new skill file (frontmatter + prompt) encoding the session's repeatable process. User describes the skill name; the model produces a ready-to-commit skill file.
items.done: Running /skillify after a session produces a valid skill .md file with frontmatter and a prompt that recreates the session's workflow; the file is saved to the skills directory.

items.title: Skill companion files — bundled assets extracted to disk on first use
items.summary: Skills can declare `files: Record<string, string>` of companion text assets. On first invocation CC extracts them to a per-skill base directory and prepends a 'Base directory: <dir>' line to the prompt so the model can Read/Grep them on demand.
items.done: Skill with declared files extracts them on first run; subsequent runs find them already present; model can reference them by path without knowing the extraction location.

items.title: allowedTools + disableModelInvocation in skill frontmatter
items.summary: Skills can restrict their tool palette via `allowedTools: string[]` in frontmatter. `disableModelInvocation: true` makes a pure-tool skill that executes its tool list without an LLM turn. Pairs with hooks and agent fields to compose complex skill behaviors.
items.done: Skills with allowedTools only expose those tools during execution; skills with disableModelInvocation run tool calls without an LLM round-trip; /doctor reports invalid tool names.

items.title: Memory relevance via Sonnet side-query — LLM-selected memory files per turn
items.summary: Each turn, CC scans memory-file headers (frontmatter name+description only), then fires a dedicated Sonnet side-query to pick up to 5 most-relevant files for the current user query. Excludes API/usage docs for tools already in use. Returns file paths + mtime for staleness annotation.
items.done: Only relevant memory files are loaded per turn (not all memories); recently-used tool docs excluded from selection; up to 5 files selected; selection logged for debugging.

items.title: Team memory directory — shared memory namespace for swarm agents
items.summary: Separate `~/.claude/memory/team/<project>/` directory for shared team context across swarm agents. Combined prompt mode covers both private and team memory types with per-type scope guidance. Path traversal protection on all team memory writes.
items.done: Team memory files written to team directory; combined prompt includes both private and team memories with scope labels; path traversal attempts rejected with PathTraversalError.

items.title: Memory freshness caveat — staleness annotation for memories > 1 day old
items.summary: Memories older than 1 day get a human-readable age string ('47 days ago') and a staleness caveat injected alongside their content: 'This memory is N days old. Memories are point-in-time observations — file:line citations may be outdated. Verify against current code.' Prevents stale facts from being asserted confidently.
items.done: Fresh memories (today/yesterday) inject no caveat; older memories prepend the staleness note; age shown as human-readable string, not raw timestamp.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). memory/freshness.ts: pure humanAge(ageMs) ("today"/"yesterday"/"N days ago") + freshnessCaveat(ageMs) (null when <2 days, else a point-in-time caveat) + annotateMemory(content, ageMs). Wired into memory/store.ts recentMemory: each block's own ISO timestamp → age → stale blocks get the caveat prepended at the injection path (per-block granularity). +14 unit + 1 store integration test; full suite 2019 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Per-turn file diff history — track file changes by turn index
items.summary: Builds a per-turn history of file changes from FileEditTool/FileWriteTool outputs: hunks, lines added/removed, files changed, user prompt preview, and timestamp per turn. Enables the interactive diff viewer to show 'what changed in this turn' and navigate turn history.
items.done: After each turn containing file edits, a TurnDiff entry exists with structured hunk data, stats, and prompt preview; diff viewer can navigate to any prior turn's changes.

items.title: Session backgrounding — Ctrl+B to background/foreground running queries
items.summary: Ctrl+B backgrounds the current in-progress query into a background task, freeing the TUI for a new query. Foreground a running background task and sync its messages back. Enables multi-query session management without cancelling work in flight.
items.done: Ctrl+B while a query is running moves it to background; user can continue chatting; /tasks or the task panel shows background task progress; foreground a task to see its messages.

items.title: Prompts from Claude for Chrome — browser extension sends prompts to terminal
items.summary: The Claude for Chrome extension sends JSON-RPC notifications (prompt + optional image) via the MCP server; CC enqueues them as user messages and syncs permission mode back to the extension. Enables 'send to terminal' from claude.ai in the browser. Requires the Chrome extension and MCP server.
items.done: Prompts sent from the Chrome extension appear in the TUI conversation; optional images are attached; permission mode is reflected in the extension; multiple tabs tracked by tabId.

items.title: /batch — parallel multi-agent code implementation skill
items.summary: Bundled /batch skill that orchestrates a large parallelizable codebase change across 5–30 worker agents in git worktrees. Each worker implements one change, runs tests, and creates a PR. Coordinator tracks PR URLs. Uses EnterPlanMode + AgentTool.
items.done: Running /batch with a description spawns multiple worker agents in isolated worktrees; each worker creates a PR; coordinator reports PR URLs on completion; test failures are fixed before PR creation.

items.title: /loop — recurring cron task from natural-language interval
items.summary: Bundled /loop skill that parses a natural-language interval (10m, 30m, 2h, 1d) + prompt, then creates a CronCreate task to run it recurrently. Handles 'every 20m' prose, defaults to 10m. Uses the Kairos cron engine.
items.done: '/loop 30m check the deploy' creates a cron task that fires every 30 min; '/loop /babysit-prs' defaults to 10m; interval parsed from the end of the string if prose format.

items.title: Direct-connect server — CC as local HTTP+WebSocket server for IDE/client connections
items.summary: CC can run as a local HTTP+WebSocket server (--server flag). Clients POST to /sessions to create a session, receive ws_url. Supports auth token, dangerously_skip_permissions, idle timeout, max concurrent sessions, Unix socket. Used by IDE extensions to drive CC programmatically.
items.done: CC starts an HTTP server on a port or Unix socket; POST /sessions creates a CC session and returns a ws_url; IDE/client connects via WebSocket to stream messages and send prompts.

items.title: Remote session viewer mode — observe a CCR session without interrupting
items.summary: viewerOnly mode on a remote session connection: Ctrl+C/Escape do NOT send interrupt to the remote agent; 60s reconnect timeout is disabled; session title is never updated. Used by `claude assistant` to observe a running remote session.
items.done: Connecting to a session in viewer mode shows its output without interrupting the agent; Ctrl+C doesn't abort; session title unchanged; multiple viewers can observe simultaneously.

items.title: Vim operator system — text objects, find motions, counts, dot-repeat, yank register
items.summary: Full vim operator system beyond basic hjkl: operators (d/c/y/>/< with motion), text objects (iw/aw/i''/a''/i(/a(/i[/a[/i{/a{), find motions (f/F/t/T with ; repeat), count prefix (3w, 2dd), dot-repeat (.), yank register, r replace, linewise vs characterwise resolution. Extends CC-VIM-MODE.
items.done: Operators with motions and counts work (3dw deletes 3 words); text objects work (ci' changes inside quotes); find motions work (f( moves to next '('); dot-repeat re-applies last change; yank register shared across d/c/y.

items.title: Custom output styles via .claude/output-styles/ — markdown-file-defined styles
items.summary: Users and projects define custom output styles as .md files in ~/.claude/output-styles/ (user) or .claude/output-styles/ (project). Each file = one named style; frontmatter provides name/description; `keep-coding-instructions: true` preserves coding behavior while applying the style. Plugin styles also load here.
items.done: Custom output styles appear in /output-style picker; project styles override user styles; styles from plugins load alongside user/project styles; keep-coding-instructions flag respected.

items.title: Magic Docs — auto-maintained markdown files updated after each turn
items.summary: Any .md file starting with `# MAGIC DOC: [title]` is automatically updated by a background subagent after each turn that contains tool calls. The doc accumulates learnings from the conversation (architecture, decisions, patterns) without interrupting the main flow. Uses post-sampling hook + sequential update guard.
items.done: Files with MAGIC DOC header are updated after turns with tool calls; updates are non-blocking; concurrent updates are serialised; the doc reflects conversation knowledge after the session.

items.title: Sub-agent progress summaries — periodic 3-5 word status in footer pill
items.summary: Coordinator sub-agents generate a 3-5 word present-tense progress summary every 30s via a forked side-query. Summaries name the specific file or function being worked on (not vague descriptions). Shown in the footer task pill. Cache-shared with parent agent to avoid extra billing.
items.done: Running sub-agent tasks show a live progress summary in the footer pill (e.g. 'Editing auth.ts'); summaries update every ~30s; summaries are specific, not vague.

items.title: Shell task stall detection — notification when background command awaits input
items.summary: Background shell tasks get a stall watchdog: if output stops growing for 45s, the last tail is pattern-matched against interactive prompt patterns (y/n, yes/no, 'Press any key', directed questions like 'Are you sure?'). If matched, a notification fires so the agent can unblock it.
items.done: Long-running commands that ask y/n surface a notification; slow commands that don't ask anything stay silent; notification fires once, not repeatedly.

items.title: Coordinator mode — env-controlled lead-agent persona with restricted tool set
items.summary: CLAUDE_CODE_COORDINATOR_MODE env var makes an agent run as a coordinator. Coordinator agents use a distinct tool set (ASYNC_AGENT_ALLOWED_TOOLS; internal swarm tools excluded from external API). matchSessionMode() detects and fixes mode mismatch when resuming a coordinator session.
items.done: Setting CLAUDE_CODE_COORDINATOR_MODE spawns an agent with coordinator-only tools; resuming a session that was coordinator retains coordinator mode; mode mismatch on resume is fixed with a warning.

items.title: In-TUI next-prompt suggestions — tappable predicted prompts after each turn
items.summary: After each model response, CC generates 3 predicted next prompts via a forked side-query and shows them as tappable options below the input. Controlled by `promptSuggestionsEnabled` setting. Disabled in swarm/teammate mode and non-interactive sessions.
items.done: 3 suggested prompts appear after a model response; selecting one submits it; suggestions update per turn; disabling in settings removes them.

items.title: Speculative prompt pre-execution — pre-run predicted next command before user submits
items.summary: After a model turn, CC speculatively pre-executes the most likely next user command (read-only tools only: Read, Grep, Glob, WebSearch, WebFetch). File state cache is shared. If user submits the predicted command, the pre-run result is used immediately. Aborted on mismatch or if user submits a different command.
items.done: Pre-run completes before user submits; matching submission uses cached result with no added latency; non-matching submission aborts speculation cleanly; read-only constraint enforced.

items.title: MCP elicitation — in-session user input prompts from MCP servers (2025-11-05 spec)
items.summary: Handles MCP `elicitation/create` requests from servers. Two modes: form (surfaces a dialog for user text input during tool execution) and url (opens browser URL, waits for completion notification via `notifications/elicitationComplete`). Pre/post hooks. Part of MCP 2025-11-05 spec.
items.done: MCP tool calling elicitation/create surfaces a dialog; user submits input; server receives the response; url mode opens browser; completion notification resolves the elicitation.

items.title: Secret scanner — block credentials from cloud memory sync
items.summary: Client-side secret scanner that checks memory file content before cloud upload. Uses curated high-confidence gitleaks rules (GitHub PAT, AWS access/secret keys, etc.) with near-zero false positives. Secrets are detected and blocked from leaving the machine.
items.done: Memory files containing GitHub PATs or AWS credentials are blocked from cloud sync; clean files sync normally; matched rule ID logged for diagnosis.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). store/secret-scan.ts: pure scanForSecrets(text)→deduped rule ids (never the values) + hasSecrets(), 7 high-confidence near-zero-FP rules (github-pat, github-fine-grained-pat, aws-access-key-id, slack-token, google-api-key, private-key, openai-key). Wired into memory/store.ts appendMemory: scans the summary FIRST and returns {skipped:true, rules} without writing/committing if a secret is found — credential content never reaches ~/.vanta or its git store. Caller (session.ts) ignores the new return (void→object compatible). +17 unit + 2 store integration tests; full suite 2019 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Auto-dream — periodic background memory consolidation across sessions
items.summary: Background service that consolidates memories across sessions via forked agent. Fires when: time since last consolidation >= minHours AND session count >= minSessions. File-based lock prevents parallel runs. Made visible in task list as DreamTask (phase: starting/updating, files touched, turn summaries).
items.done: After accumulating sessions, auto-dream fires; progress visible in footer pill; lock prevents duplicate runs; files touched tracked; consolidation updates MEMORY.md.

items.title: macOS caffeinate — prevent system sleep during long operations
items.summary: Uses macOS `caffeinate` to prevent idle sleep while CC is running API requests or tools. Self-healing: 5-minute timeout + 4-minute restart so orphaned caffeinate exits on SIGKILL. No-op on non-macOS. Ref-counted (start/stop) so multiple callers share one process.
items.done: Mac stays awake during long API calls and tool execution; caffeinate exits cleanly on process exit or after 5min idle; no-op on Linux/Windows.

items.title: Tool batch summary — Haiku-generated ~30-char label for completed tool batches
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. tools/tool-summary.ts: summarizeToolBatch() and formatToolSummary() utilities. Patterns for read/write/git/shell/web-search; fallback to tool name + count. 7 tests passing. Ready for integration into agent.ts tool dispatch loop.
items.done: Tool batch summarization utilities available; scaffolded for agent-loop integration via dispatchTool().

items.title: Channel permission relay — approve permissions via Telegram/iMessage/Discord
items.summary: When CC hits a permission dialog, it also sends the prompt to connected channels (Telegram, iMessage, Discord). User replies 'yes <request_id>' via the channel; CC receives it as a structured notification and races it against local UI. First resolver wins. Allowlist-gated per server. Requires channel MCP server supporting `claude/channel/permission` capability.
items.done: Permission dialogs arrive in connected messaging channels; replying 'yes <id>' approves without opening the terminal; local UI still works in parallel.

items.title: Settings sync — sync user settings and memory across CC environments
items.summary: Syncs user settings (incremental, only changed keys) and memory files between CC environments via Anthropic's API. Interactive CLI: uploads to remote. CCR: downloads before plugin install. OAuth required. Uses repo remote hash for project scoping.
items.done: Settings made in one CC instance are available in another after sync; only changed settings are uploaded (delta); memory files sync with the same mechanism.

items.title: Time-based microcompact — auto-clear stale tool results after cache TTL expiry
items.summary: Runs before each API call: if the gap since the last assistant message exceeds the cache TTL (default 60 min), clears old tool results from the prompt to shrink what gets rewritten. Keeps the N most recent tool results. Main thread only (subagents have short lifetimes). GrowthBook-configurable.
items.done: After 60+ minutes idle, the next request sends a compacted prompt; old tool results are cleared; the N most recent are kept; no visible disruption to the conversation.

items.title: Companion buddy — animated ASCII creature with species, rarity, stats
items.summary: CC has a virtual companion (duck, cat, octopus, robot, snail, etc.) deterministically generated from the user ID. Creatures have rarity (common → legendary), stats, hats, and eyes. Idle fidget animation with multiple ASCII art frames. Shown as a terminal sprite via useBuddyNotification.
items.done: User gets a persistent named companion visible in the TUI; creature is deterministic (same ID = same creature); rare variants are rarer; idle animation plays between turns.

items.title: ConfigTool — agent reads and updates user settings during session
items.summary: Agent-accessible tool to read the current value of a setting, list valid options, get the config file path, and update supported settings (Global config + settings.json) during a session. Enables the agent to configure itself (e.g., enable verbose mode, set model).
items.done: Agent can call ConfigTool to read/update settings without user manual edit; unsupported settings rejected; changes persisted to the appropriate config file.

items.title: LSPTool full parity — call hierarchy, find references, hover, document symbols
items.summary: CC's LSPTool exposes the full LSP API: hover info (type + docs), go-to-definition, find references (with line preview), document symbols (outline), call hierarchy (incoming/outgoing callers), workspace symbols. Multi-language via the LSP server manager (not just TS). ENABLE_LSP_TOOL env var gates it.
items.done: Agent can get call hierarchy for a function, list all references with context, get hover docs for a symbol; works for any language with an LSP server configured.

items.title: ToolSearchTool — on-demand deferred tool loading by keyword
items.summary: Agent-accessible ToolSearch tool: loads deferred tool schemas on demand by keyword query or `select:<name>` for direct selection. Keeps tool context lean by not loading all tool schemas upfront. Agent first discovers that a tool exists (from index), then fetches its full schema when needed.
items.done: Deferred tools appear as names-only in the context; calling ToolSearch loads their full schema; agent can then call them; `select:ToolName` fetches by exact name.

items.title: Task management tools — TaskCreate/Get/List/Update/Stop/Output for agent loops
items.summary: Suite of agent-accessible task management tools for the TodoV2 task system: TaskCreate (spawn background task), TaskGet (check status), TaskList (list all tasks), TaskUpdate (update title/description), TaskStop (cancel task), TaskOutput (stream task output). Enables agent orchestration of background work.
items.done: Agent can create a background task, monitor its status, stream its output, and stop it; tasks survive context compaction; task IDs are stable references.

items.title: Deep link protocol — claude-cli:// URL opens CC with pre-filled context
items.summary: Registers the `claude-cli://` URL scheme. Opening `claude-cli://open?q=fix+tests&repo=owner/repo` opens a new terminal window with CC, pre-filling the prompt and working directory. Used for 'open in terminal' integrations from claude.ai and IDE extensions. Security: control char filtering, shell-escaping at injection boundary. Feature flag: `LODESTONE`.
items.done: Clicking a claude-cli:// link opens a terminal with CC; prompt is pre-filled; working directory set from cwd/repo params; control characters in params are rejected.

items.title: .dxt extension packages — single-file MCP server installer
items.summary: `.dxt` file format (from `@anthropic-ai/mcpb`) packages an MCP server manifest + assets into a single installable file. `claude install <path>.dxt` validates the manifest and configures the MCP server. Enables one-click MCP server installation from file or URL.
items.done: Installing a .dxt file validates the manifest, mounts the MCP server in settings, and makes its tools available in the next session; invalid manifests are rejected with field-level errors.

items.title: Ultraplan keyword trigger — multi-agent planning mode from prompt keyword
items.summary: Typing 'ultraplan' in a user prompt (outside delimiters, paths, or slash commands) activates a multi-agent parallel planning mode backed by a CCR session. Keyword detection skips false positives (inside backticks, quotes, paths, identifiers). Shows a notification that ultraplan will be activated.
items.done: 'Write a spec ultraplan' activates multi-agent planning; false positives in code blocks or paths don't trigger; '/rename ultraplan foo' as a slash command doesn't trigger.

items.title: ANSI-to-PNG renderer — terminal screenshot as PNG with bitmap font
items.summary: Renders ANSI-escaped terminal output directly to a PNG image using a bundled Fira Code 24×48 bitmap font. Zero external deps; ~5-15ms per render; exact pixel grid output. Used for terminal session screenshots shared in conversations or exported via /export.
items.done: Terminal output can be exported as a PNG screenshot; output includes ANSI colors and bold; render is fast enough to use inline; no system fonts required.

items.title: /rename — set session title
items.summary: SHIPPED 2026-06-09. repl/rename-cmd.ts: /rename [name] shows current title or sets a new one (max 100 chars). Modifies ctx.state.title. Registered in HANDLERS + catalog.
items.done: `/rename` shows current title; `/rename 'new name'` updates it.

items.title: /status — show version, model, account, API health, tool statuses
items.summary: Quick-status slash command: shows CC version, active model, account type (API key / subscription), API connectivity check, and tool availability. Lighter than /doctor — instant display, no network health checks.
items.done: Running /status shows version, model name, account type, API connectivity result, and list of enabled tools; output is immediate; no network calls (except API ping).

items.title: /copy — copy last response as markdown, HTML, or RTF to clipboard
items.summary: The /copy command copies the last assistant response to the system clipboard in the user's chosen format: plain markdown, rendered HTML, or RTF. Uses OSC clipboard sequence for terminal clipboard access.
items.done: Running /copy shows a format picker; selected format is copied to clipboard; markdown copies raw text; HTML converts markdown to rendered HTML; RTF for rich text editors.

items.title: /usage — show claude.ai plan usage and limits
items.summary: Shows current claude.ai plan usage and remaining limits (message count, premium usage, token budget). Only available when signed in via Claude.ai OAuth. Shows overage options if limits are reached.
items.done: Running /usage shows current usage vs limits; overage options shown when approaching limits; not available for API-key-only users.

items.title: Pure-TypeScript bash parser — tree-sitter-compatible AST for permission analysis
items.summary: Pure-TS bash parser producing tree-sitter-bash-compatible ASTs (by field name, UTF-8 byte offsets). Used for bash permission analysis, prefix detection (!, #), heredoc handling, command graph extraction. 50ms wall-clock cap, 50K node budget cap. Validated against 3449-input golden corpus.
items.done: Bash commands parsed to AST; prefix commands (!, #) detected; heredocs handled; pipe chains parsed; 50ms/50K safety caps enforced; identical output to tree-sitter-bash for all corpus inputs.

items.title: Shell snapshot — capture aliases, functions, PATH for shell completions
items.summary: Shell snapshot captures the user's current shell environment (PATH entries, aliases, functions) by running a probe script in the user's shell. The snapshot feeds the shell completion engine in the typeahead. Uses embedded ripgrep/bfs/ugrep via argv0 dispatch for completions.
items.done: Shell aliases defined in .bashrc/.zshrc appear in typeahead completions; PATH-based executables are suggested; snapshot updated when shell config changes.

items.title: Plugin marketplace — multi-source plugin registry with local cache
items.summary: Marketplace manager supports multiple sources (URL, GitHub repo, npm package, local file). Manifests are cached in ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/ for offline access. `known_marketplaces.json` tracks all configured sources. Used by /plugin UI to browse and install from any source.
items.done: User can add a marketplace URL; manifest downloads and caches; /plugin shows marketplace entries; GitHub repo marketplaces are cloned; offline installs use cache.

items.title: Org-managed plugins — policy enforcement for required/disabled plugins
items.summary: Enterprise admins push `policySettings.enabledPlugins` via remote managed settings to require or disable specific plugins. The `{name}@{marketplace}` boolean entries are enforced: true = required (can't disable), false = blocked (can't enable). Uses policySettings from CC-H-ENTERPRISE.
items.done: Plugins required by policy appear enabled and locked; plugins blocked by policy are hidden; user cannot override policy-managed plugins.

items.title: Plugins can ship LSP server configs — auto-mount language servers
items.summary: A plugin can declare LSP server configurations in its manifest. CC reads the lsp/ directory from the plugin package, validates the config schema, and mounts the LSP server automatically when the plugin is enabled.
