items.done: Installing a plugin that includes an LSP server config makes that language server available; CC's LSP manager mounts it; /doctor shows the LSP server status.

items.title: 1M context upgrade prompt — suggest extended-context model when approaching limit
items.summary: When approaching the context window limit and the user has access to a 1M-context model variant (Opus 1M, Sonnet 1M), CC surfaces a tip or warning suggesting the upgrade with a 5× multiplier message. Context window check gated on claude.ai subscription access.
items.done: Warning shows when context is near limit and 1M variant available; tip suggests upgrade proactively; message distinguishes warning vs tip contexts; hidden when already on 1M model.

items.title: macOS Keychain credential storage — secure OAuth token persistence
items.summary: Uses macOS `security` CLI to read/write OAuth tokens and API keys to the system Keychain. TTL-cached reads (avoid repeated `security` subprocess overhead). Fallback to plain-text ~/.claude/credentials.json on failure. Handles 4096-byte stdin limit of the security tool.
items.done: OAuth tokens stored in Keychain on macOS; reads are cached for TTL ms; plain-text fallback on Linux/Windows; credentials.json is the fallback path.

items.title: Dangerous bash patterns — strip interpreter allow-rules at auto-mode entry
items.summary: A curated list of code-execution interpreters (python, node, deno, tsx, ruby, perl, php, lua, npx, bunx, bash, etc.) that are stripped from allow-rules when entering auto-mode. Prevents `Bash(python:*)` rules from bypassing the classifier. Cross-platform list shared between Bash and PowerShell checks.
items.done: Entering auto-mode strips interpreter-wildcard allow-rules; non-interpreter rules are preserved; a stripped-rules notification is shown; the list covers all common code-exec entry points.

items.title: Fix: image drag-drop from Desktop fails ENOENT (path not in scope)
items.summary: Dragging an image from ~/Desktop into the TUI fails with ENOENT. describe_image resolves the path via resolveInScope which rejects Desktop paths even though ~/Desktop is in resolveReadableZones. Tilde expansion may also be missing from the image tool's path handling.
items.done: Dragging ~/Desktop/image.png into the TUI attaches it correctly; no ENOENT; tilde paths expanded before scope check; Desktop is treated as readable zone for image attach.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. Root cause: describe_image (and compare_vision) called resolveInScope WITHOUT expandHome — '~/Desktop/x.png' resolved to a bogus '<root>/~/Desktop/x.png' that passed the in-scope test then ENOENTed; an absolute Desktop path was refused outright though ~/Desktop is a default readable zone. Fix mirrors read_file exactly: expandHome() first, then allow out-of-root paths that isInZone(resolveReadableZones()). Tool descriptions updated to say ~-paths / readable zones are OK. Fixed BOTH describe_image and compare_vision (same bug). The drag-drop attach path (repl/format.ts maybeDroppedImage) already expanded ~ and was fine. +~6 tests across describe-image/compare-vision; suite green, tsc clean. (screenshot.ts/lsp.ts keep the old in-root-only message by design — not image-from-Desktop.)

items.title: Detailed session cost tracker — per-model usage, cache tokens, tool+API duration, lines changed
items.summary: CC tracks: total cost USD, per-model token usage, API duration (with/without retries), tool duration, cache creation/read tokens, web search request count, lines added/removed, unknown-model cost flag. All aggregated per-session and per-turn for the cost footer and /stats display.
items.done: Session end shows total cost, model breakdown, cache efficiency, API vs tool time split, lines changed; /stats shows all counters; reset on /clear.

items.title: Conversation history with large-paste external storage
items.summary: CC stores conversation history (max 100 entries) in ~/.claude/history including paste contents. Large pastes are stored externally via content hash (PasteStore) rather than inline, keeping the history file small. Supports both text and image paste history. Read with lock for concurrent access safety.
items.done: History includes pasted text/images; large pastes stored externally by hash; history file stays compact; concurrent readers don't corrupt entries.

items.title: Project onboarding wizard — first-run step-by-step setup for new workspaces
items.summary: When opening CC in an empty workspace or a workspace without CLAUDE.md, a step-by-step onboarding wizard guides the user through creating a project (ask Claude to create an app, clone a repo, etc.). Steps track complete/completable/enabled state and persist to project config.
items.done: Empty workspace opens with onboarding wizard; steps complete as user progresses; CLAUDE.md creation step marks complete when file appears; wizard dismissed after completion.

items.title: TUI FPS metrics — render performance monitoring
items.summary: CC tracks TUI render FPS (frames per second) via a reservoir-sampled histogram. FpsMetricsProvider injects the getter into React context; components can read current FPS for debugging render performance. Useful for /doctor and internal perf monitoring.
items.done: FPS visible in /doctor or debug mode; histogram tracks p50/p95/p99; low FPS triggers a diagnostic note.

items.title: Agent scratchpad directory — gated workspace for temporary files
items.summary: ENABLE_SCRATCHPAD gate creates a scoped temp directory for agent use. Files written to the scratchpad don't affect the project. Coordinator workers can use it as a shared workspace for intermediate results without polluting the repo.
items.done: With ENABLE_SCRATCHPAD set, agent has a temp dir it can write to freely; dir is cleaned on session end; not accessible from outside the scratchpad scope.

items.title: mTLS + custom CA cert config — enterprise proxy TLS support
items.summary: CC supports mutual TLS (mTLS) via global agent configuration and custom CA certificate bundles loaded from config. Allows running behind enterprise MITM proxies (like Zscaler) that intercept TLS. Configured via settings.json or env vars.
items.done: With mTLS config set, CC connects through enterprise proxy without TLS errors; custom CA cert trusted globally; works for all API calls and MCP connections.

items.title: CCR bridge — connect local TUI to remote cloud sessions with polling
items.summary: The bridge module creates cloud sessions via the CCR API, polls for capacity, handles JWT token refresh, and connects the local REPL to a remote container. Supports capacity-wake (immediate wake when session frees), session spawning with timeout, trusted device tokens, and graceful shutdown with analytics drain.
items.done: Local TUI connects to remote CCR container; sessions created via API; polling pauses at capacity and wakes immediately when a slot frees; JWT auto-refreshes; metrics draining before process exit.

items.title: Files API integration — upload session files to Anthropic cloud storage
items.summary: BYOC (Bring Your Own Container) mode uploads modified files to Anthropic's Files API at the end of each turn. 1P/Cloud mode queries Files API listDirectory instead. Tracks persisted files, upload concurrency limit, and file count limit. Enables remote sessions to access session outputs.
items.done: End-of-turn modified files uploaded to Files API in BYOC mode; cloud mode syncs via rclone; file IDs tracked and available for subsequent turns.

items.title: TodoItem activeForm field — track current action verb in todo items
items.summary: Each TodoItem has a Zod-validated `activeForm` field (e.g. 'Building', 'Testing') that stores the current progressive tense of the action being performed. Displayed in the task list UI next to in_progress items as a live status line.
items.done: In-progress todo items show their activeForm (e.g. 'Building auth.ts'); completed items show a checkmark; pending items show content only.

items.title: Background remote sessions — spawn CCR sessions in background with precondition checks
items.summary: Background remote sessions spawn CCR containers in the background (type='remote_session') with todo list, title, and log. Pre-conditions: claude.ai login, remote environment available, in git repo, git remote exists, GitHub app installed, policy allowed. Status: starting/running/completed/failed/killed.
items.done: Spawning a background remote session checks all preconditions and shows clear error if any fail; running sessions tracked in task list; log accessible.

items.title: Built-in agent types — Explore, Plan, Verification, Claude Code Guide, General Purpose
items.summary: CC ships 6 built-in agent definitions: Explore (read-only codebase search), Plan (planning with EnterPlanMode), Verification (test/verify changes), General Purpose, Claude Code Guide (meta-help), Statusline Setup. Disableable via CLAUDE_AGENT_SDK_DISABLE_BUILTIN_AGENTS for SDK users. A/B gated.
items.done: 6 built-in agent types selectable in /agents or @AgentType completions; env var disables all for SDK users; coordinator mode loads coordinator-specific agents instead.

items.title: Per-agent-type memory — scoped memory dirs per agent (user/project/local)
items.summary: Each named agent type gets its own memory directory: user (~/.claude/agent-memory/<type>/), project (.claude/agent-memory/<type>/), local (.claude/agent-memory-local/<type>/). Remote memory dir for cloud (CLAUDE_CODE_REMOTE_MEMORY_DIR). Colons in plugin-namespaced types replaced with dashes for path safety.
items.done: Explore agent has its own memory separate from the main session; agent memories persist across invocations; remote mount supported for cloud sessions.

items.title: .claude/agents/ — custom agent definitions from markdown files
items.summary: Load custom agent definitions from .claude/agents/*.md. Frontmatter declares effort, allowed tools, MCP servers, tool filters, description. Like skills but specifically for spawning as named sub-agents via @AgentType mention in prompts.
items.done: Files in .claude/agents/ appear in @-completions and can be spawned as named sub-agents; frontmatter fields (effort, tools, description) parsed and applied.

items.title: Tmux swarm backend — spawn agents in tmux panes with locking + color coding
items.summary: Spawns CC agents in separate tmux panes. Sequential pane creation lock prevents race conditions. 200ms shell init delay for starship/oh-my-zsh. Leader pane cached for performance. SWARM_SESSION_NAME/HIDDEN_SESSION_NAME constants. Agents identified by color from team palette.
items.done: Running /team spawns agents in tmux panes; each pane auto-labeled with agent color; panes created sequentially without races; shell ready before agent starts.

items.title: In-process swarm backend — agents share Node.js process via AsyncLocalStorage
items.summary: Alternative to tmux backend: agents run in same Node.js process with isolated context via AsyncLocalStorage. Share API client and MCP connections. File-based mailbox IPC. Killed via AbortController. Enables swarms without tmux dependency.
items.done: Swarm agents spawn in-process when tmux unavailable; share API/MCP connections; isolated via AsyncLocalStorage; mailbox IPC works same as tmux backend.

items.title: SSRF guard for HTTP hooks — block private/cloud-metadata IPs
items.summary: HTTP hooks resolve target IPs before connecting and block private ranges (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x), CGNAT (100.64.x), cloud metadata (169.254.x), IPv6 unique-local (fc00::/7, fe80::/10). Loopback (127.x, ::1) explicitly ALLOWED for local dev policy servers. Bypassed when proxy in use.
items.done: HTTP hook to 169.254.169.254 is blocked; hook to 127.0.0.1 is allowed; private IPs blocked; DNS resolution happens before connection attempt.

items.title: FileChanged + CwdChanged hook events — file watcher triggers
items.summary: Chokidar file watcher fires FileChanged hooks when project files change and CwdChanged hooks when the working directory changes. CwdChanged also clears cached env files. Watcher only starts when hooks are configured for these events.
items.done: Writing a file triggers FileChanged hook if configured; changing cwd triggers CwdChanged + env cache clear; no watcher overhead when hooks not configured.

items.title: Auto skill improvement — periodic LLM review of invoked skills from session turns
items.summary: Post-sampling hook fires every N turns to review which skills were invoked, compares against recent messages with a small/fast model, and generates targeted skill updates (section, change, reason). Improves skills organically from actual usage patterns without disrupting the main flow.
items.done: After N turns with skill invocations, skill content is automatically refined; updates scoped to specific sections; uses a small/fast model to minimize cost; non-blocking.

items.title: Teammate color coding — unique color per agent in swarm UI
items.summary: Each spawned teammate is assigned a unique color from the AGENT_COLORS palette in round-robin order. Colors persist per session and are displayed in the TUI teammate tree, footer pills, and @-completion suggestions to distinguish agents at a glance.
items.done: Each teammate shows with a distinct color in the TUI; same agent keeps same color throughout session; colors cycle across the palette for large swarms.

items.title: Teammate default model — Opus 4.6 for swarm agents, provider-aware
items.summary: When the user hasn't set `teammateDefaultModel` in /config, spawned swarm agents use Opus 4.6 as the default model. Provider-aware: Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry users get the correct model ID for their provider.
items.done: Spawned teammates default to Opus 4.6; /config teammateDefaultModel overrides it; Bedrock users get the Bedrock Opus 4.6 model ID, not the direct API one.

items.title: MCP server reconnect + enable/disable toggle in live session
items.summary: useMcpReconnect() and useMcpToggleEnabled() hooks allow reconnecting a failed MCP server or toggling it enabled/disabled without restarting the session. Accessible from the MCP settings panel in the TUI.
items.done: Failed MCP server shows a Reconnect button; toggling a server off stops its tools from appearing; toggling back on reconnects; no session restart required.

items.title: /security-review — built-in security audit of current branch changes
items.summary: Bundled /security-review prompt skill: runs git status/log/diff, then uses an LLM to perform a focused security review of the pending changes. Scoped to git-diff tools only. Defined as an inline markdown skill with frontmatter allowed-tools.
items.done: Running /security-review audits the current branch diff for security issues; output is structured finding report; only git-read tools permitted during review.

items.title: /init-verifiers — create project-specific verifier skills
items.summary: Bundled /init-verifiers command: analyzes the project and creates one or more verifier skill files that the Verify agent can use to automatically check code changes. Supports multiple verifiers for different subsystems (API + web UI). Progress tracked via TodoWrite.
items.done: Running /init-verifiers creates appropriate verifier skill files; multiple verifiers generated for multi-subsystem projects; output tracked with todos.

items.title: /ultraplan — launch multi-agent CCR planning session from keyword or command
items.summary: The /ultraplan command spawns a remote CCR agent task for multi-agent parallel planning. Polls for approval/exit-plan-mode signal, archives sessions, handles teleport. Triggered by keyword detection or direct slash command. Requires Claude.ai OAuth and remote environment.
items.done: Running /ultraplan (or typing 'ultraplan' in a prompt) starts a remote planning session; leader waits for plan approval; approved plan returned to local session.

items.title: Fast mode — Opus 4.6 with rate-limit cooldown circuit breaker
items.summary: Fast mode (`shift+tab` toggle) runs Opus 4.6 at higher throughput. Has a cooldown circuit breaker: when rate-limited or overloaded, fast mode enters cooldown (onCooldownTriggered/onCooldownExpired signals). handleFastModeRejectedByAPI disables fast mode if the API permanently rejects it. Notified in TUI when unavailable.
items.done: Shift+tab cycles through permission modes including fast mode; fast mode uses Opus 4.6; rate-limit triggers cooldown with countdown notification; API rejection disables fast mode for the session.

items.title: Cyber risk instruction — system prompt section for security task safety
items.summary: A carefully crafted system prompt section (owned by Safeguards team) injected when handling security-related requests. Defines the boundary between acceptable defensive security assistance (pentesting, CTF) and harmful offensive activities. Cannot be modified without team review.
items.done: Security requests get the cyber risk instruction in context; instruction guides appropriate vs. inappropriate security assistance; unchanged from upstream.

items.title: Tool result disk storage — persist large outputs (>50K chars) to disk
items.summary: Tool results exceeding 50K characters (DEFAULT_MAX_RESULT_SIZE_CHARS) are persisted to disk; the model receives a preview and file path reference instead of the full content. Individual tools can declare a lower maxResultSizeChars. Prevents context window explosion from large tool outputs.
items.done: Shell commands producing >50K output store to disk; model gets preview + path; subsequent Read can retrieve the full content; per-tool limit respected.

items.title: Custom spinner verbs — user-configurable loading message verbs
items.summary: Spinner loading messages use verbs from a configurable list (default: 'Accomplishing', 'Actioning', 'Actualizing', etc.). Users can replace the list entirely or extend it via `spinnerVerbs` setting with `mode: 'replace'` or `mode: 'extend'`. Empty replace uses built-in list as fallback.
items.done: Setting spinnerVerbs.mode='replace' uses only user verbs; mode='extend' adds to built-ins; empty replace falls back safely.

items.title: Task budgets — per-task token allocation via beta header
items.summary: Beta header `task-budgets-2026-03-13` enables per-task token budget allocation. Lets the model allocate a token budget to specific sub-tasks or agents, preventing runaway token usage in complex multi-step operations.
items.done: Task budget headers sent with API requests; sub-agents respect their token budget; budget exhaustion returns a structured signal; main session unaffected.

items.title: Redact thinking beta — server-side redaction of thinking blocks
items.summary: Beta header `redact-thinking-2026-02-12` enables server-side redaction of thinking blocks. Thinking content is processed server-side but not returned to the client — the model can think without exposing the thinking output.
items.done: With redact-thinking beta, thinking blocks are not sent back to client; model still benefits from thinking; TUI does not show thinking content.

items.title: LocalWorkflowTask — workflow script task type (WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS gate)
items.summary: LocalWorkflowTask is a task type that runs a workflow script (multi-agent orchestration). Gated behind the WORKFLOW_SCRIPTS feature flag. Enables the Workflow tool to spawn structured multi-step agent workflows tracked in the task list.
items.done: Running a workflow script creates a LocalWorkflowTask visible in the task list; progress tracked; workflow completion shows results.

items.title: MonitorMcpTask — background MCP monitoring task (MONITOR_TOOL gate)
items.summary: MonitorMcpTask is a background task type for monitoring MCP server output. Gated behind MONITOR_TOOL feature flag. Enables the Monitor tool to run background MCP watchers that fire callbacks when conditions are met.
items.done: A Monitor tool call creates a MonitorMcpTask running in the background; task visible in footer; fires callback on condition; cancellable.

items.title: MDM enterprise settings — OS-level policy enforcement (macOS/Windows/Linux)
items.summary: Reads enterprise policies from OS-level MDM: macOS (com.anthropic.claudecode plist, /Library/Managed Preferences/), Windows (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Policies\ClaudeCode admin-only + HKCU user-writable), Linux (/etc/claude-code/managed-settings.json). Priority: remote → HKLM → managed-settings.json → HKCU. Blocks user overrides of managed settings.
items.done: Enterprise admins push policies via MDM; CC reads them at startup; managed settings override user settings; /doctor shows policy source.

items.title: Shell output size limits — 30K default, 150K cap, BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH override
items.summary: Shell tool outputs are capped at 30K chars by default (BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_DEFAULT), with a hard upper limit of 150K (BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_UPPER_LIMIT). Override via BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH env var (clamped to valid range). Prevents large outputs from flooding context.
items.done: Commands producing >30K output are truncated with a message; BASH_MAX_OUTPUT_LENGTH=50000 expands the limit; values above 150K are clamped.

items.title: Read-only command validation maps — git/gh/shell safe-flag allowlists
items.summary: Comprehensive allowlist maps for read-only validation: GIT_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS (all git subcommands + safe flags + danger callbacks), GH_READ_ONLY_COMMANDS (gh CLI read-only ops), EXTERNAL_READONLY_COMMANDS (cross-shell). containsVulnerableUncPath detects Windows UNC paths that can leak credentials.
items.done: In read-only mode, only allowlisted git/gh commands pass; any unsafe flags rejected; UNC paths blocked; PowerShell and bash share the same validation logic.

items.title: File history snapshots — hardlink-based file state capture before edits
items.summary: Before file edits, CC takes file history snapshots using hardlinks (cheap copy). Snapshots record the file state at each turn. Used by the /rewind command to restore files to a prior state, and by the IDE extension to show diffs. Notifies VS Code via MCP when files change.
items.done: Editing a file records a snapshot; /rewind restores the file to a prior turn's state; snapshot is a hardlink so cost is minimal; VS Code is notified of changes.

items.title: Settings live reload — detect and apply settings changes without restart
items.summary: changeDetector watches settings.json for changes (via file hash); applySettingsChange applies them live to the running session. internalWrites.ts marks writes made by CC itself to avoid false-positive change notifications. Settings panel shows changes in real time.
items.done: Editing settings.json in a text editor reflects in the TUI within seconds; CC-initiated writes (e.g. /config) don't trigger false 'settings changed' notifications.

items.title: Cross-project session resume — resume sessions from different projects
items.summary: When resuming a session that was created in a different project root, CC detects the mismatch via checkCrossProjectResume and warns the user. The session can still resume in the current project context. Prevents silent context confusion.
items.done: Resuming a session from /project-a while in /project-b shows a warning; user can proceed or cancel; file paths in the session are noted as potentially wrong.

items.title: Plugin-only customization policy — lock skills/hooks/styles to plugins only
items.summary: Enterprise policy `strictPluginOnlyCustomization` locks customization surfaces (skills, output styles, hooks, etc.) to plugin-only sources. User-level (~/.claude/*) and project-level (.claude/*) sources are skipped for locked surfaces. Pairs with CC-MDM-SETTINGS.
items.done: With strictPluginOnlyCustomization set, user-defined skills are ignored; only plugin-provided skills load; managed sources always load regardless.

items.title: Apple Terminal backup/restore — recover from interrupted sessions
items.summary: On startup, CC checks for an Apple Terminal backup that may have been interrupted. If found, offers to restore the previous terminal state. Prevents data loss from unexpected Terminal window closure.
items.done: After a crash or unexpected close, reopening CC offers to restore the previous session; backup is cleaned after successful restore.

items.title: Bash security blocks — process substitution, Zsh expansions, heredoc injection
items.summary: BashTool blocks dangerous shell patterns: process substitution `<()` `>()` `$()` `${}`, Zsh equals expansion `=cmd` (bypasses deny rules), heredoc in substitution, malformed token detection, PowerShell comment syntax. Each pattern has a specific error message. Defense-in-depth on top of the permission system.
items.done: Commands using `$(cmd)` are blocked with a specific error; `=curl` is blocked explaining Zsh equals expansion; all patterns surface the reason they're blocked.

items.title: Sed in-place edit rendering — show sed -i commands as file diffs
items.summary: When the agent runs `sed -i 's/pattern/replacement/flags' file`, CC parses the sed substitution and renders it as a file-edit diff in the permission dialog. Shows exactly what text will change instead of the raw command. Supports BRE→ERE regex conversion, extended regex flags.
items.done: Permission dialog for `sed -i` shows a diff preview of the file change; regex pattern and replacement displayed clearly; non-sed commands show the raw command.

items.title: Destructive bash command warnings — informational notes in permission dialog
items.summary: Permission dialogs for commands like `git reset --hard`, `git push --force`, `git clean -f`, `git checkout .` show an informational warning ('Note: may discard uncommitted changes'). Purely informational — doesn't affect permission logic or auto-approval. Curated list of dangerous git patterns.
items.done: Running `git reset --hard` shows the warning note in the dialog; `git push -f` shows the force-push warning; non-destructive commands show no extra note.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. tools/destructive-warn.ts: pure destructiveWarning(command) → curated note for allowed-but-destructive git commands (reset --hard, push --force [but NOT --force-with-lease], clean -f, checkout . / -f, restore ., branch -D, stash drop/clear), else null. shell-cmd.ts prepends "⚠ <note>" to the result (success or failure) so the user/agent sees it in the transcript. Purely informational — never blocks (rm -rf etc. stay hard-refused by the DESTRUCTIVE gate). +7 tests; suite green, tsc clean.

items.title: Fork subagent — implicit context inheritance when subagent_type omitted
items.summary: When the Agent tool is called without `subagent_type`, it forks the current conversation: the child inherits the parent's full context and system prompt. All spawns run async for unified task-notification model. `/fork <directive>` slash command. Mutually exclusive with coordinator mode.
items.done: Calling Agent without subagent_type spawns a fork with full parent context; `/fork fix the lint errors` creates a focused fork; task-notification pattern used for all async agents.

items.title: Skill usage tracking — 7-day half-life ranking by usage count + recency
items.summary: Records usageCount and lastUsedAt per skill invocation (debounced 60s to avoid write spam). Persisted in globalConfig. Ranking uses a 7-day half-life decay. Skills used more recently and more often rank higher in the skill picker and @-completions.
items.done: Frequently-used skills appear first in completions; recently-used skills surface above rarely-used ones; usage decays over time (7-day half-life).

items.title: Plugin dependency resolution — apt-style presence guarantees with cycle detection
items.summary: Plugins can declare dependencies on other plugins. Resolution: DFS walk at install time with cycle detection; load-time `verifyAndDemote` demotes plugins with unsatisfied deps (session-local, no settings write). Cross-marketplace deps blocked. Bare dep names inherit declaring plugin's marketplace.
items.done: Installing plugin A with dep on B installs B first; circular deps rejected at install time; if B is removed, A is demoted (not errored) at load time.

items.title: /remote-setup — BYOC container setup with GitHub OAuth token
items.summary: The /remote-setup command configures a Bring-Your-Own-Container (BYOC) remote environment. Uses `ccr-byoc-2025-07-29` beta header. Wraps a GitHub token with redaction protection. Fetches available environments from the CCR API. Claude.ai subscriber only. Feature flag: `BYOC_ENVIRONMENT_RUNNER`.
items.done: Running /remote-setup guides through container configuration; GitHub token stored securely; environments listed from CCR API; BYOC sessions usable via /teleport.

items.title: /context — show context window usage breakdown with microcompact option
items.summary: The /context command analyzes and displays context window usage breakdown (analyzeContextUsage): how many tokens are used by system prompt, conversation, tool results, etc. Optionally runs microcompact to clear old tool results and show the savings.
items.done: Running /context shows current context breakdown by category; shows % of window used; optional compact button clears old results; savings displayed.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. Replaced the untested inline /context handler with repl/context-cmd.ts: pure contextBreakdown(messages, window) → per-category token estimates (system/user/assistant[+tool-call args]/tool results) + total + pct, and formatContextBreakdown() rendering a usage bar + each category's token count and % share. Richer than the old role-count version AND now unit-tested; also shrinks handlers.ts (inline handler removed, registered via import). +5 tests; full suite 1985 green, tsc clean.

items.title: /passes — view Claude usage passes and referral reward balance
items.summary: The /passes command shows available usage passes and referral rewards (checkCachedPassesEligibility, getCachedReferrerReward). Passes are additional usage credits beyond the plan limit. Claude.ai specific feature.
items.done: Running /passes shows current pass balance and available referral rewards; eligible users see how to redeem; not available for API-key-only users.

items.title: /code-review ultra — quota-tracked cloud deep review with overage detection
items.summary: Ultrareview is a cloud-based multi-agent code review. CC fetches the quota (reviews_used, reviews_limit, reviews_remaining, is_overage) before starting. Server-side quota consumed at session creation. Shows nudge when approaching limit. Claude.ai subscribers only.
items.done: Running /code-review ultra checks quota first; remaining count shown; overage warning when limit approached; non-subscribers see a 'subscribe' prompt.

items.title: Commit attribution — track CC-modified files for git co-authorship
items.summary: CC tracks which files it modifies across a session using content hashes and session IDs. Attribution snapshots feed into git commit co-authorship metadata. Excludes generated files. Tracks git remote URL for cross-repo attribution. Used to attribute changes to the AI assistant in commit messages.
items.done: Files edited by CC tracked with hashes; git commits include proper Co-Authored-By attribution; generated files excluded; remote URL included in attribution metadata.

items.title: Concurrent session management — track multiple running CC instances
items.summary: CC tracks all running instances (session ID → PID → timestamp). On startup, dead sessions are cleaned up. Session switching triggers onSessionSwitch callbacks. Prevents stale session locks. Cross-platform process health check.
items.done: Multiple CC instances run without conflicts; dead session entries cleaned on startup; isProcessRunning checks prevent orphaned state; session IDs unique.

items.title: Agentic session search — LLM-powered semantic search across past sessions
items.summary: The /resume picker uses agenticSessionSearch to find relevant sessions by content, not just title. Scans up to 2000 chars of transcript per session (max 100 sessions), then side-queries a small/fast model to rank sessions by relevance to the search query.
items.done: Typing a search term in the resume picker finds sessions by content (not just name); LLM ranks results by relevance; limited to 100 sessions to stay fast.

items.title: Bash comment-as-label — first # line becomes tool-use display label
items.summary: CC extracts the first `# comment` line from a bash command and uses it as the tool-use display label in fullscreen mode, and as the collapse-group hint. Users can annotate scripts with human-readable descriptions that appear in the UI instead of the raw command.
items.done: First `# comment` in a bash command block renders as the display label in tool call UI; raw command still executes.
items.notes: bashLabel() in tool-display.ts; shell_cmd uses first # comment as display label, falls back to truncated command.

items.title: Bash per-command exit code semantics — grep exit 1 = no-match, not error
items.summary: CC maps per-command exit code semantics: grep/rg exit 1 = 'No matches found' (not an error), find exit 1 = partial success, diff exit 1 = 'differences found'. Without this, these commands falsely appear as failures and the agent may retry unnecessarily.
items.done: grep/rg returning exit 1 shows 'No matches found' as info, not error. diff exit 1 shows 'Differences found'. find exit 1 shows 'Some directories were inaccessible'.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. tools/shell-cmd.ts: pure lastCommandWord() (first token of the last pipeline/chain segment, git-aware, basename-stripped) + classifyExitCode() — grep/rg/egrep/fgrep exit 1 → 'No matches found', diff exit 1 → 'Differences found', find exit 1 → 'Some paths were inaccessible', all ok=true; exit ≥2 and unrelated commands stay errors; ENOENT/timeout (non-numeric code) stay errors. Returns the info note as the result so the TUI renders it as a normal (non-error) outcome — no display-layer change needed. +11 tests in tools.test.ts; suite green, tsc clean.

items.title: Bash image output — base64 data URI in stdout renders as inline image
items.summary: CC detects when bash stdout contains a base64-encoded `data:image/...;base64,...` URI and renders it as an inline image in the terminal output, rather than printing the raw base64 string. Enables commands like screenshot tools, image-generating scripts, etc. to show their output visually.
items.done: A bash command whose stdout is a data URI image renders the image inline in terminal output.

items.title: AI-powered permission explanation — risk level + reasoning for tool calls
items.summary: When a tool call requires permission, CC fires a side query (sideQuery) to an LLM that analyzes the command and returns a structured explanation: risk level (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH), plain-English explanation, reasoning, and risk summary. This explanation shows in the permission dialog to help the user make informed decisions.
items.done: Permission dialogs show a risk-level badge (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) with one-sentence AI explanation of what the command does and why it's being run.

items.title: Rate limit utilization display — 5hr/7day windows, per-model, extra credits
items.summary: CC fetches and displays rate-limit utilization info: 5-hour window, 7-day window, per-model limits (opus, sonnet), OAuth-app-specific limits, and extra_usage credit balance (monthly limit, used credits, utilization %). Used to show users how close they are to limits before hitting them.
items.done: Session displays current utilization percentage and reset timestamp for the active rate-limit windows.

items.title: Accept Edits permission mode — auto-approve filesystem ops without prompt
items.summary: CC has an 'acceptEdits' permission mode (distinct from bypassPermissions) that auto-allows a specific subset of filesystem commands (mkdir, touch, rm, rmdir, mv, cp, sed) without prompting, while still prompting for bash commands outside that set. Cycling through modes with Shift+Tab offers: default → acceptEdits → plan → default.
items.done: Permission mode cycle includes acceptEdits. In acceptEdits mode, filesystem commands (mkdir/touch/rm/mv/cp/sed) execute without prompt; other shell commands still prompt.

items.title: Conditional skill activation on file edit — skills auto-load for matched paths
items.summary: When FileEditTool writes a file, CC calls `activateConditionalSkillsForPaths` and `discoverSkillDirsForPaths` to automatically activate any skills that declare a path match for the edited file. Skills can conditionally register themselves when specific files are touched, enabling project-aware tool sets.
items.done: Editing a file in a directory with a matching skill definition triggers that skill to activate for subsequent calls in the session.

items.title: Team memory secret scanner — block file edits that introduce secrets to team memory
items.summary: When FileEditTool writes a file, CC calls `checkTeamMemSecrets` to scan the edit for secrets before syncing to team memory. Prevents accidental secret propagation (API keys, tokens) to shared team memory storage.
items.done: File edits that contain secret patterns (API keys, tokens) are flagged or blocked from team memory sync; warning shown in tool output.

items.title: Admin request flow — limit increase / seat upgrade for team users
items.summary: CC has a `createAdminRequest` API for team/enterprise users without billing access to request a limit increase or seat upgrade from their org admin. The request is queued server-side; admins can approve or dismiss it. Used in the upsell flow when a user hits a limit.
items.done: Users on team plans who hit rate limits can submit a limit-increase request. Admins see pending requests and can approve/dismiss.

items.title: Overage credit grant — one-click extra credits when hitting rate limit
items.summary: CC fetches overage credit grant eligibility (available, eligible, granted, amount, currency) and allows users to claim extra credits when approaching rate limits. Backend resolves tier-specific amounts. The CLI reads eligibility state and shows a claim prompt.
items.done: When a user hits rate limits, a 'Claim N credits' option appears if eligible; claiming updates the grant state and extends usage.

items.title: Bootstrap API — server-side additional model options list
items.summary: CC's bootstrap API fetch returns `additional_model_options` — a server-configurable list of extra model choices (value, label, description) that are added to the model picker beyond the hard-coded defaults. Allows Anthropic to ship new models to CLI users without a code update.
items.done: Model picker includes any server-returned additional_model_options from the bootstrap API, alongside hard-coded defaults.

items.title: Cowork mode — separate plugin directory for teammate/collaborative sessions
items.summary: CC supports a `--cowork` CLI flag (and `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_COWORK_PLUGINS` env var) that switches the plugin directory from `plugins/` to `cowork_plugins/`. Used in teammate/collaborative multi-user sessions where a different skill/agent set is appropriate. Integrates with the Teammate feature set.
items.done: `--cowork` flag loads skills and agents from `cowork_plugins/` directory instead of `plugins/`. Teammate sessions default to cowork plugin context.

items.title: Shell tab-completion in bash input — command, variable, and file suggestions
items.summary: CC's bash input supports real-time shell tab-completion: typing a partial command shows up to 15 completions from the shell (command names, shell variable names, or file paths). The completion type is inferred from the prefix: `$` = variable, `/`/`~`/`.` = file, otherwise command. A 1-second timeout prevents UI blocking.
items.done: Pressing Tab on a partial bash command shows a completion dropdown; results adapt per prefix type (command/file/variable).

items.title: Fig/withfig autocomplete spec loading — command argument security validation
items.summary: CC loads @withfig/autocomplete specs for 1000s of CLI commands. These are used to structurally validate command arguments for security (extracting safe command prefixes, identifying dangerous flags), not just for UI completion. The registry has a built-in specs list plus dynamic Fig spec loading as a fallback, with LRU memoization.
items.done: Permission system uses structured command specs to validate args; `git`, `docker`, `npm`, and other common commands have their flags validated against known-safe lists.

items.title: /release-notes — view in-session release notes for current version
items.summary: CC has a `/release-notes` command that loads and displays formatted release notes for the current CLI version within the session. Supports non-interactive mode, so it can be piped/scripted. Keeps users informed about new features and changes without leaving the terminal.
items.done: `/release-notes` displays formatted changelog for the installed version. Works in both interactive and non-interactive mode.

items.title: /terminal-setup — install Shift+Enter keybinding for terminal newlines
items.summary: CC has a `/terminal-setup` command that configures the user's terminal to support Shift+Enter for newlines in the input composer. It detects the terminal emulator (Ghostty, Kitty, iTerm2, WezTerm, Apple Terminal, others) and installs the appropriate keybinding. The command is hidden for terminals with native CSI-u/Kitty keyboard protocol support.
items.done: `/terminal-setup` detects terminal emulator and configures Shift+Enter (or Option+Enter on Apple Terminal) for multi-line input. Shows instructions for unsupported terminals.

items.title: /color — set prompt bar accent color for current session
items.summary: CC has a `/color <color|default>` command that changes the prompt bar's accent color for the current session. Useful for distinguishing multiple concurrent sessions by color. Executes immediately (`immediate: true`) without waiting for a model response.
items.done: `/color red` changes the input bar accent color; `/color default` resets it. Persists for the session lifetime.

items.title: /stats — usage statistics and activity dashboard in-session
items.summary: CC has a `/stats` command that shows usage statistics and activity data for the current user within the session. Distinct from the per-turn cost footer — this provides aggregate metrics (total sessions, turns, tools used, tokens spent) with a visual summary.
items.done: `/stats` opens a usage stats view showing session/turn/cost aggregates for the account.

items.title: /extra-usage — configure overage provisioning to keep working past rate limits
items.summary: CC has an `/extra-usage` command (both interactive and non-interactive variants) that allows users to configure 'extra usage' — additional paid capacity beyond the subscription limit. Only shown when `isOverageProvisioningAllowed()`. In non-interactive mode, supports scripted configuration (e.g., `claude /extra-usage enable`).
items.done: `/extra-usage` shows current overage configuration and allows toggling additional usage credits; gated on subscription eligibility.

items.title: /privacy-settings — view and update data collection privacy preferences
items.summary: CC has a `/privacy-settings` command (consumer subscribers only) for viewing and updating privacy preferences around data collection, conversation storage, and product feedback settings. Surfaced in the account settings flow.
items.done: `/privacy-settings` opens privacy controls. User can opt out of conversation storage or feedback data collection.

items.title: /upgrade — in-session subscription upgrade to Max plan
items.summary: CC has a `/upgrade` command (claude.ai only, non-enterprise) that presents the subscription upgrade flow — 'Upgrade to Max for higher rate limits and more Opus'. Used as the CTA when users hit rate limits on free/Pro plans. Shown inline without leaving the CLI.
items.done: `/upgrade` shows the Max subscription benefits and initiates the purchase flow via the web.

items.title: /thinkback — year-in-review animation for annual usage summary
items.summary: CC has a `/thinkback` command (feature-gated: `tengu_thinkback`) that generates and plays a '2025 Claude Code Year in Review' animation summarizing the user's annual coding activity. Uses a two-step flow: a generation skill followed by a hidden `/thinkback-play` command that renders the animation.
items.done: `/thinkback` generates and plays a personalized year-in-review animation from session history.

items.title: /voice — voice input mode toggle for hands-free use
items.summary: CC has a `/voice` command (claude.ai only, GrowthBook-gated) that toggles voice input mode — allowing hands-free, speech-to-text interaction. Distinct from Vanta's text-based voice notifications. Requires a live microphone and the voice mode feature flag.
items.done: `/voice` toggles microphone input. Spoken words are transcribed and submitted as prompts.

items.title: /web-setup — connect GitHub account and configure remote web sessions
items.summary: CC has a `/web-setup` command (feature-gated: `tengu_cobalt_lantern`) that guides users through setting up Claude Code to run in the cloud via a web browser, connecting their GitHub account and provisioning a remote container environment.
items.done: `/web-setup` guides through GitHub OAuth + remote environment provisioning; session continues in the browser.

items.title: /remote-env — configure default remote environment for teleport sessions
items.summary: CC has a `/remote-env` command (claude.ai + `allow_remote_sessions` policy) that lets users configure the default remote compute environment used when teleporting a session to the cloud. Integrates with the teleport infrastructure for selecting region, instance type, and environment.
items.done: `/remote-env` shows available remote environments and persists the default selection for teleport sessions.

items.title: NVIDIA NIM provider — cheap worker routing for summarize/classify/code
items.summary: Add an NVIDIA NIM provider adapter (OpenAI-compatible API at build.nvidia.com) so Vanta can route low-cost subtasks (summarize, classify, first-pass code) to NVIDIA-hosted models like meta/llama-3.1-8b-instruct. Uses NVIDIA_API_KEY + VANTA_ROUTE_SUMMARIZE=nvidia:model config. Since NIM is OpenAI-compatible, implementation is a baseURL swap in the OpenAI adapter — minimal new code.
items.done: VANTA_PROVIDER=nvidia with NVIDIA_API_KEY resolves the OpenAI adapter to build.nvidia.com. /routes shows nvidia as a configured route. A summarize task auto-delegates to the configured NIM model.
items.notes: Provider adapter + /routes command done. Auto-delegation (VANTA_ROUTE_SUMMARIZE) parked — needs routing infra, scope exceeds S/low-effort.

items.title: iTerm2 swarm backend — multi-pane swarm via iTerm2 Python API
items.summary: CC supports spawning teammate swarms as iTerm2 panes using the iTerm2 Python API (`it2`). The setup flow guides users through detecting their Python package manager (pip/uv/pipx), installing the `iterm2` package, verifying the API connection, and preferring tmux if it's available. Each teammate runs in its own iTerm2 pane with a named split layout.
items.done: Spawning a 3-agent swarm creates 3 iTerm2 panes each running a separate Claude Code instance, coordinated by the leader. `/swarm iterm2` prompts for setup if it2 is not configured.

items.title: Swarm synchronized permissions — workers forward permission requests to leader UI
items.summary: When a worker agent in a swarm needs a permission prompt, CC's `permissionSync` module forwards the request to the team leader's UI via the mailbox system, rather than requiring a separate terminal. The leader user approves/denies, and the response is sent back to the worker. Sandbox permission requests are also forwarded. Workers poll their mailbox for responses.
items.done: A worker agent needing a shell permission shows the prompt in the leader's UI. Approval/denial propagates back to the worker via mailbox.

items.title: Org policy limits — admin-configurable feature restrictions via remote API
items.summary: CC fetches organization-level policy restrictions from an API for Team/Enterprise subscribers. These policy limits can disable specific CLI features (e.g., feedback submission, remote sessions, product improvements) at the org level. Uses ETag-based caching, background polling (1hr interval), fail-open on fetch failure, and persists the last-known policy to disk.
items.done: `isPolicyAllowed('allow_remote_sessions')` returns false when org admin has disabled it. Admin can set policy from the org settings UI. CLI features check policy before activating.

items.title: Diagnostic baseline tracking — surface only new LSP errors after file edits
items.summary: CC's `DiagnosticTrackingService` captures a baseline of IDE diagnostics before each file edit, then computes a delta after the write. Only errors and warnings that appeared after the edit are surfaced to the agent, preventing false noise from pre-existing issues. Tracks per-file diagnostic state with timestamps for right-file (cursor-context) diagnostics.
items.done: After writing a file, the agent sees only diagnostics introduced by that edit (new errors/warnings). Pre-existing issues are filtered as baseline.

items.title: Swarm idle notification — teammate sends idle state to leader on Stop
items.summary: When a teammate agent's session stops, CC's `teammateInit` module fires a Stop hook that writes an idle notification to the team leader's mailbox. The leader polls the mailbox and updates the teammate's status in the team UI (showing it as idle rather than running). Includes the last peer-DM summary for context.
items.done: When a worker completes its task, the leader's team view shows the worker as 'idle'. The leader can assign new work or clean up the team.

items.title: LSP async diagnostic push — publishDiagnostics notifications as conversation attachments
items.summary: CC's LSP layer registers a `publishDiagnostics` notification handler. When LSP servers push diagnostics asynchronously (not just on request), they're stored in an LRU-based registry and delivered to the conversation as attachments automatically. The agent doesn't need to explicitly poll for errors — they appear as context when LSP pushes them.
items.done: After a file write, new LSP errors pushed via publishDiagnostics appear in the next turn as an attachment, without the agent having to call an LSP tool.

items.title: Official MCP registry — prefetch Anthropic's server registry for trust lookups
items.summary: CC fire-and-forget prefetches the official Anthropic MCP registry at startup. This populates a URL set used to identify whether a connected MCP server is 'official' (from Anthropic's registry) vs. third-party. Used to show trust indicators in the MCP connection UI and to gate certain behaviors on official servers.
items.done: MCP connection list shows an 'official' badge for servers from `api.anthropic.com/mcp-registry`. Trust lookup is instant (in-memory Set after prefetch).

items.title: AskUserQuestion tool — structured multi-question UI with options, previews, multi-select
items.summary: CC's AskUserQuestion tool lets the model present 1-4 structured questions in one call, each with 2-4 labeled options (plus auto-added 'Other'), optional per-option preview content (markdown in a side panel), optional multi-select, and a short header chip. The user's selections plus free-text notes are returned as structured annotations. Richer than a plain clarify tool — preview content enables side-by-side comparisons of code snippets or mockups.
items.done: Model can call AskUserQuestion with up to 4 questions. Each question shows options in a tappable list; options with `preview` show a preview pane. User can select + add notes. Returns structured answers.

items.title: McpAuthTool — pseudo-tool that starts OAuth flow for unauthenticated MCP servers
items.summary: When an MCP server requires OAuth but hasn't been authenticated, CC surfaces a pseudo-tool (McpAuthTool) in place of the server's real tools. The model can call this to start the OAuth flow — it returns an auth_url for the user to open, then clears the auth cache and reconnects the server. The user never has to manually restart.
items.done: Calling the mcp_auth pseudo-tool for an unauthenticated server returns an auth URL. After browser auth, re-calling the tool confirms the connection is live and restores the server's real tools.

items.title: StructuredOutput tool — schema-validated JSON output for SDK/non-interactive sessions
items.summary: CC has a SyntheticOutputTool (`StructuredOutput`) enabled only in non-interactive/SDK sessions. When an SDK caller provides a JSON schema, the model is forced to call this tool as its final action, and the output is validated against the schema before returning. This is how workflow agents return structured data instead of free text — and how `agent({schema})` in workflows works.
items.done: SDK sessions with a schema constraint receive a validated JSON object as the agent's return value. The agent calls `StructuredOutput({...})` as its terminal action.

items.title: Durable cron jobs — persist scheduled tasks across session restarts
items.summary: CC's CronCreate tool has a `durable` flag (default false). Durable crons are written to `.claude/scheduled_tasks.json` and survive session restarts — OS-triggered cron invocations can fire them. Non-durable (default) crons are in-memory only and die when the session ends. The `recurring` flag (default true) controls one-shot vs repeating. Vanta's cron is in-memory only.
items.done: `cron_create(durable: true)` persists the job to `.vanta/scheduled_tasks.json`. After a session restart, `cron_list` shows the job and it fires at the next scheduled time.

items.title: SendMessageTool — agent-to-agent messaging within swarms (mailbox-based)
items.summary: CC's SendMessageTool lets agents send messages to named teammates (`to: 'agent-name'`), the team lead (`to: 'leader'`), or all (`to: '*'` broadcast). Messages are written to the recipient's mailbox file. The leader can also initiate shutdown of a worker via a shutdown request/approved/rejected flow. Peers resolve addresses from the team file.
items.done: An agent calls `send_message({to: 'researcher', message: '...'})`. The researcher's next turn includes the message in context. Broadcasts reach all active teammates.

items.title: TeamCreate/TeamDelete tools — spawn and disband named agent teams from within a session
items.summary: CC has TeamCreateTool and TeamDeleteTool: callable agent tools (not just CLI commands) that let the model itself spawn a named team of workers with a description, assign colors, initialize a task list, and register for cleanup. TeamDeleteTool tears down the team and tasks. This is what makes self-directed swarm orchestration possible without human-initiated `/spawn`.
items.done: The model calls `team_create({team_name: 'researchers', description: '...'})` and immediately gets worker IDs. It can then call `agent({})` to hand off tasks. `team_delete` when done.

items.title: Agent hook type — hooks backed by a full multi-turn LLM agent
items.summary: CC supports an 'agent' hook type where each hook event fires a full multi-turn LLM agent (tool-enabled, with access to a restricted toolset). The agent runs in isolation, receives the hook event as JSON input, and returns a structured JSON result via StructuredOutput. Enables complex conditional logic in hooks — e.g. 'analyze this command with a reasoning model before allowing it'.
items.done: A hook configured with `type: agent` fires a full query per event. The agent can call tools, reason across turns, and return a structured block/allow verdict.

items.title: Prompt hook type — hooks backed by a single LLM query
items.summary: CC supports a 'prompt' hook type: each hook event fires a single non-streaming LLM query (small/fast model). The hook receives the event JSON as `$ARGUMENTS` substituted into its prompt, and returns a structured `{decision, reason}` response. Lighter-weight than agent hooks — useful for classification/labeling tasks without multi-turn logic.
items.done: A hook configured with `type: prompt` fires one LLM call per event. Returns a structured `{decision: 'block'|'allow', reason}` that determines hook behavior.

items.title: Frontmatter hooks — agents and skills declare hooks in YAML frontmatter
items.summary: CC agents and skills can declare hook configurations in their YAML frontmatter (`hooks:` key). These hooks are automatically registered when the agent/skill activates and deregistered when it exits. Sub-agent Stop hooks are converted to SubagentStop events. Session hooks share the same lifecycle as the agent.
items.done: A skill with a `hooks.PreToolUse` frontmatter entry runs that hook for every tool call while the skill is active. The hook is removed when the skill session ends.

items.title: Once-only hooks — `once: true` hooks auto-deregister after first fire
items.summary: CC skill hooks support a `once: true` flag that causes the hook to automatically deregister itself after its first successful execution. Useful for one-shot setup hooks (e.g. 'on the first tool call after this skill activates, do X') without polluting subsequent tool calls with unnecessary hook processing.
items.done: A skill hook with `once: true` fires exactly once, then disappears from the hook registry. Subsequent tool calls in the same session don't trigger it.

items.title: Hook exit code semantics — exit 0 silent, exit 2 blocks + shows model, other shows user
items.summary: CC defines specific semantics for hook exit codes: exit 0 = success (stdout/stderr not shown), exit 2 = show stderr to model AND block the tool call (the model sees the message and can change strategy), any other non-zero = show stderr to user only but continue with the tool call. This gives hook authors precise control over whether the model or user sees the hook's output.
items.done: A PreToolUse hook returning exit 2 with a message causes the tool call to be blocked and the model receives the hook's stderr as a constraint.

items.title: Away summary — auto-generate context recap when terminal regains focus after 5+ min
items.summary: CC detects terminal focus loss via `subscribeTerminalFocus`. If the terminal has been unfocused for 5+ minutes and no away summary exists since the last user turn, CC auto-generates a `away_summary` system message summarizing recent activity. The model sees this as context when the user returns, reducing the need for manual context re-establishment.
items.done: After 5+ minutes away, returning to the terminal shows an auto-generated 'While you were away...' summary of recent turns injected as a system message.

items.title: CLI auto-updater — version notification + background update with semver comparison
items.summary: CC's AutoUpdater/AutoUpdaterWrapper components check for new CLI versions and notify the user via a bottom-right notification. `useUpdateNotification` tracks the last-notified semver to avoid repeat notifications for the same version. The update is downloaded in the background and the user is prompted to restart.
items.done: When a new version is available, a notification appears in the corner. User can dismiss or restart to apply the update.

items.title: `claude ssh` — remote session over SSH with SSHSessionManager
items.summary: CC supports a `claude ssh` command that creates an SSH-based remote session (sibling to `claude --remote` WebSocket sessions). The SSH child process and auth proxy are created at startup and handed to `useSSHSession`. The hook bridges SSH session messages into the REPL UI using the same SDK message adapters as remote WebSocket sessions.
items.done: `claude ssh user@host` opens an interactive session over SSH. Tool calls execute on the remote host; permission prompts appear locally.

items.title: Plugin recommendation engine — proactive install suggestions for LSP and hint plugins
items.summary: CC has a `usePluginRecommendationBase` shared state machine that powers proactive plugin install recommendations. Sources include: LSP server plugins (for detected file types) and claude-code-hint plugins (from skill frontmatter). The machine handles gates (remote mode, already-showing, in-flight), async resolution, and shows success/failure notifications. Users can install from the suggestion.
items.done: Opening a `.py` file when no Python LSP is installed shows a 'Install pylsp plugin?' suggestion. Accepting installs and activates it.

items.title: Deferred SessionStart hooks — async hook execution without blocking REPL startup
items.summary: CC's `useDeferredHookMessages` runs SessionStart hooks asynchronously so the REPL renders immediately (~500ms savings). Hook messages are stored in a pending promise and merged into the conversation before the first API request (ensuring the model always sees hook context). The deferred pattern avoids REPL startup blocking on slow hooks.
items.done: SessionStart hooks with slow shell commands (~500ms+) don't delay the initial REPL render. Their output appears as the first hook attachment when the user sends their first message.

items.title: Official marketplace auto-install — startup check installs missing official plugins
items.summary: On startup, CC calls `checkAndInstallOfficialMarketplace()` which detects if the official Anthropic marketplace plugin is missing and auto-installs it silently. Success/failure notifications appear in the bottom-right corner. Config save failures show a specific message with remediation instructions.
items.done: First launch after adding an official marketplace entry auto-installs it. A brief notification confirms success or shows an actionable error if config save fails.

items.title: Memory usage monitor — high/critical heap warnings at 1.5GB / 2.5GB
items.summary: CC polls Node.js heap usage every 10 seconds via `useMemoryUsage`. At 1.5GB (high) or 2.5GB (critical) thresholds, a visible warning appears so the user can /compact or restart before hitting an OOM crash. Returns null while normal so there's no UI noise in typical sessions.
items.done: When heap reaches 1.5GB, a warning appears in the status area. At 2.5GB it escalates to critical with a recommendation to /compact.

items.title: OS system notification — desktop alert after long tool runs when terminal is inactive
items.summary: CC sends an OS-level system notification (macOS/Linux/Windows) when a tool response completes and the user hasn't interacted with the terminal for 6+ seconds. Uses `sendNotification` via the system notifier. Also respects terminal focus events via `useTerminalNotification`.
items.done: After a long bash command finishes while the user is in another app, a macOS/OS notification appears. Clicking it focuses the terminal.

items.title: Copy-on-select — auto-copy terminal text selection to clipboard (iTerm2-style)
items.summary: CC's `useCopyOnSelect` mirrors iTerm2's 'Copy to pasteboard on selection': when the user finishes a drag-select or multi-click selection in the terminal, the selected text is automatically copied to clipboard. The selection highlight remains visible. Only active in alt-screen (Ink) mode.
items.done: Dragging to select text in the Vanta TUI automatically copies it to clipboard, matching iTerm2 behavior.

items.title: Clipboard image hint — notification on focus when clipboard has an image
items.summary: When the terminal regains focus and the clipboard contains an image, CC shows a transient notification hinting 'Press /paste to attach image'. The hint has a 30-second cooldown to avoid spam on repeated focus changes. Pairs with the /paste command for clipboard-to-context image workflow.
items.done: After copying a screenshot and switching to the Vanta terminal, a hint appears: 'Image in clipboard — /paste to attach'. Dismisses after 30s.

items.title: Asciicast session recording — record sessions as terminal replay files
items.summary: CC can record sessions to asciicast v2 files (`.cast`) when `CLAUDE_CODE_TERMINAL_RECORDING=1` is set. Files are written buffered to `~/.claude/recordings/<session-id>.cast`, cleaned up on exit. Asciicast format allows replaying the full terminal session in any compatible player (asciinema.org, terminals).
items.done: With `VANTA_TERMINAL_RECORDING=1`, a `.cast` file is written during the session. After exit, the file can be replayed with `asciinema play`.

items.title: Screenshot to clipboard — copy ANSI terminal output as PNG image
items.summary: CC can convert ANSI-colored terminal text to PNG (via a pure-TS bitmap renderer — no WASM or system fonts) and copy it to the clipboard. `copyAnsiToClipboard()` supports macOS (pbcopy), Linux (xclip/xsel), and Windows. Used for sharing terminal output as images.
items.done: A `/screenshot` or export action produces a PNG of the current terminal output and copies it to clipboard.

items.title: AI session title — auto-generate session name via Haiku from conversation content
items.summary: CC uses a dedicated Haiku query to auto-generate a short, descriptive session title from conversation content. `sessionTitle.ts` is the single source of truth shared by: teleport (6-word title + branch name), /rename (kebab-case name), and SDK control requests. Titles are generated asynchronously and don't block the session.
items.done: After the first exchange, the session gets an auto-generated title (e.g. 'fix-auth-token-refresh') visible in /sessions, status bar, and teleport UI.

items.title: JetBrains IDE integration — detect and connect to PyCharm, IntelliJ, WebStorm, etc.
items.summary: CC detects JetBrains IDEs (PyCharm, IntelliJ, WebStorm, PhpStorm, RubyMine, CLion, GoLand, Rider, DataGrip, DataSpell) by scanning known config directories for the `claude-code-jetbrains-plugin`. When detected, CC connects to the IDE for diagnostics, go-to-definition, and file sync — matching the VS Code integration.
items.done: Opening a project in IntelliJ with the CC plugin installed: Vanta detects it and offers the same LSP-style diagnostics and diff features as the VS Code integration.

items.title: PDF reading — extract text from PDFs as context with size/error handling
items.summary: CC can extract text from PDF files as tool context. Handles error cases: empty (no text), too_large (size limits), password_protected, corrupted, unknown. Uses pdftotext/poppler if available, with fallback. Output is persisted to tool-results dir for large PDFs. Integrates with FileReadTool so the model can `read` a PDF path.
items.done: `read_file('report.pdf')` returns extracted text. Password-protected PDFs return a helpful error. Files over the size limit return a partial extract with a note.

items.title: Context suggestions — actionable recommendations when context window fills up
items.summary: CC's `contextSuggestions.ts` generates actionable suggestions when context fills: identifies which tools (bash, read, grep, web-fetch) are consuming tokens, shows estimated savings, and suggests /compact or specific file removal. Surfaced in the ContextVisualization component (CC-CONTEXT-VIZ) with severity levels (info/warning).
items.done: When context is 70%+ full, the context visualization shows specific items to remove with token counts: 'Remove 3 bash outputs (~4k tokens)'.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). repl/context-suggestions.ts: pure contextSuggestions(messages, window) → ContextSuggestion[] — returns [] below 70% fill; at ≥70% groups role:"tool" messages by name and emits "Remove N <name> output(s) (~Xk tokens)" for each ≥1k-token group (savedTokens desc), always appends a /compress entry; severity warning ≥85% else info. Wired into the /context command (context-cmd.ts contextCmd): appends the suggestions (⚠/· prefixed) under the breakdown when context is high. +6 tests; full suite 2032 green, tsc clean.

items.title: In-session transcript search — full-text search across conversation messages
items.summary: CC supports full-text search within the current session transcript. `transcriptSearch.ts` flattens messages to lowercased searchable text (WeakMap-cached for efficiency), filtering out sentinel interruption strings that would cause phantom matches. Used by the search UI (`SearchBox.tsx`, `QuickOpenDialog.tsx`).
items.done: Typing in the transcript search box highlights matching turns. Filtering by query jumps to the first match and shows match count.

items.title: claude-code-hint protocol — subprocess plugin recommendations via stderr tag
items.summary: CLIs and SDKs running under CC can emit a self-closing `<claude-code-hint v='1' type='plugin' name='...' marketplace='...' />` tag to stderr. CC strips it before the model sees it, and surfaces an install prompt to the user. Lets external tools request plugin installs without inference — a documented vendor-facing spec (`docs/claude-code-hints.md`).
items.done: A subprocess emitting `<claude-code-hint type='plugin' name='pylsp' marketplace='agent-skills' />` causes Vanta to show 'Install pylsp plugin?' prompt after the tool call.

items.title: Token budget directives — parse +500k / use 2M tokens from user messages
items.summary: CC parses token budget directives from user messages: shorthand at start/end (`+500k`, `+2M`) and verbose anywhere (`use 500k tokens`, `spend 2M tokens`). Parsed budgets are passed to workflow/agent execution as spending caps. Supports k/M/B multipliers with decimal values.
items.done: User types 'Review all these files +500k' — the session sets a 500,000-token budget ceiling. `budget.total` and `budget.remaining()` are available to orchestration code.

items.title: Plugin auto-update — background marketplace and plugin update check on startup
items.summary: CC calls `autoUpdateMarketplacesAndPluginsInBackground()` during startup housekeeping. Official marketplaces with `autoUpdate: true` are refreshed automatically. Plugin updates are downloaded in the background without blocking the session start.
items.done: On next launch after a plugin update is released, the update is applied silently. A notification appears if a restart is needed.

items.title: Release channels — stable/beta/custom update channel selection
items.summary: CC's auto-updater reads a `ReleaseChannel` setting (stable/beta) stored in global config. The update URL and version check vary by channel. Shell aliases are detected and updated alongside the main binary. Supports `CLAUDE_CODE_RELEASE_CHANNEL` env override.
items.done: Setting `VANTA_RELEASE_CHANNEL=beta` opts into pre-release builds. `/status` shows the active channel. Updates pull from the correct channel endpoint.

items.title: SDK idle timeout — auto-exit SDK sessions after configurable idle delay
items.summary: CC supports `CLAUDE_CODE_EXIT_AFTER_STOP_DELAY=<ms>` for SDK/non-interactive sessions. After the agent stops and the session has been idle for the specified duration, the process exits cleanly. Prevents dangling SDK subprocesses in CI/automation pipelines.
items.done: `VANTA_EXIT_AFTER_STOP_DELAY=5000` causes Vanta to exit 5 seconds after the last tool completes and no new input arrives.

items.title: Standalone agent name/color — custom identity for non-swarm agent sessions
items.summary: CC supports assigning a custom name and color to a standalone agent session (non-swarm). `getStandaloneAgentName()` returns the name only when not in a swarm team. Used by SDK/headless launchers to give sessions a recognizable identity in the UI (e.g. 'researcher', 'code-reviewer') without forming a full team.
items.done: Launching with `--agent-name=reviewer --agent-color=blue` shows the agent badge in the terminal and in task lists.

items.title: Plan mode v2 — multi-agent plan execution (Max=3 agents, enterprise=3)
items.summary: CC's plan mode v2 (`getPlanModeV2AgentCount`) spawns multiple parallel agents during plan execution. Max 20x subscribers and enterprise/team users get 3 concurrent plan agents; others get 1. The count is GrowthBook-configurable and has a `CLAUDE_CODE_PLAN_V2_AGENT_COUNT` env override (1-10). Also includes `getPlanModeV2ExploreAgentCount` (default 3, env override) — dedicated exploration/research agents that run in parallel before main plan execution.
items.done: On a Max plan, entering plan mode and running `/plan` spawns 3 concurrent sub-agents for different plan steps. Progress shows all 3 working in parallel.

items.title: Auto-mode denial history — /permissions tab showing recently blocked commands
items.summary: CC tracks commands recently denied by the auto-mode transcript classifier (up to 20, newest-first) via `recordAutoModeDenial()`. These are surfaced in a 'RecentDenials' tab in the `/permissions` UI, showing tool name, display description, reason, and timestamp — helping users understand and tune what the classifier blocked.
items.done: `/permissions` has a 'Recent Denials' tab. When auto-mode blocks a command, it appears here with the classifier's reason.

items.title: Auto memory extraction — background LLM extraction of facts from session turns
items.summary: CC has a feature-gated (`EXTRACT_MEMORIES`) background service that analyzes session turns after completion and extracts durable facts/preferences into a persistent memory store. Different from skill improvement — this extracts user facts and context rather than improving tool skills.
items.done: After a session, relevant facts ('user prefers TypeScript strict mode', 'project uses pnpm') are extracted and stored. Future sessions automatically include relevant memories as context.

items.title: Session Memory service — background LLM notes file maintained during conversation
items.summary: CC's Session Memory service (`sessionMemory.ts`) auto-maintains a per-session markdown notes file in the background using a forked subagent. It runs periodically via post-sampling hook, extracting key info (decisions, context, in-progress items) from conversation without blocking the main loop. Distinct from extractMemories (which writes to ~/.claude/projects/memory) — this is an in-session scratchpad.
items.done: A `.claude/session-memory.md` is auto-populated and updated throughout the session. The notes are injected into context on /compact and session resume.

items.title: Team memory sync — per-repo shared memory across org members via API
items.summary: CC syncs team memory files (`~/.claude/team_memory/<repo>/`) between local and a server API. Pull (server wins) downloads files on launch; push (delta, hash-based) uploads only changed entries. Scoped per git remote hash. Includes a secret scanner (`teamMemSecretGuard`) that blocks writes containing API keys/tokens to team memory.
items.done: Two org members on the same repo see the same shared memory. Writes sync within seconds. Secret patterns in edits are blocked from reaching team memory.

items.title: Auto-compact — automatic context compaction when window fills to threshold
items.summary: CC automatically triggers conversation compaction when context fills. `isAutoCompactEnabled()` checks settings + env (`CLAUDE_CODE_AUTO_COMPACT_WINDOW`). Reserves 20K tokens for summary output, respects manual/auto compact buffer sizes. Runs `compactConversation()` then `runPostCompactCleanup()`. Distinct from manual /compact and time-based microcompact.
items.done: When context reaches the auto-compact threshold, a compact runs automatically without user action. A compact boundary message shows what was summarized.
items.notes: onAutoCompact callback on AgentDeps; trackedSummarize wrapper in runTurn emits callback when compressMessages fires; wired in app.tsx + interactive.ts. VANTA_AUTO_COMPACT_THRESHOLD configures fill pct. Test proves callback fires with seeded history.

items.title: Session memory compact — compact into persistent session memory files (not just summary)
items.summary: An experimental compaction variant (`sessionMemoryCompact`) that compacts the conversation into structured session memory files (`.claude/session-memory/`) rather than a plain summary message. On the next session, the memory is loaded to restore context. Tracks tool names seen since last compact via `extractDiscoveredToolNames`.
items.done: After compaction, key facts persist to `.vanta/session-memory.md`. On `/resume`, the memory is injected as context so the agent recalls prior decisions.

items.title: Remote managed settings — enterprise org policies via API with dangerous-change security gate
items.summary: CC fetches org-level settings from a remote API and merges them as a `remoteManagedSettings` source. When fetched settings contain dangerous changes (bypassPermissions mode, hooks that execute arbitrary commands, MDM-style tool restrictions), CC shows a blocking security dialog requiring explicit user approval before applying.
items.done: Org admin sets `bypassPermissions: true` in remote settings. On next launch, a security dialog shows the dangerous change. User approves → takes effect. User rejects → session exits.

items.title: Claude in Chrome setup — install native messaging host for all Chromium browsers
items.summary: CC's `/chrome` command installs a native messaging host that bridges the Claude for Chrome browser extension to the CLI. Supports all Chromium-based browsers: Chrome, Brave, Edge, Chromium, Vivaldi, etc. Writes the host manifest to each browser's native messaging path on macOS/Linux/Windows, including registry entries on Windows.
items.done: `/chrome` installs the native messaging bridge for detected browsers. After setup, the Chrome extension can send prompts and images directly to the running Vanta session.

items.title: Chrome automation GIF recorder — capture multi-step browser interactions as GIF
items.summary: During Chrome browser automation (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*` tools), CC can record multi-step browser interactions as animated GIFs via `mcp__claude-in-chrome__gif_creator`. The agent is prompted to capture extra frames before/after actions for smooth playback, and to name the file meaningfully. GIFs are saved locally for review or sharing.
items.done: After a multi-step browser automation, a named GIF file appears in the working directory showing the full interaction replay.

items.title: Parallel tool execution — concurrent read-only tool calls with configurable cap
items.summary: CC's tool orchestration batches tool calls marked `isConcurrencySafe` and runs them concurrently (up to `CLAUDE_CODE_MAX_TOOL_USE_CONCURRENCY`, default 10). Read-only operations (Glob, Grep, Read, WebSearch, WebFetch) run in parallel; write/mutating tools run sequentially. Speeds up multi-file reads and search-heavy turns significantly.
items.done: A turn with 5 parallel Glob calls completes in ~200ms instead of ~1s. `VANTA_MAX_TOOL_CONCURRENCY=10` controls the cap.

items.title: VCR record/replay — record API request/response cassettes for test replay
items.summary: CC has a VCR (cassette recorder) mode that records all API requests and responses to disk when `FORCE_VCR=1` (for ants) or in `NODE_ENV=test`. Cassettes are keyed by content hash; on replay, matching requests return cached responses instantly without hitting the API. Used in integration tests and for debugging.
items.done: `VANTA_VCR=1` records a session's API calls to `.vanta/cassettes/`. Re-running with the same inputs replays from cassette without network calls.

items.title: Push-to-talk voice input — native CoreAudio/cpal + SoX/arecord fallback
items.summary: CC records microphone audio for voice input using a native NAPI module (`audio-capture-napi`) backed by CoreAudio on macOS, with lazy-loading (~1s first-press delay).
