items.title: `OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES` — emit full API request/response bodies as OTEL log events
items.summary: CC (v2.1.111) adds `OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES` env var to emit full Anthropic API request and response bodies as OpenTelemetry log events. Opt-in for debugging — not enabled by default due to token cost and PII exposure.
items.done: With `OTEL_LOG_RAW_API_BODIES=1`, every API request and response body appears as an OTEL log event, enabling full trace replay.

items.title: OTEL `app.entrypoint` metric attribute — segment metrics by session entrypoint
items.summary: CC (v2.1.152) adds `app.entrypoint` as an OpenTelemetry metric attribute (opt-in via `OTEL_METRICS_INCLUDE_ENTRYPOINT=true`). Allows slicing usage metrics (token counts, tool calls, costs) by how the session was started: cli, sdk, bg, etc.
items.done: With the env var set, OTEL metric dashboards show separate series for `entrypoint=cli` vs `entrypoint=sdk`.

items.title: `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` as metric labels — slice usage metrics by custom dimensions
items.summary: CC (v2.1.161) forwards `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES` values as labels on metric datapoints. Organizations can tag sessions with `team=platform` or `repo=api` and slice usage data by those dimensions in their OTEL backend.
items.done: Setting `OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES='team=backend,env=prod'` results in those labels appearing on all metric datapoints.

items.title: MCP result `maxResultSizeChars` — allow up to 500K chars via `_meta` annotation
items.summary: CC (v2.1.91) adds MCP tool result persistence via `_meta['anthropic/maxResultSizeChars']` annotation (up to 500K chars). MCP tools can annotate their results to persist large outputs — e.g. database exports, full file reads — beyond the default truncation limit.
items.done: An MCP tool returning a 200K-char result with `_meta['anthropic/maxResultSizeChars']: 300000` has the full result persisted and available to the model.

items.title: `skillOverrides` setting — control per-skill visibility to model and menu
items.summary: CC (v2.1.129) makes `skillOverrides` work: `off` hides a skill from both the model and `/`-menu; `user-invocable-only` hides from model but keeps it in the menu; `name-only` collapses the description to save context tokens.
items.done: Setting `skillOverrides: {my-verbose-skill: 'name-only'}` keeps the skill available but strips its description from the system prompt.

items.title: `ANTHROPIC_WORKSPACE_ID` — scope minted OAuth tokens to a specific workspace
items.summary: CC (v2.1.141) adds `ANTHROPIC_WORKSPACE_ID` env var for workload identity federation. When set, scopes the minted OAuth token to a specific Claude.ai workspace. Useful for enterprise deployments that assign distinct workspaces per team or project.
items.done: With `ANTHROPIC_WORKSPACE_ID=ws_xyz`, all API calls are scoped to that workspace. Usage and billing are attributed correctly.

items.title: `/color` with no args — picks a random session color
items.summary: CC (v2.1.128) makes bare `/color` (no arguments) pick a random color for the session. Previously it required an explicit color name. Useful for visually distinguishing multiple concurrent sessions at a glance.
items.done: Running `/color` with no argument assigns a random accent color to the session.

items.title: Cedar policy syntax highlighting — `.cedar` and `.cedarpolicy` files
items.summary: CC (v2.1.97) adds syntax highlighting for Cedar policy files (`.cedar`, `.cedarpolicy`). Cedar is Amazon's policy language used in AWS Verified Permissions and other authorization systems.
items.done: Opening or diffing a `.cedar` file shows syntax-highlighted Cedar policy with keyword coloring.

items.title: Skill `\$` escape — include literal `$` before a digit in skill command bodies
items.summary: CC (v2.1.163) adds `\$` escape syntax in skill command bodies to include a literal `$` before a digit. Without escaping, `$1` is interpreted as a command argument placeholder.
items.done: A skill with `command: 'echo \$1.00'` outputs the literal string `$1.00` instead of substituting argument 1.

items.title: Managed domain security — `allowManagedDomainsOnly` enforced across all settings sources
items.summary: CC (v2.1.126) fixes `allowManagedDomainsOnly` and `allowManagedReadPathsOnly` being ignored when a higher-priority managed-settings source lacked the key. Both are now enforced regardless of override hierarchy.
items.done: With `allowManagedDomainsOnly: true` in admin policy, even if a lower-priority settings file lacks the key, domain restrictions still apply.

items.title: Shell startup file write prompt — confirm before writing `.zshenv`, `.bash_login`, etc.
items.summary: CC (v2.1.160) adds a confirmation prompt before writing to shell startup files (`.zshenv`, `.zlogin`, `.bash_login`) and `~/.config/git/`. Prevents silent writes that could execute arbitrary code on every shell launch.
items.done: When an operation would modify `.zshenv`, CC shows a confirmation: 'This will run on every shell launch — allow?' Declining leaves the file unchanged.

items.title: Auto mode GA — no opt-in required, available for Max/Opus 4.7+
items.summary: CC (v2.1.111) removes the `--enable-auto-mode` requirement. Auto mode is now on by default for all users. Also made available for Max subscribers on Opus 4.7+ and later Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry with `CLAUDE_CODE_ENABLE_AUTO_MODE=1`.
items.done: Auto mode is available in the mode picker without any CLI flag or opt-in. Users on Max/Pro tiers with Opus 4.7+ see it immediately.

items.title: `Ctrl+U` clears entire input; `Ctrl+Y` restores — readline kill-buffer update
items.summary: CC (v2.1.111) changes `Ctrl+U` from 'delete to start of line' to 'clear entire input buffer'. The cleared content goes into the kill ring and can be restored with `Ctrl+Y`. Matches zsh/bash Emacs kill-line behavior.
items.done: Pressing `Ctrl+U` on a multiline prompt clears all input. `Ctrl+Y` restores it.

items.title: `/proactive` alias for `/loop` — proactive autonomy mode discoverability
items.summary: CC (v2.1.105) adds `/proactive` as an alias for `/loop`. Users who search for proactive mode by its conceptual name find it without knowing the `/loop` command name.
items.done: Running `/proactive` starts the same recurring autonomous loop as `/loop`.

items.title: `.husky` in acceptEdits protected dirs — git hooks directory is write-protected
items.summary: CC (v2.1.90) adds `.husky` to the list of protected directories in `acceptEdits` mode. Prevents agents from silently modifying git hook scripts, which could allow arbitrary code execution on commit.
items.done: In acceptEdits mode, attempts to write to `.husky/pre-commit` prompt for explicit confirmation.

items.title: Esc interrupts the running agent
items.summary: Pressing Esc while the agent is running should abort the current turn (set the AbortSignal). Currently Esc has no effect mid-run, which means the only escape is Ctrl+C (kills the whole process). Should fire the existing abortRef in use-agent-send.ts and show a cancellation note.
items.done: Pressing Esc while busy=true cancels the current agent turn gracefully. A note appears confirming the interrupt. The composer becomes active again.
items.notes: Signal now flows through getCompletion -> provider.complete/stream. Codex uses fetch signal. OpenAI/Anthropic SDKs use RequestOptions signal. Abort checks every iteration + mid-stream chunk. Esc fires abortRef.abort() immediately.

items.title: Scroll up while agent is streaming (normal mode)
items.summary: In normal (non-alt-screen) mode, the transcript keeps scrolling down as the agent streams. The terminal raw mode blocks native scroll. User cannot read earlier content while the agent is running.
items.done: User can scroll up (pgup/pgdn or terminal scroll) to read history while agent is generating. View snaps back to bottom when the turn completes.
items.notes: Reported via screenshot: continuous downward scroll during generation, no way to scroll back mid-run.
